author: Cara Ellis
body:
"HOW I MADE WEB POETRY🌟 Web Poetry started on paper, like most of my projects, in very messy sketches. I then designed each page in Figma, outside of Figma Make, to ensure I had full control over the final look. 

Once the design was ready, I pasted everything into Make with a detailed prompt. Then came hours of iteration. I tested it endlessly, writing the whole alphabet with my mouse each time until I couldn't stand it anymore, then got friends to test it too. In all, I used nearly 100 prompts to get it perfect.

I was so surprised by how easy it was to create something (anything!) in Make, "

author: Sabrina Polanco
body: "Hot take from the Make-a-thon 🌶️  AI isn’t killing creativity, it’s testing it. There are plenty of strong opinions about AI and its role for creatives, often along the lines of “it will steal our jobs!!!” What I noticed while using Figma Make is how powerful it can be for developing taste and growing as a creative. It’s not just about spitting out designs. It can push you to sharpen your creative direction skills, learn how to give feedback, build the vocabulary to get the results you want, and recognize what’s working (and what’s not). Used the right way, AI isn’t a shortcut. It’s a mirror."

author: Cassi Ferraz
body: "🌸 FloreIA is an app created to support people dealing with compulsions and addictions - food, drugs, medications, shopping, social media... More than an app, it's a pocket companion that embraces, listens and offers small tasks and motivational messages during difficult moments. The name FloreIA is a play on the Portuguese word ""floreia"" 🌸, which refers to the idea of flourishing and transforming, combined with the use of AI (artificial intelligence) as support. Addictions don't have a cure, but can be controlled with support. The app wants to stand alongside people on this journey, strength"

author: Cheryl Fang
body: "To wrap up my Make-a-thon journey, I’d like to share Lost & Found. At the end of every journey, it’s worth pausing to reflect: what did you lose, and what did you find along the way? Through reflection, we grow. Check out my final Figma Make submission and print your own receipt to wrap up your journey 🌱 https://new-ion-98275935.figma.site"

author: Rami Rikka
body:
"Love is the most powerful feeling in the world, but when it ends, it becomes the most painful. We’re taught how to treat physical pain, but for heartbreak we’re told to just move on. That pain inspired me to create Breakup Recovery, an emotional first aid kit for the broken-hearted. 

Breakup Recovery is a science-based tool grounded in cognitive and behavioral therapy, helping people flip negative thoughts into positive ones and take real steps toward healing. I designed it to feel engaging with quests and streaks that track progress, a Punch-It-Out arcade game to release frustration, an AI C"

author: Mudit Dhangar
body:
"I've been obsessing over AgriSense for months - a smart farming app that actually helps Indian farmers make money. When the Figma Makathon started and I saw everyone building basic portfolios and simple tools, I thought: ""Let's see how far I can push Figma Make!""

 https://lint-sage-73623084.figma.site"

author: Kristine Chang
body:
"https://onlymom.figma.site  
5 minute left! My friend Minh and I spent 2 days building a proof-of-concept for OnlyMom - a personalized alarm clock/reminder in the tone of your chosen mama. She can be spicy, she can be sweet, depending on how you tune her. The goal is to gaslight your way into being productive, like how many moms are so good at enforcing. We hope this would be a gift to everyone who need an OnlyMom for their productivity streak. ~ Open to feedback ^^"

author: Georgia Mashford
body:
"For the Figma Makeathon, I designed  Taste. , an app that lets you swipe, save, and share your food vibe; so finding restaurants is actually FUN. Think Tinder, but for vibey spots to eat and drink. Swipe right on what looks good, swipe left on what doesn’t, and the will app learn your vibe. Here’s what you can do on Taste: 
• Book a table or check walk-in wait times without leaving the app
• Save spots into custom lists (by city, cuisine, date-night energy, you name it)
• Follow friends and peek at their saved spots + recent finds
• Share your profile so people can see your curated food person"

author: syncrely studio
body:
"We built Escape the Workday - a tiny Severance TV series–inspired escape room for designers, made in Figma Make ✨ You’re stuck at a retro workstation with a to-do list on the wall: finish the tasks to escape. Link –  https://proxy-kale-24146399.figma.site/   Every interaction is alive:
💡 Toggle the desk lamp
🗑️ Drag “Old Files” to Trash
✍️ Try cursed fonts like Comic Sans & Papyrus
🚫 Attempt (and fail) to delete Figma
✅ Watch tasks cross off the wall Why we built it
We wanted to see if Figma Make could go beyond prototypes, what if you could build an escape room with it? Turns out yes. Make"

author: Elizabeth Anderson
body:
"Have you ever felt like you’ve spent so much time in Figma that it feels like you’re dating it? Well, now you can with “Figma: The Dating Simulator” with 56 total unique endings! Romance these four Figma features brought to life: Frame, Style, Variant, and Prototype. Will passion align… or will you just get lost in the flow? Open your heart and your design file to find out!

 I’ve never even made a game (or published anything this silly) before, but I decided to try to push Figma Make to its limits and see what it was capable of. It ended pushing me to its limits mostly, haha, but it was super"

author: Samuel Oladokun
body: "Hi everyone 👋, here’s my submission for the Figma Hackathon! Meet Jottyme — a feedback and collaboration platform built for designers and teams. With Jottyme, you can upload a link, PDF, or image, share it with clients, and gather clear, contextual feedback — all in one place. The goal was simple: take away the endless back-and-forth on email and chat, and replace it with a clean, intuitive space where collaboration just flows. Thanks to Figma Make, what would normally take weeks to develop the UI came together in just hours. https://ocean-swept-33113810.figma.site"

author: Cheryl Fang
body: "Have you ever wondered if other people face the same problems when communicating across teams? 🤔 For my third submission, I created  Bridge the Gap , a playful prototype where: 😬 Adding a challenge (Gap) makes the bridge stretch further apart. 🧱 Adding a solution (Step) lays down a brick to close it. It’s a fun way to reflect on the frictions between Design and Development, and celebrate how small steps can bring us closer. 👉 Try it out and add your own Gaps + Steps here:  https://puppy-deer-64254971.figma.site   Can’t wait to see the challenges and solutions you’ll add!"

Happy creating, everyone!"

author: Shubham S
body:
"Addictive solution alert!

When boredom hits, I catch myself doom-scrolling endlessly—seeing everything, but remembering nothing.
So I decided to flip the script. 2 days ago, I built a small experiment inside Figma Make to break this cycle. Since then, I’ve been using it religiously—no endless scrolling, no wasted hours. It’s simple, but it works. And the best part? It makes me want to stay away from doom-scrolling. I added ultra-rare dots as well, but so far I have had 0 luck in finding any, so I have updated the engine for better odds. Please LMK if you find any ultra-rare dots.

View LIVE -"

body: "For the FigmaMakeathon, I built Keyboard Warrior,  a rhythm‑piano game that turns your keyboard into an instrument. Hit notes in time with procedurally generated tracks and see how far you can push your score. ✨ 10 lanes mapped to A S D F G H J K L ;  🎵 Procedural patterns so every run feels fresh 🧠 Dynamic difficulty that adapts as you play 🏆 Built‑in leaderboard for friendly competition 🎚️ Beat‑synced visuals for extra juice 📱 Touch keyboard support for mobile Try it:  https://spent-expand-71115186.figma.site/   From Figma design to a working React game, Keyboard Warrior shows how desig"

author: Hailey  Li
body:
"I was in my DIY tile era but struggled to picture how different color combos would actually look together. So I built a little Tile Design Partner, a tool that lets you play around with tile color combos or upload your own design, and use AI to help you visualize how it might look in a realistic space before making any big commitments. The AI image generation is still a work in progress, and I’ll keep exploring ways to make it better.

author: Guille de Armas
body:
"I just finished the first version of a retro-style Windows 98 made with Figma Make. There are still some improvements to work on, like refining file saving in Paint and Notepad, and making folder navigation smoother, but I’m happy with this result to present at the hackathon. Switch to desktop mode, turn on this old monitor and take a trip back in time with this old operating system. (Don’t leave without checking out the screen savers right click on the desktop => properties 😂). Honestly, I’m really impressed by what we can build with Figma Make. These past few days have been a lot of fun.
Wo"

author: Henry Ugo
body: "Adaptive A/B Insight — v1 (Make + Figma) What v1 does Loads a sample CSV (or your own) and parses it automatically Lets you compare segments with chips and a default Seesaw (Matter.js) view Applies palette/theme globally across the UI and visuals Generates a Recommendation callout based on the data Captures the Compare view and sends it to a Deck Editor Exports to PDF from the Deck (editable titles, captions, notes) Why it matters Fast A/B + multi-segment comparisons without leaving Figma/Make Opinionated, readable visuals and a one-click path to a shareable deck Click Export to Deck → tweak c"

author: Mayur kothari
body:
"UI Vitals is a design tool that checks your UI's health. Think of it like a doctor for your designs. It looks for issues in things like spacing and colors, so you can fix them before you share your work. Just upload your Figma file, and it gives you a simple report. This helps you make sure your designs are perfect.

 Check My Work here"

author: Akash  Chouhan
body: "https://gush-tutor-28241457.figma.site/  FigmaArcade is a gamified learning platform that transform your design education into an exciting adventure. Instead of passive tutorials you will embark on quests and challenges to master Figma, 1 skill at a time. As you complete you challenges you will rewarded with points, collect badges and get ranked on leaderboard."

author: Abhishek Dhande
body: "✨ ContraQuest – Gamified Learning Platform Every creator here is doing amazing work 👏. In my case, I wanted to design something for the future of learning. Many students struggle with boring, theory-heavy lessons, so I created ContraQuest — a gamified e-learning platform where students earn XP, solve MCQs, and climb leaderboards. I’m not a professional designer. I’m a student with just 6 months of self-learning from YouTube and online resources. Still, I gave my 100% and let the design speak. ContraQuest is my vision of making learning engaging, fun, and modern — built fully in Figma Make. 👉"

author: Ismael Scorzelli

Hackathon Entry 
Submitting The Last Cabin. If you enjoy psychological horror and tension, step into the forest…

author: Naga Abirami V
body: "With last few hours to go, I kinda decided my problem statement 🙃 😅 I believe no good food should ever go to waste while people around us go hungry. That’s why I created this web application —to connect restaurants, grocery stores, and even households with anyone nearby who can use their surplus food. With just a few taps, extra meals find a new home instead of ending up in the trash. It’s simple, sustainable, and impactful—helping people save money, reduce waste, and fight hunger.  Thanks in advance ❣️❣️for your love and support ❤️"

author: Abhinav Chadha
body: "About 36 hours before the deadline, I got reminded about the #FigmaMakeathon. No big plan, just one thought stuck in my head: what if life had a Settings page? That’s how  Toggles  came to life. Almost everything: transitions, animations, flows was built directly in Figma Make. No extra tools. Just pushing the boundaries of what’s possible inside the app. I only had ~36 hours, and midway Figma threw errors, so some parts still glitch. I’ll keep fixing and polishing iteratively. Quick note to the judges: if this project resonates, DM me on X (@ac9teen). I’d love to scale Toggles further on a sh"

body: "Frame by frame SVG and free form animator. This is the app I worked on the most: an app to create small, frame-by-frame animations. You can copy the SVGs you create and paste them into the app to start animating them. author: Charmaine Fernandes
body: "✨ Inspired by Figma Make, I created  Make Good Happen (MGH)  — a platform that reimagines how Volunteers, NGOs, and Brands connect in India. India is a unique market where Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) is mandatory for eligible companies. MGH provides a structured yet engaging ecosystem that not only enables NGOs to access funding and volunteers, but also gives brands transparency, recognition, and measurable CSR outcomes. Volunteers, in turn, gain a rewarding experience with tangible perks and gamified recognition. The interface is inspired by dating apps — built around swipe gestures"

body: "Whenever we miss someone, it's not the big milestones we want to share; it's the little things in our daily lives. A flower on the street, the smell of rain in a café, a cookie fresh from the oven. That’s the concept behind my second submission: The Moon is Beautiful Tonight. Instead of creating another chat app, it focuses on sharing the little things and slowly building a shared star map together. body: "Here’s my submission for the #figmamakeathon ✨ I built Creative Reset,  a playful web app that helps creatives shake off stuck energy with short somatic exercises. You choose how you’re feeling (foggy, overthinking, scattered, etc.), and Calmi, your little guide, walks you through a quick reset. A few breaths, a shake, some tapping…and suddenly things feel lighter. This came together between two other deadlines — wasn’t sure I’d finish, but I’m so glad I did 🫶 If you try it, comment which reset you need! 👇"

 https://cactus-pivot-01176039.figma.site

 5 days! One person! First time using Figma Make! Absolutely loved every minute of it! Why OctoRate? Small hosts with one or a few properties are stuck with tools for big operators. Market-average calculators drag prices down, and spreadsheets never show what guests actually keep. With OctoRate you:
- Set an income goal (per month, year, or night)

- Get the breakdown: guest pay → fees → your"

author: Alpheraz Design
body:
"Tried spinning up my own WebGL creator tonight. Result? A floating beach ball that… didn’t quite float. 🏀❌ But here’s the thing — progress doesn’t always look pretty. Sometimes it’s broken shaders, empty canvases, or hours of staring at error logs. What matters is pushing through and experimenting, even if the first output is a flop. Every “failed” test is one step closer to mastery. Every bug is proof I’m actually building. Next round? I’ll take what I learned from this attempt and turn it into something cleaner, faster, and way more interactive

body: "Do you ever catch yourself doing this? Opening the fridge even though you just checked it 5 minutes ago. Telling yourself “one quick scroll” that somehow eats an hour. Pretending that refreshing crypto charts counts as productivity. Yeah, me too. So instead of hiding from my distractions, I built a place for them: the Digital Distraction Playground. Here’s the idea: You type in whatever’s pulling you away — Instagram, snacks, YouTube, whatever. It spawns as a silly little character messing around in a pastel playground. When you’re ready, you can “let them go” (they float off dramatically ✨). "

author: Sergey Bertagnini
body:
"I started this project with a simple question: Why should interfaces stay static, when people never are? We live in constant flux, happy, stressed, motivated, tired, yet our tools ignore it. So I built an interface that adapts. Not just in color, but in mood, in energy, in empathy. It began as two eyes on a screen. Then it became a way for technology to feel closer, more human. This isn’t about design for design’s sake. It’s about creating digital spaces that respond to us, not the other way around. The future isn’t cold and static. It’s emotional, adaptive, alive.

 https://wing-rating-510157"

StowPlan shows what stowage planning could be today — fast, reliable, intelligent. 🚢 StowPlan’s solution:

• Safety checks for balance, trim, and DG compliance

author: Pushpanjali  Singh
body:
"One Word, Many Worlds Short Description:
What can a single word do? In One Word, Many Worlds, one word unlocks four different creative realities: 🌸 Haiku — a short poem inspired by your word 🤓 Fun Fact — something surprising connected to it 🚀 Startup Idea — a quirky business concept based on it 😂 Joke — a witty one-liner This project is built entirely in Figma Make, using one carefully engineered prompt that transforms a single input into four unique outputs. Why it stands out: 💡 Showcases the power of prompt design in Figma Make 🎨 Minimal but delightful — one input, many creative format"

It’s called WebGL Playground, a space where text and shapes can move, collide, spiral, and fall apart in different modes. Meet BugBash 🐦🐛 Right now, you can: 📸 Upload screenshot/video → annotate bugs
✍️ Auto-description from your annotation
📊 Tag impact (revenue, engagement, compliance, growth)
📲 Classify by device/browser
📌 Track progress (raised → in-progress → closed)
🎯  And to ensure you're on a BugBashing rush
  Unlock more Birds as you eat more bugs 🐣
  Evolve your profile with every bug you crush 📈 💡 Future scope? Jira ticket sync + org-level  reward systems"

author: Matthew Smith
body:
"Good breaks are the secret to productivity — but most people neglect them. Here’s my take 👇 I built a productivity app called TakeFive in Figma Make that sends microbreak reminders, helps you log recharge time, and turns microbreaks into a habit — not a guilty pleasure. — Fully-functioning microbreak reminders ⏰
— Track streaks & earn achievements 📊

author: Yui Atarashi
body: "👉🔗  Phototypesetting with Figma Make 📽️ Once a vital craft for magazines and advertisements, phototypesetting is now nearly extinct. This project brings it back to life in digital form with Figma Make!! 🔧 Choose a font, adjust lens parameters with sliders, and press “Expose to Film” to capture your text onto a virtual film strip. A lost technology, revived as an interactive UI experiment. ⚡✨ Bringing analog magic back to the screen with Figma Make ✨⚡"

author: Jake Sorrentino
body: "Maybe I’m too sentimental, but do you also have a box full of birthday, holiday, graduation cards, etc, that continues to pile up? You could scan or snap a photo of them… but that feels kinda tacky, right? If only there were a way to digitize them in a way that actually makes sense. What about as a 3D card? That’s exactly what CardKeeper does. CardKeeper transforms your physical cards into interactive 3D keepsakes you can flip through, open, and revisit anytime. No more dusty boxes in the closet, just a beautiful, organized digital collection that still feels personal. It’s memory preservation"

author: Pratyusha Patnaik

Meet Review Bot 🤖✨  the tool that scans your Figma files or websites to spot sneaky design issues (spacing slip-ups, color contrasts gone rogue, font chaos… you name it) and instantly suggests smart fixes. Bonus: it even checks against WCAG guidelines so your designs aren’t just pretty, but accessible too. ♿✅ No more late-night “Is this button too close?” spirals just clean, consistent, WCAG-friendly design at your fingertips. 🚀 We’re building it at #Figmamakeathon, because good design shouldn’t come with guesswork.

#Figma #DesignTools "

Some parts of the site are still coming soon, but the idea is already clear: here, every dream car has a home. Not all of us can show our real dream car to the world, but with Carvion, we can make it"

I kept the flow straightforward: onboarding to explain the concept, a creation screen to add memories, and a vault where all capsules are stored with their unlock dates. No extra frills, just the joy of anticipation and reflect"

"Here is my submission for FigmaMakeathon, a simple digital pomodoro timer.

How it works: 
1. Choose a task you want to work on. 
2. Set the timer for 25 minutes (one Pomodoro). 
3. Work with full focus until the timer rings. 
4. Take a short break (about 5 minutes). 
5. Repeat the cycle. 
6. After 4 Pomodoros, take a longer break (15–30 minutes).

Check out the link here  https://push-stop-90123749.figma.site  
(Turn on sound for best experience)

author: Jarar Malik
body: "SkillsCash:  Play your favorite games, stake real money, and win cash. Aggregator + Escrow + Rating App Schema SkillsCash lets players compete in games (Clash Royale, 8 Ball Pool, Among us etc.) for real-money stakes. Players deposit funds, set stakes, and get matched with opponents of similar skill levels. Games are played outside the app; results are submitted with screenshots or evidence. Stakes are held in escrow until results are confirmed or disputes resolved. Opponents have 2 hours to counter-claim; disputes are settled with evidence and support review. Winners get payouts (stake × 1.8)"

Getting text to spiral at unconventional angles took careful prompting and visual guidance, but Figma Make handled it impressively! Extracts device time. "After nights out, my friends and I always stumble back to my place with no hesitation after being offered instant ramen, but keeping track of orders is chaos. So I present... Drunken Ramen Builder! A game where they customise bowls while tipsy. The twist? The ingredients and buttons dodge taps on purpose, buying me time to cook (but also it's funny to see them struggle 😈). Their order gets written down, my friends are too busy chasing fish cakes and I stay somewhat sane.

Ideally, if I had more time, I'd build the chef's perspective of the app too!

 https://filled-drill-09667453.figma.site/"

author: Julio Aragoza
body: "For this Figmakeathon, I've developed a design tool that scores your work on a scale of 1 to 10. My inspiration comes from the work of Dieter Rams, one of the most influential industrial designers of all time. The tool's scoring is based directly on his timeless ten principles for good design. Whether you're a seasoned designer or just starting out, this tool provides an objective way to evaluate and improve your creations. The Dieter Index"

author: Jon Simon
body: "Hey everyone! I built SoundLaunch, a checklist to help indie artists stay on track with their releases. Since indies don’t have labels handling uploads, promo, and marketing, they end up juggling it all alone. My favorite parts: the Hype Meter that literally bursts into flames when you finish your tasks 🔥 and the Spin the Wheel side quest that spits out random promo plans just for fun. Still a lot I want to add (and responsiveness isn’t perfect yet), but this was such a fun build. Good luck everyone! SoundLaunch created with Figma Make"

author: Chhavi Malik
body:
"I easily clocked 20 hours straight on my desk for my #Figmamakeathon entry. A friend mockingly asked me:
“How is you sitting that long in front of your desk any different from a corporate slave?” Fair question. Here’s my answer:

When you choose the problem, when the work feels like play, when the outcome excites you more than the exhaustion; that’s not slavery. That’s flow."

author: Shivam Tiwari
body:
"Why should DJs have gear worth lakhs when kids with just a webcam could play symphonies?

Introducing The Air Piano.
Live Demo:  https://air-piano.figma.site/

 🚀 What It Does Air Piano transforms hand gestures into piano music, with no clicks, no keyboards, and no touching required. Your laptop’s camera captures simple motions like hover, tap, press, and lift. Each movement triggers a note or alters its volume, creating a fully immersive, touch-free piano experience.
 🧠 Why It’s Bold, Creative & Clever This isn’t a gimmick, it’s a reimagining of how we interact with sound. Air Piano challen"

author: Aman Jain
body:
"What happens when you change the world around people? We built a tiny universe to find out.
Add a tree, and people pause.
Place a bench, and they stay a little longer.
Draw a fence, and they change their path.
Switch to night, and the whole rhythm slows down. It’s not just a simulation.
It’s a canvas of human movement.
And you are the artist.

Try it out for yourself-  https://lifesimulator.figma.site"

author: Gerardo Vinces
body: "Tribe is a new kind of social app where users don’t just join communities — they create them. Built in Figma Make, Tribe empowers anyone to design and launch their own “tribes” with custom names, colors, and vibes. In just a few taps, a user can spin up a community, invite friends via QR code, and start shaping culture together. Unlike traditional social networks that assign you an identity or feed, Tribe is bottom-up and user-owned. Every tribe is a unique world — playful, weird, and entirely self-made. Our prototype shows how creativity, innovation, and clever prompts can make community-buil"

The Eventbrite API runs through Supabase, while SF Fun Cheap comes in via RSS. Everything gets normalized into consistent card components. The UI started from a Figma library… but I changed my mind halfway through, which definitely made things more complicated.

 https://creme-guitar-21759494.figma.site/"

Ops: auto-created tasks, envelopes, and occasions. Privacy: private by"

author: Amaan Khan
body:
"RepLight is a minimalist interval training tool built to eliminate mental clutter from workouts. It alternates between two intuitive stages — Go and Rest — and gives users full control without overwhelming them with numbers, rep-counting, or distractions. 
 RepLight features customizable round lengths, adjustable work/rest ratios with auto-calculated rest, and seamless Go–Rest cycling. Controls include Start, Pause, Stop, Skip, and a countdown visibility toggle, with visual and audio cues guiding each stage.

A traffic light, but for workout. To experience its full glory (& burn some calories)"

author: Vansh Grover
body: "I have observed my fellow designers' dislike for vibe coding tools and found out that their practice of building a design system first and then proceeding to UI gives them a sense of control. Figma Make solves this problem by bridging design files to make but what if, there was a better way to build your design system ? For my entry, I have built Aether to fix this. It helps designers onboard to vibe coding from familiar grounds.  Upload an image, choose a preset, or hit 'Chaos Mode'. Preview and tweak your system till you are satisfied. It instantly generates a complete React design system.  "

author: Stan Yu
body: "Last year, Oct 2024, I left my last full-time job and went out there looking for a career change. I spent months working my resume, sending them out with cover letters while looking for spaces to set up my work station. I was hopping around cafes, and was wondering if there's an equivalent to pay-it-forward concept but made to support people in between jobs, and could use some help. Some hope, and hope can come in the form of a cuppa coffee.  I did up a premise description, put it to AI to create a system of rules, and then transformed that into a PRD before feeding it into Figma Make to start"

author: Wagisha
body: "https://baton-table-35875647.figma.site   Task Leveling is a gamified productivity app inspired by Solo Leveling. Instead of a boring to-do list, every task becomes a Gate you must open and conquer. Creating a task opens a new Gate, and it appears in your Available Gates list. Starting a task is like entering the Gate, your timer begins, and when you complete it, you gain XP. Shorter tasks grant less XP, while longer ones reward more, making effort feel meaningful. When your XP reaches 100, you level up, unlocking new ranks. With Task Leveling, finishing real-world tasks feels like defeating m"

author: Suhavi Sunderlal
body: "Rituals is an interactive storytelling prototype for people with mild cognitive decline. Rooted in procedural memory — the memory that powers daily habits — the project explores how repeating simple rituals like brushing teeth or lighting a diya can help delay cognitive decline. Through warm, painterly visuals inspired by Indian homes, everyday acts are reimagined as guided touch interactions — swipes, taps, and rotations that unfold like a calm story. Rituals is more than a prototype; it’s a way to preserve independence, dignity, and a sense of peace through the beauty of repetition and narra"

author: Abdulrahman Akinyemi
body:
"My submission births  Swift Legal , a Lagos-based legal support startup designed to solve the persistent inefficiencies in litigation support.

Stemming from the principle of Fair Hearing which is embedded in the legal system of nations of the world, for a court of law to exercise it’s jurisdiction in a suit before it, the Defendant must have been put on Notice through service of Court Process. 

Unfortunately, legal actions in Lagos, Nigeria are plagued by unreliable and unethical service of court documents, delays in proof of service, and frequent adjournments caused by inefficient bailiffs."

body: "Is sports betting taking over your life—or the life of someone you care about? If so, I’ve built something that might help. This sports betting calculator lets you input (or import) your betting habits and compares your time and money spent with your earnings or losses. It then calculates your hourly return (or loss) based on the time you've invested. Winning $500 might sound impressive, but if it took 500 hours and $100 to earn it... is it really worth it? This tool is designed to help people understand the real return on their sports betting and encourage awareness around time and money spen"

Features at a glance: - Inner Circle Alerts → SOS + live location to trusted people. - Fake Video Call → Exit unsafe situations without suspicion. - Safety Companion → Call for volunteer support when you can’t walk alone. - Unsafe Area Map → Spot risky zones and add your own warnings. - Self-Defense Guides → Learn to prepare, not just react. "

"

You want to help, but all you get is a vague text with no photo, no clear direction, and no follow-up. Amber Alerts should do more than just interrupt us; they should guide us. That’s why I reimagined the experience into something actionable, intuitive, and human-centered - an alert that not only informs you, but empowers you to contribute meaningfully in those critical moments."

Check it out here:  https://colourcube.figma.site/  
(Use Desktop for the best Experience) 🔹 How it works: Treat RGB values as 3D coordinates inside a cube. Each axis represents one channel: Red (X), Green (Y), and Blue (Z). The cube starts from pure black (RGB 0,0,0) and extends to pure white (RGB 255,255,255). 🔹 What you can do: 🔍 Find any color → Enter RGB values and instantly locate the point"

Color Harvest is a cross-platform app that makes working with SVG colors effortless. Upload any SVG to instantly extract its full color set in multiple formats (RGB, HEX, HSL, LAB, Pantone, and more). Refine colors individually or in bulk with advanced controls like brightness and saturation, then copy codes with a single click. When you’re ready, export your work as an updated SVG, a standalone palette, or an ASE file for Adobe tools. author: Benjamin Moses
body: "I have always been particular about providing meaningful solution that impacts society, that's why I created MyDokita App. Sub-Saharan Africa is in a healthcare crisis—rural clinics often have just one worker for over a thousand people, leading to long waits and poor outcomes. MyDokita bridges this gap by acting as a digital co-worker: it speeds up triage, reduces patient backlogs, supports accurate diagnosis, ensures WHO-standard treatment, flags outbreaks early, and even works offline—empowering overstretched health workers to deliver faster, safer, and more equitable care. try it out:  http"

Every day blends sweet, salty, sour, bitter, and spicy vibes—and we can always shake them off and start fresh, just like a mixologist’s craft.

Get a Drink:  https://used-glass-78807583.figma.site   My #FigmaMakeathon submission, Emocktail, turns daily emotion reviews into a fun ritual for mental health. The name blends ""emo"" (emotions) and ""mocktail"" for a unique, alcohol-free twist. Share your mixes with friends! 🍹

author: Anas Ahmed Khan
body: "Gratitude & Wellness – Making Habits Fun I created Gratitude & Wellness for the Figma Make-a-thon to show how design can turn simple wellness habits into something fun and easy to use. This project is a mobile app prototype with 5 interactive screens (Welcome → Lesson → Quiz → Results → Share). Designed fully in Figma Make, it uses soft pastels, rounded cards, and clear typography. The flow includes a gratitude lesson, an interactive quiz, instant results, and a share option. The goal is to prove self-care and learning can feel enjoyable when paired with modern design and smooth interactivity."

author: Abdul Rahman
body:
"Vynora  Built with Figma Make ! 

 https://vynora.figma.site/   A Classic iOS Music Player – Reimagined in Figma This design is a faithful recreation of the iconic iOS music player experience that shaped a generation of digital music. Inspired by the era when Steve Jobs introduced the iPod and revolutionized how we listened to songs, this project captures the timeless elegance of Apple’s early UI philosophy—simplicity, focus, and joy in use. As a product designer, I’ve spent a lot of time exploring psychology and human behavior.

I’ve also added a feedback form inside the app, so you can share your thoughts and help me make  BiasLab  even better. I hope you discover something new here. author: Shina ETEKPO
body: "Bubble Pop Challenge is a sensory mini-game that recreates the unique satisfaction of popping bubble wrap. At the start, all bubbles appear white, then they randomly shift into different colors. The goal is simple: pop all the bubbles of the same color before the timer runs out. With each level, the number of bubbles increases and the time limit decreases, making the challenge even more exciting. A minimalistic design, smooth animations, and a satisfying “pop” sound make the experience both relaxing and addictive.                                                                                 "

author: Basha Damba
body:
"Introducing Sentinel, my Figma Make project.
Sentinel is a developer-first tool that scans your app or website to spot vulnerabilities and patch security gaps, whether you’re still building or already live. When I first started vibe coding, one question always lingered, “but how secure is my app?” That’s the problem Sentinel is going to solve. It highlights the weak spots, shows you how to fix them, and keeps your project safe without slowing you down. This idea has been brewing in me for a while, and now, thanks to #figmamakeathon, it’s time to bring it to life.

 Check it out here."

Focus Garden helps by letting you add tasks, set a countdown timer to stay on track, and actually get things done. But it’s more than a task manager. author: Manish Tomar
body:
"Design Battle Arena — Viral Design Voting with Figma Make 👉 The Problem
Design reviews often devolve into endless debates, scattered Slack threads, and messy screenshots. It’s slow, unstructured, and frustrating. 👉 The Solution (Built with Figma Make)
With Design Battle Arena, you can: 1. Create structured battles → Enter a battle name, description, and add multiple rounds. Each round can be Swipe Mode (Tinder-style voting) or Side-by-Side Mode (A vs B).
2. Generate a battle link and share instantly
3. Participants join via link → enter their name → go round by round, vote, and add comments."

author: Ashton  Gideon
body: "I used Figma Make to create an OCR (Optical Character Recognition) Document Generator that instantly produces realistic invoices and receipts (complete with stains, blurs, and faded ink) to help our company stress-test real AI computer vision models. Our vision models utilize OCR to scan and categorize user-submitted expenses, significantly reducing the time required for manual review. By giving designers and engineers a fast, no-code way to generate complex test data, Figma Make helped us bridge AI innovation and user experience design. It highlights how UX can help accelerate AI development "

body: "👓 InclusiveLens - Accessibility tool  Remember that moment when you realize your design might exclude someone who needs it?  Every day, millions of people struggle with websites that weren't built for them... buttons too small to tap, text too light to read, impossible navigation with a keyboard. Meanwhile, designers and developers want to help but feel overwhelmed by complex accessibility guidelines and looming legal deadlines. I had so much fun thinking about a MVP solution for this problem. InclusiveLens may bridge that gap. Upload your design and InclusiveLens show you exactly what needs "

author: Ayaneshu
body: "So here’s the story. Ever been stuck with a font you love but wish you could tweak just a little? Maybe adjust kerning pairs, stretch it, or make it feel more condensed. If the foundry or the designer didn’t build those variations in (or unless you’re paying big bucks for variable fonts), you’re out of luck. Not anymore!!! Introducing:  https://typographer.figma.site I built a web-based tool where you can upload any .ttf file, stretch or squeeze the font, add/remove/modify kerning pairs and then export it back as a production-ready .ttf file. Give it a try. Share your feedback & feature ideas!"

author: Shari Robertshaw
body:
"I’ve built a tool that I want to use, it is designed to streamline the start of new web projects. Instead of beginning from scratch, users can leverage what already exists. Nearly every company or organization already has a website, this allows users to respark their site. 
The tool uses AI to crawl a site and automatically generates a complete sitemap of its current pages. I can quickly add, remove, or adjust components within the wireframes, then export the entire sitemap or wireframes "

body: "Emotune is an innovative mood-expression platform that enables users to share their current emotional states by creating and sharing unique mood stamps. author: Anuj Sande
body:
"✨ Ephemeral: AR Digital Memory
A social AR platform designed around the impermanence of memory. Imagine leaving behind a short video, a voice note, or even a floating 3D scribble at a real-world location. The twist? These digital memories aren’t forever — they fade, glitch, and blur with time, just like human memories do. 👻 Fresh → Faded → Decaying → Ghost — each memory changes visually and emotionally over time, making every interaction unique. This was my submission for Figma’s prompt: “Design a location-based AR platform for leaving digital memories that gracefully decay over time.” Check "

author: vanshita kalyani
body: "Hey,MindMate is a simple yet powerful AI app that helps users gain daily mental clarity. Every day, the app asks one personalized question based on the user’s focus area (work, study, or life). Users answer by text or voice, and the AI instantly generates a clear summary, 3 actionable steps, and a motivational insight — all saved into a personal journal. Unlike heavy therapy or long journaling apps, MindMate is lightweight, habit-forming, and universal. It transforms daily stress into structured clarity within minutes, helping people stay focused, mindful, and motivated. https://www.figma.com/"

author: Prasanthi Parupudi
body: "How might we design a game that uses sound and touch as the main mechanics, so anyone — regardless of visual ability — can play, enjoy, and improve their memory? Most digital games rely heavily on visuals, leaving out people who navigate the world primarily through sound and touch. There’s a lack of fun, casual games that are non-visual first — games designed to be played using audio, haptics, and memory skills instead of screens. As a result: Players who can’t rely on visuals are excluded from mainstream play. Opportunities to build memory, focus, and spatial awareness through gaming are miss"

body: "“Second chances taste better.” I know I can’t solve food waste or hunger overnight, but I believe small steps matter. This project makes leftovers feel exciting instead of forgotten. The idea is simple: enter the ingredients in your kitchen, and the site suggests meals you can cook right away. To take it further, I’ve also included a section for community kitchens and NGOs where you can donate food to help those in need while reducing waste. It’s not a grand solution, but a tiny step toward cutting waste, sharing food, and appreciating what we already have. PS: I didn’t receive the Pro seat up"

author: Mohamed Oraby
body: "Most YouTube workout videos stick to fixed work and rest times. But when I want flexibility—changing durations or swapping intervals—I end up juggling external apps. Mobile timers rarely give me that freedom. During the makathon, I asked myself: what if I built the timer I actually need? Pick a mode (Study, Workout, Travel, Fantasy, Notes, Work). Set your work/rest time with sliders. It’s not perfect yet—Figma Make still doesn’t support video, Lottie, sounds, or hover success states. Those little cues matter for this app. Now I’m proud to release it, let people try it out, test it. 👉 This pro"

author: PRABHU PRASAD PRADHAN
body:
"App Name: Civic Service Issue Reporter

Description:
Spot it. Snap it. Sort it. This civic app turns every citizen into a watchdog—capture issues like potholes, garbage, and broken streetlights, and the app auto-tags the exact location, fetches the right ward, councillor, and engineer, and files it instantly. Track real-time progress with a clear status timeline, call or message the responsible contacts, and share updates on social media to rally community action—cleaner streets, safer neighborhoods—starting with one photo.

Publish Link:  https://myrtle-clad-17349142.figma.site"

author: Lee Black
body: "Imagine conducting ambient music in mid-air. It's like Minority Report, but instead of solving crimes you’re just gently adjusting your nervous system. For #FigmaMakeathon I built Airwwave (AR) in #FigmaMake: a fullscreen performance instrument where gestures become live ambient soundscapes. It’s built to feel like conducting, not clicking. Video is long because it’s a performance piece. I’m still hunting bugs, improving responsiveness, and cross browser performance until deadline. Try it:  https://airwwave.figma.site   Features: 28 ambient loops (hosted on Cloudinary), 12s fade in/out, 7-step"

The trickiest part: making the punch animation actually cut the right shape from the right coordinates. Harder than it sounds. Built entirely with Figma Make for the . Try it here:  https://figmatelia.figma.site"

body: "I designed a bedtime story app called Nightlight. The idea is your kid had a hard day, and you want to turn it into a bedtime story that actually helps them process it. But you're exhausted. It's 8pm. You're not writing a creative brief. So instead of a blank prompt, you tap an emotion (Frustration, Fear, Being Brave), type one sentence (""she got upset at soccer""), and the app generates a fully illustrated story with a lesson at the end. Two taps. One sentence. Story in three minutes. The age toggle changes the actual story structure, not just the word count. A 3-year-old gets simple pages. A "

author: Philippe Côté
body:
"⚠️ Is ""Doom Scrolling"" or ""Brain Rot"" affecting you or your loved ones?

Do you recognize the pattern? If so, read on.

Aza Raskin, a technology engineer and designer at the interface company Humanized, invented infinite scrolling in 2006.

Originally designed to create a smooth and uninterrupted browsing experience by eliminating the need to click to view new pages, Raskin later expressed regret about this invention, noting its role in creating an addictive, time-consuming form of ""behavioral cocaine.""

☀️ Today, we propose an update to this invention aimed at limiting the addiction mechanism"

author: Paula Gibert
body: "I’ve always been fascinated by synesthesia and how some people can actually see sound as color. I wanted to explore that idea through interaction design, so I built Chroma, a piano that paints while you play 🎹 🎨 Each note is mapped to a color based on its frequency, gradually generating a living artwork in real time and turning music into visual composition. The intention was to make generative art approachable, exploring synesthesia through play. This was my first time experimenting with vibe coding using Figma Make, and honestly I had a lot of fun making it! Would love to hear your thought"

author: Jasmine Gordon
body:
"A photo viewer inspired by the classic 3D View‑Master.

I wanted to rethink how we look back at our memories on our phones, and bring back that slow, nostalgic feeling instead of the usual quick swipe through. 

So I attempted to mimic the View‑Master lever, you press and pull the circle in either direction to switch images. Drag it right to move forward, drag it left to go back. I added the ability to move backwards (which you can’t do on the real thing) because it just felt more natural to give people that freedom. For the UI, I designed it to be clear and simple whilst allowing the image th"

body: "I wanted to create something that practical designers could use for the #FigmaMakeathon. Meet Depth, a new design tool that reimagines the ""zoom” (pinch to zoom/Ctrl+scroll to zoom) as a progressive design inspection tool.  Each level reveals a different layer of your design. Structure. System. Intent. Three levels. One gesture. Seeing the “why” behind each design has never been easier, even for stakeholders.  Inspect, copy tokens directly, and leave annotations anywhere. View the live site here:  https://zebra-chat-30501410.figma.site/ Support my on LinkedIn post here:  https://tinyurl.com/42"

author: Eddie Sosera
body:
"Esta — AI that senses your world.

Most AI assistants are blind to your physical reality. They don't know if you're walking, sitting in a dark room, or surrounded by people. You have to tell them everything. Esta fuses real-time sensor data: camera, microphone, accelerometer, ambient light, and computer vision, to understand your physical context without you saying a word. It classifies micro-motion patterns (calm, fidgeting, walking), detects faces and scene complexity, and adapts its interaction mode automatically: voice when you're moving, glanceable cards when you're busy, ambient when you"

author: David vilca obregon
body: "Hi, I’m David Vilca. I’m exploring a new way to discover resources in Figma Community — one that feels more natural for designers: drawing. https://drawyourwireframe.figma.site Sketch any screen or wireframe, and AI interprets your layout to return relevant results from Figma Community. Instead of relying only on keywords, the system analyzes visual patterns — structure, hierarchy, and components — and connects them with related projects. If no strong match is found, it expands the search using curated UI pattern libraries. The goal: reduce the friction between idea and inspiration. Try it, sk"

author: Hashfi Arissa
body:
"Stretch-it: Hand Exercises for Creatives, Built with Figma Make this weekend. Early in my design career, I injured my hands. Multiple physiotherapy sessions, lost time, and a lot of self-doubt later, I realized most of us don't think about our hands until it's too late. So I built this over the weekend 🫰 Stretch-it is a simple hand and finger exercise app with motion gestures, covering the fundamentals: strengthening, stretching, and flexibility as well as the reps and relax system. Check it out here:
 https://stretchit.figma.site/   All based on notes from my own physiotherapy journey. Our h"

author: Chantal Jemmott
body:
"A new interaction paradigm for AI. Instead of regenerating outputs through prompt rewriting, users refine a single response through gesture. A horizontal drag movement allows the result to evolve across five preset axes based on user preferences.

 Check it out  

This approach reduces prompt complexity, minimizes unnecessary iteration, and simplifies how we collaborate with AI systems. The future of AI interaction may not be about better prompts, but better ways to shape outcomes."

author: Mohammed Alyazji
body: "Wrapping up my journey in the #FigmaMakeathon ✨ Big thanks to Contra and Figma for creating challenges like this. They truly push us to grow, refine our skills, and explore new creative directions while sharing our work with such an inspiring community. For me, the real win is never just the prize—it's the learning, experimentation, and progress made throughout the journey. Wishing the best of luck to everyone who participated. P.S. This piece was inspired by the calm beauty of nature and the timeless craft of embroidery. 🌿🧵😊"

author: Ahmad Ullah
body: "The 17 Sustainable Development Goals are essentially the world’s to-do list for 2030. I wanted to build an immersive interface that actually lets countries and governments interact with their progress. It’s built for deep dives where you can compare performance across borders, analyze raw data, and even use a policy sandbox to ""dummy try"" initiatives to see simulated results before they happen.  🚀  Honestly didn’t think I could cram all of this in, but Figmake helped me pull off features and intuitive experience. I managed to build an AI-driven story mode, conversational inputs, and :a unique"

Wild.  It’s called Atay (Moroccan mint tea). We drink it at all times! Somehow I started seeing my time as liquid. Tea I pour into projects (or doom"

author: Deeksha Satapathy
body:
"🕰 Save Ana - Your one chance for saving her.  You’re a time agent sent back to one night - the night Ana dies.
 https://saveana.figma.site/   She’s a scientist about to publish research that could save millions in your future. 
Looped models - detailed side characters - dynamic entry points - Ana has autonomy. She reacts to your tone, timing, and intent. Charm earns moments. Trust saves lives. Manipulation might reward you, but it pushes you closer to failure. Time moves only when you choose.

If she dies, the night resets. Can you break the loop — or are you just another variable in it?"

author: Fabio  Torres
body: "our AI lives in a text box. What if it had an office? Meet T-R41N1… A universal embodiment layer for AI agents, built in Figma Make by  ,    and me. Designed in a 1930s rubber-hose cartoon style, T-R41N1 becomes your office companion: raise your hand to call, speak and see your words appear live, nod to continue, shake your head to adjust, or ignore it and it slips into vacation mode. Plug in your preferred AI agent via proxy to give it presence and personality. The idea is simple: not to replace the brain of AI, but to give it a cartoon body and make it feel alive. It’s published on Figma Com"

author: JATIN PALANDE
body: "Tap-to-like made engagement frictionless and feeling invisible. Imprint puts feeling back. Instead of tapping, you hold. Instead of a heart, you speak one word. That word is analyzed — its energy, warmth, pace, tone — and becomes a unique character mark that lives on the post forever. Every mark left by every visitor contributes to a generative ambient song unique to that post. Open the analytics panel and you hear the collective voice of everyone who felt something. The interaction is entirely new but immediately understood. The output is visual, musical, and deeply human. Nothing is stored e"

author: Sam Okpere
body:
"I built an agentic AI workflow builder inside   Make and used it to create this entire page workflow. This just shows how powerful Figma Make is. The builder is a visual drag-and-drop canvas that links AI agents, tools, and data into automated workflows. Each node runs an agent or action and passes its output to the next. You can add conditions, loops, retries, and error handling, and even pan the canvas. My favorite details: when you add an action node, there’s a subtle ripple under it, and you can also move nodes with your keyboard. 
With Make, you can turn any design page into a working, cl"

author: Supreet @rlvnt
body:
"I am obsessed with this reinvented way of experiencing the web.
GYRO exploration just feels right. 
It feels native. Intuitive. Alive. 
Let me tell you why.
For years, we’ve been scrolling flat surfaces. Tapping boxes. Clicking icons we don’t even think about anymore. The web became muscle memory. Efficient, yes. But predictable.
This feels different.
You don’t just scroll.
You move.
You tilt.
You explore. 
The interface responds to you.
On mobile, the gyro makes the experience physical. Subtle shifts in perspective. Depth that reacts to motion. It turns passive browsing into something immersi"

body: "Re-imagining the digital grid with Kinetica: The Elastic Workspace. 🧬 ​For the FigmaMakeathon, I wanted to move past the static ""flick-to-scroll"" interaction of 2007. Kinetica introduces physical tension and organic motion to digital discovery. By treating UI as a tactile material that stretches and snaps with real-world physics, we turn a simple gallery into a sensory experience. https://snort-swoop-89090574.figma.site ​Built entirely with Figma Make to shape the future of how we ""feel"" data. Figma Community Post X post"

author: Iñigo Hernández
body:
"What if drawing WAS music?
 I built FORMLESS, a gestural drone synthesizer where every stroke you draw on a canvas becomes a living sound. No keyboards, no sequencers, just your fingers and a blank screen. Try it:  https://formlessound.figma.site/ It runs 8 synthesis engines (sine, sub, saw, crystal, grain, noise, flutter, metal) with up to 24 simultaneous voices, real-time filter/reverb/LFO modulation, scale & root selection, and WAV loop recording, all pure Web Audio API, zero audio libraries. Works on desktop, tablet, and mobile with full multitouch.

Figma Community:  https://www.figma.com"

author: Zoe Li
body: "Start your day with good vibes and a touch of magic 📜✨ Growing up, I watched my dad consult the Chinese Almanac 黄历 for the Auspice 宜 - the specific actions that bring a day into harmony and luck. I found a beautiful parallel in the wizarding world: fate is not just a fixed prophecy, but a conversation with our own bravery and choices. As a wise headmaster once said, it is our choices that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities. Using Figma Make as my creative partner, we reimagined ancient wisdom through a modern interface, weaving threads of magic into The Scripted Prophecy 缄默预言"

Social share -  https://tinyurl.com/4jkv76dr   The Vision

Visuals: Figma AI (Asset generations/Cleanups).

author: Mauro Semedo
body:
"I chose to reimagine the infinite scroll on social media. We all know the feeling. You open your phone and 30 minutes disappear. That's doomscrolling. Let's not even talk about the guilt and anxiety that comes after. But what if a simple addition could change how millions of people use social media? That's why I built Zombie Antidote. A choice point that appears between videos, turning an unconscious decision into a conscious one. Design decisions: → Mood-based. Makes choosing easy when you're in that low-energy zombie state.
→ Skippable. You can scroll right past it and keep going.
→ Session-"

author: Dominik Buksa
body:
"Ultimate fireball :)

Great ideas don't die from a lack of talent. They die in the friction of software setup.

For the  #FigmaMakeathon , my objective was brutal and clear: eliminate the gap between musical inspiration and execution. The result is BomboKo — a phase-locked Web Audio DAW.

The true ""wow"" effect isn't just the raw tech—it's the frictionless human connection. I designed the user journey for global, asynchronous collaboration:
You record vocal harmonies in Warsaw. You click 'Share'. Your producer in Lagos opens a secure 3-day link, listens via a custom player, and downloads the of"

author: Oleh Bernov
body:
"Stop memorizing. Start placing. With  ZINE

 The Method of Loci is an ancient technique used by Greek orators to remember massive amounts of info. But in 2026, we don’t have to do it all in our heads.

I’m created ZINE — an app that lets you ""stick"" information to the physical world using your photos.

How it works:
Capture: Take a photo of a familiar spot on your daily commute.
Anchor: Place a digital ""sticker"" with the info you need to remember.
Recall: Your brain uses spatial memory to link the data to that location.

By turning your city into a giant Memory Palace, we’re making learning as"

For #FigmaMakeathon I built The Listening Room, a browser-based gradient mesh to music generator where you create sound and leave it behind for others to discover. Built as a prototype blending visuals and audio:

– Procedural gradient mesh driving both sound and color
– Each creation is saved in a shared room
– Real-time, interactive audio-visual playback
– Three.js for spatial exploration

Try it out here:  https://chute-tint-71818613.figma.site  

A small step toward rethinking how we experience music online."

body: "TOGO -Never Go Alone Ever wanted to check out that new hiking trail, try a cooking class, or catch a concert -but had no one to go with? Whether you're new in town, branching out of your comfort zone, or just tired of solo weekends -TOGO makes it easy to find your people and start doing more o"

body: "Curtains, cameras, and hand gestures. For the Figma Makeathon I built a small experiment I’ve wanted to try for a long time: controlling a scene with your hands in front of the camera. It started with a simple idea — opening and closing curtains with a gesture. Raise both hands 🙌, the curtains pull apart in 3D, and light floods in. I used three.js and WebGL to make the fabric feel as realistic as possible. Then I kept going. Now different hand signs — 🙌 👍 👎 👌 🤘 ✌️ — each trigger their own music video behind the window. There’s also a small database layer so you can map your own tracks to"

author: Ivy Kolb
body: "What if you could raid the kitchens and still lifes of five centuries of fine art and throw your own picnic with it all? Every food and drink element in the app is cut out from a real painting in the public domain. Monet’s wine bottle. Van Gogh’s lemons. Manet’s oysters. You drag them onto a canvas, arrange and layer them however you want, and compose your own still life inside an ornate gilded frame. When you're done, you title your work, sign it, and hang it in a shared gallery wall alongside everyone else's creations — labeled with a proper museum plaque and everything. It's part collage to"

author: Charlota Blunárová
body:
"I built a multiplayer embroidery sampler for the internet. It's called Common Thread.
It's the first thing I've ever vibecoded with Figma Make!

Try it here!
 https://volt-harp-38332355.figma.site/  

A sampler is a piece of fabric where you practice and collect different stitches — a reference guide and playground in one. They were meant to be passed between hands and added to over time.

Common Thread is that, but the fabric is infinite, the UI is whimsical, and the hands are anonymous.

Every visitor gets a patch on a shared canvas. You pick a thread color, choose a stitch type, and craft y"

author: Brandi Kinard
body: "Living Draft — Perception to Artifact Living Draft is an automated pipeline that turns real-world observation into a published Figma Make site. I walked into Babe & Butcher in Charlotte wearing Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses, captured the space, and wrote creative direction on paper. Those images were saved to an iCloud folder, synced to my Mac, analyzed by Claude's vision API, turned into a Figma Make prompt, submitted by Playwright — all autonomously, no human at the keyboard. The result: a mobile homepage for Babe & Butcher that reflects the actual shop — its colors, its energy, its build-your-"

author: Yuki Kayashima
body:
"Clay — Digital Pottery I wanted to capture the feeling of sitting at a pottery wheel. The hum of the motor, the clay yielding under your fingers, the anticipation of opening the kiln. Shape clay with touch, choose from 6 traditional Japanese glazes (Celadon, Tenmoku, Shino, Oribe), and fire your piece at 1280°C. Save a cinematic poster of what you made. Everything is procedural: 3D geometry, clay textures, kiln sounds, even the export image. No imported assets, just code making art.

 https://canvas-morph-92804902.figma.site/"

author: Ashdrian Spaulding
body:
"Replacement Protocol 
Replacement Protocol reimagines the traditional “edit” interaction as enforced replacement. Instead of allowing users to freely add or modify priorities, the system introduces an interception layer that requires deliberate tradeoffs. When a new opportunity appears, the interface pauses and forces a structured replacement decision. Built entirely in Figma Make, the prototype explores deliberate constraint as a new interaction model shifting from expansion-based design to subtraction-based governance. This project pushes the boundary of interaction design by turning commitm"

author: Michelle Pani
body:
"History is taught in chapters. But it didn't happen in chapters. While the Renaissance was blooming in Italy, the Aztec Empire was at its peak in Mexico.
While Newton was writing his laws of motion, the Qing Dynasty was conquering China. We never see these connections. Because every textbook, every documentary, every timeline shows history one place at a time. I built  Mappistry  to fix that. Click any dot and it draws lines to every other event happening simultaneously anywhere in the world.  5,000 years. 1,800+ events. One map. That's the click that changes how you see history. 🔗 Check it o"

author: Stephanie Asoegwu
body: "My First Figma Makeathon submission  I’ve always wanted a quiet, intentional way to send letters to future me not another productivity app, just a small ritual. This weekend I finally built it with Figma Make. It’s called Future Inbox. You write a message, pick the exact date and time it should return, and seal it in a bottle that drifts out into a digital sea. When the moment comes, that bottle floats back for you to open. I also wanted the world to feel interactive, so the sky shifts with the time of day you’re in: soft sunrise, bright afternoon, or deep night while your bottles bob on the w"

author: İlker Çalım
body:
"We built Undo for files. We never built it for feelings. Emotional Undo is a hold-to-reconsider gesture that catches you right before you send, post, or delete and shows you your emotional temperature in that moment. Not a blocker. Just a breath. The seat belt of digital communication.

 https://sorted-magma-67011003.figma.site"

author: Dawn Saju
body:
"Eclipse.

A cinematic, realtime space operations interface built with React, Three.js, and GSAP. 

It features a fullscreen WebGL globe with live ISS tracking, natural disaster monitoring from four global APIs (NASA EONET, GDACS, NOAA, ISS API), toggleable heatmap and atmosphere layers, and 71 interactive country pins. 

Users can explore a 3D solar system orrery, browse five real NASA mission archives with live imagery from the NASA API, and view unfiltered Mars surface photos from the Curiosity and Perseverance rovers. 

The universe, synchronized in one interface.

Built with Figma Make 🔥
"

author: Daun Lee
body:
"Stamp is your social circle’s living travel log. 

Try the digital postcard platform where every memory is geotagged to an interactive map. 

Sending on Stamp is like sending a 30-second vacation. Image, location, soundtrack, and weather all pinned to your friends' map forever. 

Collect the globe. 

 https://baggy-jargon-95163869.figma.site"

author: Kenneth Madukwe
body:
"Just shipped a physics-inspired competitor intelligence interface for the Figma Makeathon. It transforms static comparison data into three dynamic views: • Orbit — shows market strength through gravitational hierarchy
• Collide — reveals which competitors hold ground under pressure
• Push — visualizes displacement when strategic weight shifts Instead of tables and charts, competitors move based on weighted metrics hence making dominance, vulnerability, and positioning instantly readable. Designed to turn complex competitive landscapes into something you can understand in seconds. Try out  Mult"

author: Anjali kumari
body:
"Ink & Keys 🎹✨ An interactive experiment where words become music. Every keystroke creates a note.
Every sentence shapes a melody.
And different moods — happy, sad, romantic, cinematic — shift the entire soundscape. It feels less like typing
and more like playing something personal.
Would love to know what you feel when you experience it.

author: Rameesa Qureshi
body:
"Try it out here  
Most of us finish a design in Figma Make… but we still don’t truly know how different users will experience it. So I created Zero Reader - an innovation around the classic left/right swipe.

 community post

Have fun!"

El usuario abre el sitio en su computador y conecta su celular, que se transforma en sus ojos dentro de un territorio sagrado interactivo. Al mover el teléfono, explora un paisaje en primera persona. Al apuntar hacia nodos sagrados, despierta la narrativa de cada uno, desbloquea tarjetas coleccionables y activa las narrativas. La experiencia se desarrolla en cuatro actos, donde la narrativa evoluciona  Proyecto https://rotate-drift-21536684.figma.site/ Figma community https://www.figma.com/comm"

author: Carlos Damián
body:

author: Adelyn Soetyono
body:
"We’ve digitized sight. We’ve digitized sound.
We haven’t digitized material.

E-commerce asks us to trust pixels for decisions governed by touch. Tactile brings compression, resistance, and rebound to the screen. 

 https://tactile.figma.site/  

Press a surface. Hold it. Release it. The material responds.
The mattress is the proof of concept. The system extends to anything you wouldn’t buy without touching first.

Material has never been interactive online. Now it is.

--
 Social Post
 Figma Community file"

author: Austin Skhosana
body: "We're in the age of the abundance of code. AI can produce a lot in a lot of different tools but none of it means anything if your team can't find it, trust it, or build on it quickly. Pixel Vault is a private library for design teams to curate, share and remix the vibe coded tools, prototypes, components, and prompts worth keeping. Empowering teams with the language, workflows and syntax to create with clarity.  Not everything AI makes deserves to survive, Pixel Vault is where the curation of your orgs generative tastes begins.  Prototype Link:  https://pixel-vault.figma.site/vault"

author: Killian Le Bras
body:
"Just Draw It is a fast, chaotic party game where humans try to recreate iconic logos and Mr. James Brand (the AI) acts as a brutally honest judge 🥲. Players team up and race against the clock to draw a random logo from memory. When time’s up, Mr. Brand scores the result based on likeness, proportions, and key details, then delivers playful roast-style feedback. The best part: Mr. Brand basically the Gordon Ramsay of logos doesn’t just give a score. He reacts, compares attempts, and turns every round into a shareable highlight 🫠.

You can try it here (limited token 😉💸) :  Just Draw It"

author: Gigi  Wu
body: "Pace  — a new reading interaction Scroll FASTER! Scroll SLOWER! Pace introduces something different: the scroll itself becomes the input. Your scroll velocity is read as intent in real time - and the content shifts to match. →🚀  Scroll fast and a live AI summary catches whatever you skipped.  → 🔎 Scroll slow and a spotlight follows the exact paragraph you're reading.  → Hold still on desktop, pinch on mobile and the paragraph opens up - insight first, then data, then full research.  A page that reads how you read. Also works on mobile. Linkedin   X"

author: Gabriela   Aristizabal
body:

Check it out here:  https://makepiecepattern.figma.site/"

author: Alex Tatu
body:
"Signal simplifies earnings season in the stock market.

It brings earnings data, analyst estimates, price reactions, and company profiles into one clear view, reducing the need to switch between multiple platforms. The app uses market data APIs for earnings information and stock quotes, and includes AI-generated insights to provide simple, plain-language context around each earnings report.  https://halo-swept-00035201.figma.site"

author: jorge santos
body: "What if you could destroy the thoughts that weigh you down? Release is a therapeutic web experience where you write what's heavy, watch it disintegrate into pieces, and feel a bit of lightness. Built in Figma Make, every moment was carefully crafted to feel real. Because sometimes, to move forward, you need to see it being released. →  https://mouse-relink-18762291.figma.site   #FigmaMakeathon @Figma @Contra"

author: Dann Petty
body:
"After I finish a design project,
the last thing I want to do is open Figma again just to make a post image.

I usually default to a plain screenshot.

That's why I built Reframe. (Entirely in Figma Make for the Makeathon!)

Paste any image - and an entire grid of layout options generates instantly.

Pick one. Edit. Copy. Post.

No extra layout.
No extra steps.

Give it a try!
 https://reframeit.figma.site"

author: Léon Kojakian
body: """Biographies"" is an Awwwards-style 2.5D HTML5 Canvas open-world experience where you control a character walking along a winding path through 10 milestone objects that trace Albert Einstein's life — from his birth in Ulm to his legacy. Each milestone has 3 progressive narrative stages (Approach/Discover/Understand) with dynamically positioned story cards, chapter-specific canvas illustrations, aura glows, and orbiting emoji symbols. The character visually evolves based on which milestone you're nearest, all rendered in real-time on canvas with a rich 2.5D depth system."

author: Shikha Verma
body: "Kandinsky's song An interactive sound-painting instrument inspired by Wassily Kandinsky’s belief that colour and form carry musical meaning. In this piece, the canvas becomes a playable composition. Users enter a living abstract painting where geometric shapes are not just visual elements, they are sound generators. Each shape corresponds to a sonic character: circles hum, lines drone, triangles strike, clusters texture. Instead of pressing traditional keys, users compose by arranging form and space. When activated, the canvas transforms from static image into ambient composition. Every elemen"

author: Shania Chacon
body: "A minimal music creation platform where users compose sound, design their own vinyl, and play it by physically opening the sleeve!  https://table-wafer-31673367.figma.site/"

author: Zechariah Ward
body:
"Most infrastructure tools show logs.

LogicVeil shows responsibility. I built a spatial architecture workspace where systems become interactive blobs. You can expand a codebase into its internal nodes, drag people into ownership roles, and visualize maintenance schedules and key rotations in a unified timeline overlay. LogicVeil doesn’t crawl GitHub or read secrets. It runs on a curated .veil.json metadata export — containing nodes, relationships, and operational policies, but never .env values, tokens, or source code. The goal was to explore how interaction design can make invisible operation"

author: Panje Nambao
body:
"Social platforms flattened relationships into binary states: follow, mute, block.
But real relationships aren’t that simple.  I find the settings on a slot of these platforms frustrating so Continuum explores a new interaction architecture where relational depth and attention are treated as separate forces.
In this prototype: - Relational Depth controls what someone can see of you (vulnerability flow). - Attention Weight controls how prominently someone appears in your feed (attention flow). 
Rather than building a full-featured social app, this prototype isolates the interaction and system lo"

author: Rujuta   Sondur
body: "AI chatbots are increasingly used like personal therapists. But what are they actually inferring about you when they generate responses? I built Advice Lens in Figma Make to expose the emotional inference layer behind AI replies. Instead of hiding interpretation, it visualizes detected emotions, confidence levels, and dynamically shifts advice styles so users can see and influence how they’re being understood. Try free typing or explore a scripted scenario to see how tone analysis and reframing change the output. Built for the Figma Makeathon. https://repeat-robust-41503137.figma.site #FigmaMa"

author: Ruben van As
body:
"In a digital world we tend to miss nuance in our current interfaces. This pattern is aimed at making our communication online more human!

Please try it out for yourself and let me know what you think!

Link:  https://emotion-nuance-detection.figma.site/"

author: Cynthia Rodriguez
body: "I built this in a day because I just found out about this challenge 🫰🏼originally I had a complex project in mind but I had to pivot because I realized even though AI was building it, I had to feed it as though it was a basic level developer reading user stories without any discussion between Product Manager and Engineer (I felt the flashbacks). I got inspired by Jim Collins on the Tim Ferriss podcast, whom spoke about how he rates his days from -2 to 2 and changes his habits based on his findings. I added some AI analysis but it was not 100% bulletproof as I used the Free Tier API from Gemin"

author: Erika Pelser
body: "One HTML file. Zero dependencies. Your screen recorder just got a soul baby!! the makeR is a browser-based screen recording tool where your webcam bubble reacts to YOU. Smile and it glows gold with sparkles. Speak and a purple aura pulses with your voice. Pinch your fingers and the screen zooms. Your background disappears — replaced with a  Make.com  gradient — and your head pops out of the frame like it's breaking the fourth wall. Built for the Figma Makeathon 2026. All effects run in real-time using MediaPipe Face Mesh (468 landmarks), Selfie Segmentation, Hand Tracking, and Web Audio — comp"

author: Malick Murtaza
body: "I’m a little late to the party with this one, but when you’re building a living, breathing AI with a short fuse, you don’t rush the process. Designers, we’ve all been there: The logic is flawless. The grid is perfect. Then... the feedback arrives. ""Can we make it more... blue-ish?"" Inner Designer is my latest experimental project that says what you can't. Built with Figma Make, a live backend, and an AI brain that actually understands design theory, it doesn’t just design—it defends. The Hook: It explains its choices. The Twist: It has a breaking point. The Result: Push it too far with bad rev"

author: Andrés Naudín
body:

• A custom behavioral detection system analyzes hundreds of conversational patterns (honesty, humor, philosophy, confrontation…)
• Different unique endings based on how you actually talk, not pre-scripted choices #FigmaMakeathon   #FigmaMake   #Figma"

author: Lee Black
body:
"Imagine treating scroll like an instrument, not a wheel. For #FigmaMakeathon I built ScrollFolio in #FigmaMake. A scroll focus portfolio where scrolling and mousing becomes navigation, pacing, and focus, with “sticky” moments that lock in and release cleanly. Also works with simple keys (← → ↑ ↓ and Spacebar) to open/close items. I leaned into what Make is good at: fast iteration on interaction timing, responsiveness, and polish. The goal wasn’t a static layout, it was a working fast interaction that feels physical across devices in a single view.

Published project on Desktop and Mobile (has "

author: Ibrahim Tejuosho
body: "Scout — my Figma Makeathon entry Your next customers are on Reddit, X, and Quora right now, describing the exact problem you solve. The issue isn't finding them — it's that every tool stops at the mention. You still have to qualify the lead and write something that doesn't sound like spam. Scout handles both. It scores leads by fit and intent, then drafts a reply in your brand voice — ready to send in one click. Full prototype: onboarding, live scanning animation, swipeable feed, replies tracker with conversion funnel, and brand voice editor. 🔗  https://outer-pupil-54868266.figma.site/"

author: Kofi Wood
body: "I built Trace Muse for the 2026 Figma Make Hackathon. Using Figma Make as a creative partner, I explored a simple question: What if we reimagined scrolling as tracing? Trace Muse is a dreamy interactive experience where users check in with how they feel, choose a color and topic, and reveal short learning bites by tracing through biomorphic shapes. The goal was to create something that does not just function well, but also reflects taste, atmosphere, and a different kind of digital experience, one that feels calm, surreal, and culturally expressive. For me, this project is about using creativi"

author: Paige Latimer
body: "vintage photobooths are having a moment, and i’m obsessed. but sometimes the booth isn’t nearby… and sometimes the person isn’t either. duet booth  lets you take photos together, even when you’re not. snap 4 pics → send a link → your friend steps in. we stitch it into one gritty, vintage strip. download it. print it. tape it to your wall like it’s 2007. ...selfies also accepted 🫶🏻"

author: Rosmery Fabián
Yuki Kayashima
body:
"VibeForge

Everyone has a vibe, but no one's ever seen theirs. Answer 4 questions about your ideal view, how you spend Friday nights, what you reach for, what superpower you'd pick. VibeForge forges a one-of-a-kind 3D identity from your answers. 30 possible results, each with its own shape, material, color palette, and personality reading. The 3D reacts live as you answer. By the time your vibe name is revealed, the shape is already yours. All procedural: simplex noise vertex deformation, 6 morph targets, 7 physical materials, text scramble reveal. No imported 3D assets, just math making art.
"

author: RUCHIKA
body: "DEAUTRIX -  Stop app switching. Start shared thinking Deautrix eliminates intent friction by removing the need to jump between multiple apps when people collaborate. Designed in Figma, it introduces Ephemeral Shared Space, which is an intelligent OS-level layer that dynamically merges relevant data when users are nearby or pursuing a shared goal. This high-fidelity prototype demonstrates how social planning can become seamless and context-aware. Deautrix represents the future of agentic UX that adapts to human behaviour in real time. By making screens respond to proximity and shared intent, De"

would you stay silent?
