Check it out yourself:  silence-chamber.figma.site"

author: Sebastian Lumbi
body: "Very last minute, i tried, so for this Make-a-thon, I built Mana: a visual productivity tool for creative AI exploration. Instead of rigid external apps, Mana lets you generate custom tools—like timers or task lists—on the fly. As a visual person, I designed it to transform long AI text streams into structured UI components, like grids of cards, on an infinite canvas. To reduce mouse reliance, I integrated a mobile controller: you use your phone in one hand to navigate and move elements, while your other hand stays on the keyboard for input. It’s an ambidextrous, real-time synced workspace tha"

author: Malle Abhinay
body:
"Hi! Ever unlocked your phone and felt overwhelmed by notifications, while the important one was buried? That’s why I built Buzz Off — an intelligent lockscreen that prioritizes OTPs, banking alerts, and important messages, while organizing everything else into a clean, expandable summary. Built entirely in Figma Make with dynamic themes, tactile UI, and smooth native-like interactions. Try the interactive prototype:

body:

author: Begüm Özdoğan
body:
"Aether – Design in the air 🍃

Aether is a gesture-powered drawing playground where your finger becomes the pen.
Explore here:  https://award-gala-86638804.figma.site/

 Point to draw.
Open your palm to pause.

Community Link:  https://www.figma.com/community/file/1610497557392550104/aether-hand-tracking-design-app"

author: Halyna Sabat
body:
"SCAN.AI 

 The MCP is moving AI out of the cloud and directly into the physical world. How it works:
The Source: A business owner provides specific data, services, and rules.
The Portal: The platform generates a localized QR code for that specific space.
The Experience: When a user scans it, they unlock a suite of AI agents, strictly grounded in the owner’s data.
(QR Codes to try, you can find in the comments)


 Linkedin
 Figma Community"

author: David Hwang
body: "some of us need to pause before the day starts. i built HAY — How Are You? — a 5-minute daily check-in that asks you honest questions out loud and reflects your mood back to you. no static designs, just 116 prompts and a vision. built entirely in @figma Make using references, design language, and a lot of iteration. it's not perfect — the voice is still too robotic and there's so much more to explore — but the concept is real. we all need to externalize how we're doing sometimes. pause, reflect, understand where you are today so you can move forward with intention. can't wait for you to try it"

author: Antony Raphy
body:
"Before You Send

I’ve been thinking about how much tone shapes outcomes in conversations.

For the Figma Makeathon, I built a minimal messaging companion that introduces a small but powerful interaction: a pause.

Through a subtle long-press gesture, the interface surfaces a rewrite layer that helps reframe emotional or unclear messages in both work and personal chats without disrupting flow.

Behind the scenes, the system responds differently to strong language, defensive tone, or vague replies, adapting suggestions based on context rather than applying a generic rewrite.

It’s a small intera"

author: Karol Serarols
body: "There used to be a time when sharing a moment meant choosing it carefully. Wrote a few words, sealed an envelope, and let it travel… Using  Figma Make , I designed this as a way to reconnect with what was lost.  A Postcard From Me  is not for an audience, but for one person.  Not to perform, but to be present.  LinkdIn Post:  https://www.linkedin.com/posts/karol-serarols_figmamakeathon-figmamakeathon-ugcPost-7434422109788950528-bppg?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAEa1R0cBpNYCdDQUR6QeFbPlY5XCutt-jBE   Figma Community Post:  https://www.figma.com/community/file/16104880414849"

author: MD YUSUF
body: "ENTROPY — Where humanity's greatest art dies, evolves, and becomes something never seen before. Every click cracks the canvas. Every scroll melts the paint. Every hover chars the surface. When the art dies, AI absorbs the chaos you created and births something entirely new — shaped by your unique behavior. No two visitors see the same thing. No moment can be revisited. Built for #FigmaMakeathon using Figma Make."

author: Mahdi nour
body: "Can a complete beginner build a living, breathing global universe in just one night? With Figma Make, the answer is of course yes. PULSE is a real-time emotional canvas where over 1.8 million heartbeats connect. Every interaction, from the unique 'breathing' animations to the dynamic color shifts and responsive layouts, was generated and refined through AI prompts in Make alone. ​This prototype demonstrates how Figma Make can unlock complex ideas and shape culture by turning human emotion into a shared, interactive UI no manual coding required. ​#figmamakeathon #FigmaMake #AI #InteractionDesig"

author: Zainab Shaikh
body:
"I asked one question while building this: What if your interface knew how you were feeling? Not in a data-collection way. In a human way. Soul UI is my answer. An emotional operating system that transforms its entire design language based on your mood — colors, typography, animations, sound, and even a handwritten letter from your emotion to you. Built with Figma Make for #FigmaMakeathon Because great design should make you feel something:
Try here:  https://grit-ribbon-77699016.figma.site/  
Editable figma :  https://www.figma.com/make/uNUNa3p9L2jHKLrRbfZMZi/Soul-UI-Dashboard-App?p=f&t=8tALY7"

author: MD YUSUF
body: I'm truly honored and overwhelmed that my project- Entropy was just featured on Contra! # figmamakeathon

author: Md Mahdi Hasan
body:
"Most of our digital lives are built for the 'now'—disposable feeds and cluttered cloud folders. But what if software felt like stone? I built Monumentum with  Figma Make  to bridge the gap between generations. It’s a digital sanctuary where memories aren't just uploaded; they are sealed. The Story:
I’ve always wondered how my grandfather lived, but those stories were lost because there was no place to 'keep' them. I designed this for the grandchildren I haven't met yet—a way to leave a celestial map of our lives.

Try it here:
 https://buffet-run-99426884.figma.site

author: Jesus Custodio II
body:
"Last entry for Figma Makeathon! Thank you and enjoy!

Ever wanted to control the ocean? Now you can. Guide the 'Night Waves' in this immersive experience where your gestures dictate the environment. Grow the moon to swell the tides, or point to the sky to scatter twinkling stars across the dark. The horizon is yours to command.

Notes: 
Spread your fingers to grow the moon & stir the waves.
Point your index finger to paint stars in the sky.
Must have camera on to enjoy this experience. 
(*Just added a mouse fallback if needed*)

author: Kimber Washington
body:
"https://knob-widget-40297376.figma.site  
I built an interactive ASL learning experience called ‘Ember.’ It turns language learning into a physical, rhythmic interaction using camera-based gesture tracing. This project explores embodied learning, where interaction becomes language, and movement becomes understanding."

author: Prabhav Srivastava
body:
"What if your voice was a gas pedal? I built Wowdrive for #FigmaMakeathon — a racing game with zero controllers. 🗣️ Speak LOUDER → car goes faster
🤫 Go quiet → car slows down
👤 Tilt head LEFT → car steers left
👤 Tilt head RIGHT → car steers right No keyboard. No mouse. No touch. No buttons.
Your volume is the accelerator. Your head is the wheel. This is what interaction design looks like when the interface disappears completely. Built entirely in Figma Make. 🎤 Try it:  https://tower-plasma-28416019.figma.site/    #Figma #InteractionDesign

 https://x.com/Prabhavthinks/status/20285302593695"

author: lavanya karpagam
body: "Project Title: Venting Vault Tagline: A Multi-Sensory Ritual for Emotional Release & Inclusive Healing. The Story (Problem & Concept) We live in a world where stress is constant, but digital tools for ""letting go"" are often just static lists or simple buttons. I wanted to create a space that feels tactile, physical, and deeply personal. Venting Vault isn't just an app; it’s a digital ritual. It takes your stress—whether typed or shouted—and turns it into a journey of destruction and reconstruction. The goal was to bridge the gap between our emotions and the digital interface. figma make link: "

author: Akshay Wani
body:
"I built a ""Build Your Motorcycle"" configurator using Figma Make. You can change parts, and see the bike update in real time. It started as simple frames in Figma and turned into a fully interactive experience. For bike asset I used some Pinterest images and vectorised them on Figma.

The trickiest part was managing states and variants without breaking the layout logic. Built entirely with Figma Make. Try it here:  https://donut-color-26976553.figma.site"

Additional features: 
- Adjust brush size & "

⭐ Meet Pixel: I hand-drew an NPC in Procreate to add a personal touch; I’m a sucker for pixel art!
⭐ Full retro vibes: from the palette to the 8-bit sound fx, everything i"

author: Debashis Sarker
body:
"As an independent freelancer, my work life is not very colorful; rather, it is confined to the small world where I live. No matter how the world unfolds, I come back to my same old nest where I find my inner peace... myself... from where I see the days go by outside the window. I tried to capture my little world inside a mobile, mimicking how my work life goes each day: work... music... sleep... repeat.

try it yourself -  https://inside-app-30256951.figma.site"

body: "Hey Contra! I’m joining the   Makeathon with  Qeam  - a gamified RPG workspace for remote teams. Most remote work tools feel like spreadsheets with anxiety: No visibility. No personalization. No motivation. With Qeam you can: 😍 Turn tasks into  Earn XP, streaks, and achievements 😊 Upgrade your team space as you ship work 😍 Discover hidden easter eggs (because teams deserve personality too) And honestly, I’m still a bit shocked by how capable Figma Make is - you can integrate with APIs and build features like managing music streams right inside the experience. Try the live prototype: https:/"

author: Alvis @ Designlabb
body: "This is Ink It. The first online Chinese Calligraphy practice platform, built entirely with Figma Make. Calligraphy is one of the oldest art forms in Chinese culture. It's meditative. It's expressive. And for most people outside of Asia, it's completely out of reach. 2 ways you can go about using Ink It:  1) Mouse mode - write strokes using your mouse or trackpad 2) Hand mode - use your camera to write in the air, just like real ink on paper Strokes not looking right? Toggle on ‘Accurate mode’ and the ink ‘snaps’ into the correct calligraphic path as you follow the stroke order. It's forgiving"

author: Nanako Arisaka
body: "Holding onto unspoken thoughts before you sleep?🌙 https://dock-flaky-95618102.figma.site   We all have those ""unsent"" messages—cluttering our minds, with nowhere to go. Introducing UNSENT, a ritual to transform mental noise into absolute stillness. Pour it out: Externalize your shadows into the text field. Dynamic Dissolve: Watch your words shatter and scatter with 15 unique, random patterns. The 4-4-8 Ritual: Re-center with 3 sets of guided breathing, finding peace in the newfound space. Don’t send it. Release it. Find your quiet with UNSENT."

author: Steven Grey
body: "What if pinch-to-zoom-out worked on meaning, not pixels? Squeeze reimagines it as semantic. Pinch any text and it compresses in meaning. Paragraphs fold into sentences. Sentences collapse into phrases. Phrases crunch into keywords. The animation feels physical, like wringing out a sponge. Release, and it springs back to full detail. Lock at any density you want. One gesture. No UI to learn. Works on articles, code, chats, comment threads, anything. Check it out here:  https://number-oil-14831362.figma.site Built entirely in Figma Make for the #FigmaMakeathon."

body: "1 Interface, the only one. Every tool we've ever loved knew what we came to do. It shaped itself around the task before we said a word. AI gave us a box. The same box for everything. We lost the tools that understood us. OneInterface gives that back, but starts with language. The best of both worlds.  A blank canvas. A blinking cursor. You type what's on your mind, and the right interface forms around you, quietly, as you speak.  Say you need a flight. A writing tool. A plan maker. A code assistant. The controls appear the moment the intent does. You keep talking, or you reach out and touch wh"

Deadline is distance.
Complexity is mass. Excited to"

author: Nidhi Singh
body:
"Nobody tells the truth when someone asks how they're doing. I built the place for the real answer. One idea. One night. One question: what if your feelings had somewhere to live instead of disappearing into the day? Type how you actually feel, whatever words you have, and your whole world changes color. Happy → Golden 
Loved → Rose 
Calm → Aqua 
Sad → Steel blue Write what happened. Keep this moment. Over time, your feelings become a Memory — each one preserved in the color it was actually felt. Not edited. Not softened. The real one. That's Felt! Maybe you need it today.

App Link:  https://p"

author: Bansi Manvar
body:
"Hi, I’m Bansi Manvar 👋 I recently participated in the Figma Makethon and used the opportunity to experiment with something fun — an AR Visualizer for interior design. The idea is simple: before printing or buying artwork, you can see how it will look on your wall in real space. What it can do:
• Upload artwork and place it on your wall
• Move and scale using hand gestures
• Control it with 90+ voice commands
• Try gallery wall templates
• Preview real-world sizes before printing or buying Built with React, TypeScript, Three.js, MediaPipe, and Web APIs — exploring how AR + AI can simplify inte"

author: Donna Tang
body: "Using FigmaMake for the second time and created Postcard Garden 🌱 Everything is digital now, so I created a digital postcard app. You draw your alphabet to create a custom handwriting font, pick a beautiful landscape from anywhere in the world, and write your message. Watch it appear on your postcard in your own handwriting. Then you send it to the Postcard Garden and see other people's handwritten postcards appearing in real time from all over the world. I built this to bring the warmth of handwriting and connections in a digital space. Something that feels intimate and authentic (and cute!)"

• Duration differences
• Motion properties
• Combined transformations Each interaction includes:
• Custom cursor system

• Dynamic round logic
• Expressive motion feedback

author: Muhammed Fuwad P K
body:
"⚠️ Open the link in your browser and allow camera access for a new 3D experience. Link: https://3d-interactive-experience.figma.site/  

This project transforms any browser into a spatial window using real-time head tracking. Instead of dragging to rotate a 3D object, you simply move your head and the scene responds instantly, creating natural motion parallax and the illusion that digital objects exist behind your screen. No VR. No controllers. Just a webcam. Built in Figma Make, introduces a new interaction -  look to explore 👀

author: Supreet @rlvnt
body:
"My hardest rodeo, for the grandest prize!
Here's what this entry is all about:
Being someone who spends 75% of my day on the web, I know that the web at times feels “template-ish.”
Maybe in the quest to create the most creative designs, we ended up ignoring the core fundamentals that make innovation feel “creative.”
With this in mind, I tried to assign an interaction to the web’s most commonly used actions and icons. Not only that, I redefined galleries, carousels, endless scrolling, gestures (hold the screen to enter the sphere and double tap to reset), design language, SVGs, and SVG animatio"

author: Amisha Khemka
body: "I absolutely love postcards, so for the Figma makeathon challenge, I had to make a digital postcard maker. I wanted to build that tactile, warm joy of sitting down with a blank postcard and making something personal. Pick a destination. Draw on the front. Drag and place stickers. Flip it over, write your message, stamp it. Copy and send. Every interaction was designed to feel physical: the paper texture, the stamp snap, the sticker peel, the card flip. The kind of thing that makes you smile while you're using it. It’s not entirely mobile optimized for now, but there’s scope for sure. Check it "

author: Alexander Dubinin
body:
"Most “requirements” are not requirements. They are assumptions. That’s why I built Product Clarity Lab for the Figma x Contra Makeathon. Paste a rough requirement. Get an instant Clarity Score. The tool highlights what’s missing across Core Clarity, Scope, and Project Risk, and generates focused questions you can answer or send directly to stakeholders. As you add the missing information, the score increases and the requirement becomes structured, precise, and build-ready. The result is simple: Requirements everyone understands. Less ambiguity. Less rework. More clarity.

 https://spree-volume"

author: Wojciech Ziółkowski
body:
"https://glide-lilac-97505722.figma.site  

AiCursor puts context directly at your cursor. Highlight anything on screen, hold a key, and AI actions appears instantly around it — definitions, conversions, summaries, actions — tailored to exactly what is highlighted. Your focus never breaks."

author: Leon Haffie-Hobday
body:
"StepSync - Put the beat under your feet

Walking in time with music boosts your mood — so why shouldn’t it happen with every step? Why can’t you always feel like you’re living in an Edgar Wright film? I took this weekend’s Contra #FigmaMakeathon as an opportunity to finally build an idea I’ve had since I was a kid. Made entirely using Figma and Figma Make over the weekend, StepSync adjusts a song’s tempo to your walking, running, or dancing pace — so that every step you take lands perfectly in time with the music. You can try out the concept with a few demo tracks yourself
👉  https://stepsync"

author: Gary McEneaney

author: Popil art
body:
"Floatnote Calendar, designed by Popil, is a minimal music-sharing calendar player. It recreates the feeling of listening on vinyl — bringing back the romance of mailed poetry in a digital form. Through music, it invites you to step out of reality and into a daydream. Create your own monthly music postcard to share both sound and sentiment — a quieter alternative to the noise of social media. Each day, listen to one carefully selected track and generate a digital postcard to share. You can also personalize your postcard and stamps, so it feels like sending poetry through the mail again.

Enjoy!"

author: Anushka Katiyar
body: "We text “thinking of you.” But what if we could also convey it through a simulated presence? That’s why I built   ""Presence""  as an interaction for the #FigmaMakeathon – a way to send more than words, GIFs or emoticons. With a simple press-and-hold, your emotion travels to someone else’s phone – as colour, rhythm, motion, and haptic pulses. They don’t just see your message; they feel it. Calm, feeling warm, excited, or simply “I’m here with you”. And when they hold back, the connection becomes reciprocal, real, and human. It’s a way to bring human presence into digital conversation – the part "

These can be a jingling key, a squeezing bottle and many, many more (the possibilities are infinite🚀!). If you liked it, feel free to share it :)"

author: Aleyna Çatak
body:

This prototype explores an operating-system-level interaction model where movement and commitment are separated. 💆‍♀️  Face-to-Navigate:  subtle head movement controls exploration

😚 Pucker-to-Confirm: a lip gesture signals explicit intent

👀 Intent-Aware Mode: apps request different confirmation thresholds. In sparse interfaces, movement is enough.
In dense environments, actions require intention. What starts as a playful question  “what if you had to blow a kiss to"

Focus is a Mac only task management app, not for businesses or teams but for individuals and freelancers.

With Focus, you are in full control of your tasks even if there’s a sudden urgent task that needs to be taken care of. 
No need to readjust your plans because you are not bound by a fixed task time. 

Human + AI made it happen:

This challenge was probably"

author: Jae Messersmith
body: "My submission for #FigmaMakeathon. While I was listening to a podcast, the hosts started apologizing for creating duplicate episodes. They weren't able to track their previous episodes and had no idea they were repeating an entire show. I built Podmemo to better organize and find stories, plan for episodes, and track what has already been done. How Podmemo can help: Planning an episode: Add listener stories and pitches, tag them by theme, search and filter your library, and slot them into upcoming episodes all in one place. Sponsorships: Track brand deals, write scripts, and add them to episod"

author: niraj dev
body: "Why look at a textbook when you can step inside the experiment? 🥚💧 I just ""shipped"" my latest project for the #FigmaMakeathon: An interactive, physics-based simulation of the classic Egg Density Experiment. The Problem: Most science education is static. We see a drawing of an egg sinking or floating, but we don't participate in the change in density. The Solution: Using Figma Make, I’ve transformed this 2D concept into a ""Living Lab."" 🚀 Check out the live site here:  https://theegg-experiment-67387903.figma.site"

author: Carlos Damián
body:
"Noodles  is a web application for image and video processing based on visual flows (node-based). You connect nodes by dragging them onto a canvas to create editing pipelines—from simple adjustments to animations and video export—all in the browser, without a backend. 

 https://noodles-app.figma.site  

Import images — drag files onto the canvas or use the Media node Adjust color — brightness, contrast, saturation & hue Apply filters — grayscale, sepia, invert, blur, etc. Visual effects — glitch, pixelate, vignette, noise, posterize & more Crop — free crop with position & size controls Compres"

author: Anh-Minh VU
body:
"Loup-Garou is a real-time social deduction and bluffing game played entirely on smartphone, designed for groups of 10 to 40 players. Orchestrated by a Game Master from a dedicated interface, each player secretly discovers their role by flipping their virtual card — and the hunt begins. The village alternates between Night phases, where creatures act in the shadows, and Day phases, where villagers debate and vote to eliminate a suspect. Every decision matters. Every silence is suspicious.

Check it out !  https://loups-garou.figma.site/"

I wanted to showcase different elements of a Japanese kissaten — a traditional, retro-style Japanese coffee shop, known for their classic, nostalgic drinks and desserts. You can interact with elements in the cafe, choose your own menu, and most importantly, make your own drink! Take a polaroid photo once you have your drink :) I had fun utilizing the wonderful features of Figma Make and Figma Draw to make all the visual assets 😊  Check out this  Figma site Here is the link to the  Figma Community file   Check it out on  X:"

An embedded AI colleague. Built with @figma Make for #FigmaMakeathon."

author: Marigold Hua
body: "Don’t let the season stop you from shaping culture. Mooncake Makeria explores iconic point and click + Papa’s -ia style game interactions through my cultural lens. Lately, I’ve been following the thread between medieval European ornament, modern AI systems, 2025 Config glyphs, and classic East Asian patterns. This project explores that overlap by blurring the boundary between physical and digital interfaces, using the keyboard as a translator for pressing and carving gestures. It’s a playful system where a childlike keyboard smash transforms into ornament 'n artwork.  Curious? Give it a go bel"

author: Yash Moharir
body:
"Sonart  🎵🎶🎼 Where Sound Meets Art What if every drawing had a sound? Sonart transforms drawings into music in real-time. Five colors, five instruments — Piano, Drums, Strings, Claps, Bass. Vertical position controls pitch. Thickness controls volume. Pentatonic scale ensures everything sounds musical, no matter what you draw. Draw a circle - it becomes a loop.
Upload a photo - hear what it sounds like. 
Put on headphones - sound moves left to right as the playhead sweeps your canvas. Tempo control, 
loop toggle, 
snap for perfect lines, 
glowing visual feedback, 
light/dark mode, 
save as PN"

author: Anuran Mukherjee

For #FigmaMakeathon I built Vapourwave Space Shooter in #FigmaMake: a full-screen arcade space shooting game with rhythmic vapourwave soundscape, which comes alive with your hand.

So instead of removing the screen, I used it. It’s a simple room that reacts to ambient sound by changing the background, so what you hear shapes what you see. And it feels worth it.
350 prompts and 12 Diet Cokes later, here’s everythin"

Large phones make buttons hard to reach. The Dial moves everything to the 'Natural Zone', the bottom corner where your thumb rests or where it is most comfortable for you. Inspired by the old-school rotary phone dial. Why it works: No Stretching: Everything stays within thumb’s reach. Movability: Slide the dial to either side to match your grip. Contextual: Icons change automatically (e.g., Feed vs. Chat). The best part? You get mas"

Yeah. That’s what this is about. This is just the beginning - a prototype I’ve been building with Figma Make AI 🤍 to solve a problem I’ve had too many times. In the future can be opened on any bag product page, so you can drag in your items (phone, lipgloss, hairbrush, laptop, water, whatever you carry daily) and see if they actually fit. Visually. In real scale🪄 Very early"

• Visual tone shifts with cognitive load

Focus — structured breakdown with intentional hierarchy
Inspired — expressive, non-linear idea capture The core interaction is a circular drag-based selector with live visual feedback before confirmation — reinforcing conscious mode selection. Shift isn’t about doing more.

The Brutal Truth receives your deepest, darkest imposter thoughts and gives you:

author: Luke Fielding
body:
"What if music wasn’t just something you hear, but something you respond to visually? I built a live visual instrument in Figma Make. It listens to sound in real time. Bass, mids, transients and energy  become a shifting visual form. As I play, the form breathes, stretches, fractures, stabilises. And I can respond to that. It creates a different kind of feedback loop:

play → see → feel → adjust. I’ve always thought visuals in music should be more than decorative - stage lighting, projection, branding. What happens when the visual layer becomes part of the instrument? This is an early prototype"

author: Gracie Williams

 Stop overthinking in a vacuum. Inner Council is voice-first AI experience where you summon different archetypes to help you think through... whatever you're thinking through. Pick who you want at the table, ask your question, and see what they say. Almost like a Magic 8-ball, but with multiple personalities.

It's not about getting *the answer*. It's about getting perspective.

Check it out and let me know if it gives ya any good advice!"

Across multiple contexts: books, browser tabs, designer portfolio cards, and news articles, the same interaction gesture reveals hidden layers: Digital Book Reader: Tilt to preview the next or previous page. Browser Tabs: Lean to explore other open tabs in a spatial fan. Designer Team Card: Peek behind the card to view "

The compiler then translates your raw thoughts into a structured, 5-stage blueprint using the AI Experience (AIX) Framework. By forcing the AI to understand your context before it answers, you get exponentially better outputs. How it was built: The entire application, including the facial-tracking interaction and spatial UI, was built 100% o"

A shared wall where you and your friends can pin your photos, videos, and notes together no matter how far apart you are. author: Dibyendu Chatterjee
body:
"I built a cognitive operating system to challenge reaction culture. It’s called ClarityOS. We live in a world that rewards fast opinions and impulsive decisions.
ClarityOS encourages something different: structured reflection. You define a decision, separate emotion from logic, evaluate risk, and simulate its impact across 1 week, 1 year, and 5 years. It doesn’t tell you what to choose.
It improves the way you think before you choose. The goal isn’t productivity.
It’s decision health. If adopted widely, tools like this could shift us from reactive choices to intentional ones. Try it:  https://"

author: Anchit Gupta

author: Gracie Williams
body:
"unfortunately, we're all a bit brainrotted. myself included.

that's why I made  focusfocus , a live monitor for your attention span. when you look away from your computer to go doomscroll reels, a light ambient buzz plays at increasing volume in your headphones until you come back to what you were working on. an *actually helpful tool made to run in the background of your digital life. also, included a little bts clip at the end with my gaze tracking experimentation process. 

"

author: Benny Hermes
body: "For this Figma Makeathon, I brought together 2 things, one that genuinely fascinates me and the other which is a daily aspect of my life - space and personal finances. As someone who’s always been drawn to the universe (the depth, the mystery, the sense of scale) I started thinking about finances in a similar way. As a family man, keeping track of finances is essential… but let’s be honest, it can also feel overwhelming and heavy. So I decided to combine both worlds. A Financial Universe, where finances become a system of planets and orbits. Instead of staring at cold numbers and spreadsheets,"

author: sairaj bhagat
body: "Have you noticed the tiny “hide / show replies” button under Instagram comments? It’s so small it frustrates me. That’s when I realized: as designers, we’re basically collecting taste all day; & then forgetting it 3 seconds later. So for the Figma x Contra challenge, I built Notice. Anytime you’re using an app and something feels designed, you tap Notice. The screen freezes, a lens shows up, and you frame the exact detail you noticed. Then you note your observation & save it as “inspired” or “to avoid.” The interaction language is inspired by cameras: a viewfinder lens, a focus pull, a shutter"

author: Tivadar Ekpo
body:
"We struggled to secure a lawyer for an NDA.
That frustration sparked a question: why do I even need one? That’s how Pacta began — with one idea: trust should be visible.
Agreements shouldn’t vanish in chats or paperwork. On Pacta, I click “Start a Pact.”
I drag one avatar to another — the agreement forms instantly. We set terms, enable escrow, choose a payment plan.
It adjusts to local regulations automatically. Both parties verify with face and voice — or fingerprint and face. Funds lock. The contract activates. Trust scores update. No ambiguity.
Just a promise — structured and secure. Shouto"

As a person with Protan Color Blindness (red-green deficiency), I wasn't aware that there were tools that try to help with this issue. But once I tried one that didn't do what I was expecting, I felt the need to build one myself using Figma Make, hoping that this can bring some joy to other people like me. https://clock-boar-53691613.figma.site"

author: Sadie levy
body: "consumetheinternet is a reimagining of 'scrolling'. Every post, perspective, fact, and piece of misinformation shapes who you are within today's culture. On an open internet canvas, you move through organic, floating shapes, each an artifact of the internet. When you consume them, you change. Your size, speed, and shape shift. The experience makes visible what usually feels invisible: how constant exposure subtly rewires your mood, beliefs, attention span, and sense of self. The project also explores how access shapes identity. Censorship, service outages, inaccessible design, tech inequity, a"

author: mayu sugiura
body:
"I built a digital screen-printing piece where you draw a plate, layer ink, and end up with a one-of-a-kind mark. It’s called LayerPress STUDIO. Give it a try:  https://near-modal-33190727.figma.site   LayerPress STUDIO recreates the core mechanics of screen printing:
Black = mesh (blocks ink) / White = holes (ink passes through).
You design hole patterns on a “plate,” print through it, then change colors/offsets and layer again.  
The magic is in misregistration and layering.
Tiny offsets and stacked colors create unexpected depth—an analog “happy accident” feel inside a digital workflow."

author: Ogheneovie Akpome-Ebojoh
body: "Constellation A multidimensional reputation study Most platforms reduce people to a single rating that shapes trust and opportunity. Constellation does it differently. Instead of five stars, users adjust Trust, Empathy, Reliability, and Energy. Each change reshapes a live constellation in real time. Multiple contributors overlap without averaging. The prototype uses sliders, motion, and layered blending to model complex perception. Prototype:  https://sample-chip-13381847.figma.site   Potential applications include hiring platforms, team feedback systems, creator communities, and peer review n"

author: Hulya Masharipov
body: "I built a collaborative pixel art town that folds itself into 3D. It's called Paper Town. Try it here →  https://spice-bunch-07751270.figma.site/   Here's how it works: you get a 16×16 pixel grid. Draw anything — a pattern, a face, your mood, whatever. Pick a building shape. Then watch your flat drawing fold up into a 3D isometric building, like paper rising off a table. Your building lands on a shared town map. Zoom out and you'll see dozens of other people's pixel art sitting next to yours as buildings. A heart pattern cafe. A striped tower. Something completely unreadable that somehow works"

author: Prarthan B
body:
"I built an interactive book about my design career inside Figma Make. It's called Design Chronicles. Try it here!

A voice-controlled experience where you navigate my journey through a 3D book model ,speak to turn pages, explore chapters, and move through time. The book is 3D. The navigation is voice-driven. And all of it lives inside Figma Make. Figma Make made me focus on how it should feel, not how to ship it.🔥"

author: Joshua Odoi
body:
"Remember the last time someone wrote you a letter by hand? Not a text. Not an email. A letter. Inkwell brings that feeling to the screen. You type on one side, and on the other, a pen writes your words in real time. But here's the part that matters: when they open it, they don't just read your letter. They watch it being written. 
No skipping ahead. Just presence. In a world that optimized every form of communication for speed, Inkwell is a small act of resistance. A reminder that some things deserve to take their time. Built with Figma Make. Written with care.

Try it here :  https://buy-blan"

author: Rachna Ravi
body:
"We interact with video the same way we did 20 years ago - click, scrub, pause. I wanted to ask a different question: what if your hand was the interface? So I created a  gesture controlled video player .

This project reimagines video control through static hand poses. No buttons. No cursor. Just intent, translated into action.
Each gesture maps to a specific trigger. A held pose becomes a command. It shifts the interaction model from finding the control to becoming the control.

This is an exploration in embodied UI, where the body isn't adapting to the interface, the interface is reading the"

author: Daniel Simeonov
body: "Every person is a world. Most apps give you a profile, we give you a planet. What if we connected with people not through profile pictures, but through worlds? Your world is shaped by your answers, tended by your presence, and discovered by people who were curious enough to land. Connections here aren't made - they're found, the same way you find anything worth keeping."

author: Alpgiray Kelem

"Synaptic is a new interaction layer between perception and AI. We've only ever commanded our AI assistants. Synaptic proposes the opposite — feed it impressions. A universal capture gesture that works on screen, in browser, through camera. Drag to mark, hold to add context, release to send. Your AI accumulates what you notice. Over time, it thinks more like you.

 https://stall-sort-92482390.figma.site   Reimagining tap-to-like: instead of feeding an algorithm that serves others, training a mind that serves only you."

author: Senyan  Luo

"Zoom to Traverse: 
 https://bliss-pluck-73029083.figma.site/ 

 When you zoom all the way on a UI, what happens? Usually nothing. You hit a wall. You zoom until it is all pixels, then you retreat. That is what design felt like for a long time. A craft inside a boundary. A handoff at the end. The rest was out of your hands. But making in 2026 feels different. AI is making the boundaries between product layers, such as wireframes, UI, code, PRDs, and prompts easier to cross. Traverse zoom is my metaphor for that. Zoom stops being a dead end and becomes a way to travel across the layers of what y"

author: Varesh Mukhekar
body: "Built with  Prajyot The Void is a reading experience that reacts to how fast you scroll. Scroll slowly, and the article remains clean, editorial, and fully readable. Peaceful, even. Scroll faster, and the content begins to fracture. Text cracks, letters slip off their baseline, and layouts distort. The article is still there, but your speed starts tearing it apart. Scroll too fast for too long, and you reach The Void. A black hole pulls the words into it, while a single message appears: “You scrolled faster than you could read.” It’s about doomscrolling. About the way we consume information at"

author: Kehinde Oyekanmi
body: "Perspective Shift — Swipe to Understand Imagine a social media post: “Protesters clash with police downtown.” Normally, you see just one version, maybe a headline, maybe a video snippet, and you react without the full picture. Perspective Shift changes that. For a single post or news story, you can swipe to see it from multiple angles: A protester’s view — hope and fear A police officer’s view — duty and tension A journalist’s view — capturing the facts A bystander’s view — confusion and curiosity Long press to dive deeper into each perspective. “Break the Bias” shows all views side by side. B"

body: "You came back from holiday with 1,000 photos. You said you'd sort them ""later."" That was three months ago. And somewhere along the way, we stopped doing the other thing too. Sitting on the couch, flipping through a photo album. ""Oh, I remember that day"". We don't do that anymore. Our best photos live in a scroll we never finish. Momentous   This brings that feeling back. And made it easy to finally go back. Drop your photos in and swipe through them like a deck of cards — right to keep, left to skip. The ones you keep land in a scrapbook that makes you feel it all over again when you see it, o"

Recent thoughts cluster near the core, older ones drift out. Zoom in and a thought surfaces as a floating card. Low effort and seamlessly organized. It’s a multiplayer arena where up to 7 players enter a white room, choose a unique color, and throw water balloons at each other. Every splash stays, slowly transforming t"

author: Shivangi C
body:
"TL;DR A shared globe where conversation depends on presence, not availability.
 https://within-reach.figma.site   We’re used to talking to anyone, anywhere, instantly. Distance has become invisible. Within Reach explores what happens when connection depends on being near enough. You place yourself on the globe alongside others who are present at that same moment. You don’t message across the world by default. You open your reach. Sometimes that means staying local. Sometimes it means flying to where they are. Conversation happens only when you and someone else exist close enough in that shared"

author: Pragathi Ilango
body: "Come Join the Shared Table 🍽️  As an avid cook, I’ve always loved those tiny boxes of handwritten recipe cards — slightly stained, softly creased, passed down through generations. "WhyLayer — UX Integrity Stress Lab Most interfaces don’t fail suddenly.

They drift. WhyLayer is a live UX integrity stress lab built with Figma Make for the Makeathon. Instead of prototyping flows, it simulates system pressure. Adjust accessibility, cognitive load, ethics, and automation — and watch interface integrity shift in real time. In an AI-accelerated design era, output is instant, but integrity is fragile. author: Barsha Saikia
body:
"https://raft-mute-65054203.figma.site/  

A one of a kind Fabric Gifter where you can create your own fabric or use traditional textile blocks from around the world to create one of a kind fabric- render it on a great piece and send it to your friend!
This prototype transforms cultural block printing into a digital gifting experience, blending heritage, customization, and real-time garment previews into one seamless flow."

author: Ettouzany Abdelkader
body:
"Planning shouldn’t feel like a spreadsheet.
So I made WeekFit — a weekly planner that behaves like a constraint puzzle.

WeekFit turns weekly time-blocking into a constraint-based puzzle: you fit color-coded task blocks onto a 7-day grid instead of typing into a spreadsheet. Every round you get 3 blocks in a tray. Drag-and-drop (or click-to-place) them into your week, snapped into time slots. The goal is a perfect week, every slot filled, everything fits.

Try it here!
 https://relume-wad-90397426.figma.site"

author: Matea Raić
body:
"I built a website where the only interaction is waiting. No buttons. No scroll. No clicks. You just stay. The longer you stay, the more it reveals — poetry, meaning, beauty. And then it all disappears. Because nothing is permanent. Not even the things you earned by being patient. Your cursor becomes a flashlight into the past — briefly illuminating what was there. But you can never see the whole thing again. Still Here — built for the Figma Makeathon.  A screen that asks for nothing but your presence.

 https://unify-poppy-13936731.figma.site"

author: Sarah Comlan
body:
"We talk about “the state of the world,” but we rarely see how it actually feels. Emotional Field is a live, multiplayer instrument built in Figma Make that turns collective emotion into light and sound. Users place themselves on the map, share a 50-character reflection, and set its intensity. Each entry becomes a diffused glow and a tonal fragment in a generative global composition. When activated, the Earth scans across longitudes and plays the emotional state of the planet. It reimagines the social feed as harmony instead of noise.
Check it out:  https://fleck-cotton-11513516.figma.site"

author: Juweriya Afreen
body:
"Introducing: My Calendar × My Memory Book Reimagining the Calendar. 
We use calendars to plan our lives.
We use photos and journals to remember them. So I asked a simple question: Why are they separate? For this hackathon, I built a calendar with a twist, a space where productivity and presence coexist. You can schedule meetings, set priorities, and organize your week with clarity. But every day is also a memory page  add a photo, write how you felt, capture a moment, preserve something real. You plan your sprint at 10 AM. You capture the bunny photo that made you smile at 6 PM. Both belong to"

Fully structured. Component-driven. High-contrast. No glass UI. Neubrutalism meets SaaS logic.
Built in @figma Make. try it:  https://report-ankle-33639077.figma.site"

author: romil khunt
body:
"FiG Florist  Digital Bouquet Builder I built FiG Florist as a small experiment to explore how far I could push interaction design using Figma Make. The idea was simple instead of sending a static card, what if you could build and arrange your own bouquet digitally? You can:
• Pick flowers
• Shuffle arrangements organically
• Change greenery
• Write a personal note
• Share it instantly The fun part was building the arrangement logic, balancing randomness with structure so it feels hand-placed, not messy. A lot of tweaking went into spacing, overlap, and keeping that clean florist silhouette. Ho"

author: Rotash Shrestha
body:
"I've always wondered if I could recreate those JavaScript drum
machines I used to obsess over on CodePen, you know the ones.
Blinking step sequencers. Keyboard-mapped pads. That satisfying thwack on a synthesised kick. Years later, I finally did it. Not in code. In Figma Make. 🤯 WebDrums is my answer to that childhood what-if, a fully
interactive drum machine where every pad fires, every step toggles mid-beat, and a hand-drawn kit animates with every hit. The kid who used to fork CodePen experiments at 2am didn't expect to build this in a design tool. But here we are. 🥁🔥 Try it 👉  https://"

Features: Dynamic color themes & gradients that shift with mood changes Mood-specific particle animations (sparkles, waves, energy bursts) Smart task prioritization based on emotional state Interactive analytics with Recharts visualizations AI-powered contextual suggestions Smooth micro-interactions using Motion animations Tech: React, TypeScript, Motion, Recharts, Tailwind CSS v4 Purpose: Showcase interactive prototyping with emotion-driven UX design"

author: Angela Andreea Paralescu
body: "Me And My, Fall for Who They Are, Not What They Look Like. What if you could feel butterflies for someone's mind before ever seeing their face? MeAndMy is the anonymous connection app for people who crave something deeper. No real names. No profile photos. No small talk. Just raw, honest conversations between two souls matched by personality, emotional needs, and compatibility. Here's what makes it different: you can't share photos for 3 months. That's not a limitation, it's the magic. By the time you finally see each other, you'll already know their humor, their fears, their 2 AM thoughts. Th"

author: Danny Nah
body:
"There was a time when learning about the world meant opening a heavy atlas in the quiet corner of a library. I grew up clicking through Microsoft Encarta Kids, exploring different countries and their cultures. Now, for the Figma Makeathon, I’m reimagining that feeling. Try it:  https://hurry-flock-38286264.figma.site/   I built an interactive digital atlas of Africa but instead of flipping pages, you navigate the continent in a jeep, driving across landscapes, discovering landmarks, and unlocking stories along the way. Not just learning about Africa.
Exploring it."

author: Yuri Hwang
body: "There is no spatially aware text-generation interaction. We measure or check character counts, or go back and forth to get the right amount of text. With this new drag interaction, the LLM generates, edits exactly the amount of text you want."

The universe is vast, but in Cosmology Arena, only the sharpest pilots rise to the"

Reactive audio can combine:  - Binaural tones  - Dynamic notes with adjustment pad - Microphone input (distorted or not) You can use it to either create weird hypnotic sounds, or just connect your microphone and use it with music."

author: Emilie Joseph
body: "I built Planetology to show how learning about space can be playful and immersive. Traditional educational content is often static and passive — my goal was to design an interface that lets people explore planets, not just read about them. Planetology is a 3D, gesture-controlled planet explorer built entirely in Figma Make. Through spatial and motion-based interactions, it transforms planetary science into an engaging, hands-on experience — turning curiosity into something you can physically explore! Try it:  Planetology  🪐 👥  LinkedIn Post 🌐  Figma Community Published File"

author: Jia Singh
body:
"MyAnalogJournal ❤️
For #FigmaMakeathon, I built a tactile digital journal inspired by Hobonichi planners, reimagined for screens. Unlock a leather journal. Flip real pages. Slow down. Each entry updates a monthly calendar with emotion markers and a live mood trend line. Not replacing pen & paper, just making journaling private and expressive in a digital-first world. Try it here:  Published Figma Community Linkedin Post"

author: Wojciech Ziółkowski
body:
"https://drop-delete-53026660.figma.site  

AI removed the need to explore — one question, one answer, loop closed. Apollo brings the wandering back. Ask a question and instead of a final answer — you get a constellation of connected ideas you can navigate freely. Click any thread, follow any direction, wander as far as you want. There is no final answer. The exploration never ends."

author: Eurey Noguchi
body:
"We scroll faster.
We read slower. But documents don’t know the difference. Adaptive Reading rethinks what a document is. Instead of forcing one static format, it responds to attention. As you skim, it compresses cognitively — surfacing inline summaries.
As you slow down, it restores depth — seamlessly revealing the original text. Interfaces shouldn’t be static. What if they adapted to how you think? #FigmaMakeathon Figma Site:  https://hungry-tiny-74002287.figma.site/   Figma Community:  https://www.figma.com/community/file/1610512188521118102   X:  https://x.com/eureyuri/status/20286811038930"

author: bagisha kumari
body:
"Scroll_Shift : Earn your scroll.
We don’t fail at habits because we lack discipline.
We fail because digital noise pays instantly. Social media platforms are engineered for variable rewards, endless feeds, and effortless dopamine. Habit apps, on the other hand, ask for effort first and reward later. ScrollShift flips that loop.

Take a feel of it here :  https://www.figma.com/make/33BCRtRyiUshANotKDLmSl/Build-app-from-file?t=OQjkqHjf4yurkzF6-1   Complete small habit milestones and turn progress into scroll credits. Reach the unlock threshold, and your social app (eg., Instagram) opens guilt-fr"

author: Nicole Lucero-Huerta
body: "For #FigmaMakeathon we reimagined the scroll using #FigmaMake. The deeper you go, the heavier it gets. Cards frost over. Hold the heart watch it grow, launches up, and falls back down like rain. Because social media started as a way to connect. Somewhere along the way, the scroll took over. The gestures are muscle memory now. The connection is still there. We just stopped feeling it. Vibefeed is our attempt to give it weight again. Try it:  https://global-run-58533171.figma.site"

You still experience the immersive feeling of being inside a booth, layering colorful Y2K stickers and customizing frames just like before. author: Menna Mousa
body:
"We’re constantly collecting visual inspiration. Scrapboarder helps you slow down and capture it intentionally. Grab snippets from the web, annotate what caught your eye, connect patterns, and watch your taste take shape over time, think obsidian - made visual.

Give it a try and let me know what you think!
 https://party-line-50590451.figma.site/"

author: Stacy Belding
body:
"Last week I was in the middle of planning a Backstreet Boys Vegas weekend with 10 of my girlfriends — all navigating spouses, jobs, kids, and competing schedules. What should have been fun quickly turned into chaos inside a group text full of side quests, GIFs, and zero clear answers.
I knew immediately what I wanted to build for this Makeathon.
Kept — helping friends be better friends.
Kept helps you organize your people into groups, plan gatherings, lock in that near-impossible-to-find event date, remember birthdays, suggest meaningful gifts, and collect the little details that make someone "

author: Harsh Gogri
body:
"You wake up in a room you can’t see. The walls are invisible.
The enemies aren’t. The only way to reveal the world around you is to speak. Every word sends a pulse.
Every pulse exposes the maze.
And exposes you. At first, it feels like you’re escaping.
Then the system starts responding faster. Adapting. Learning. By the time you reach the final chamber, something feels off. What if Subject 07 was never escaping?
What if you were training the chamber all along? Play it here:  https://silence-chamber.figma.site/"

author: Nanda Maulana
body:
"So yesterday I tried some of the other Makeathon apps, and their projects were awesome.  It made me realize that my game still has some friction for people who want to try it out.  So in less than an hour, I added a new mode called City World. In this mode, people can join and play anytime or just explore. The quiz resets every hour, and there’s no need for a lobby—similar to how  works. I'll be there if you want Znap some knowledges. 1/8 Just fill your name and join world.

visit  znap.figma.site"

author: Shreha B
body: I really enjoyed being part of FigmaMakeathon. It was so inspiring to see what everyone created ✨

author: Okezie Ferdinand
body:
"SOVEREIGN -Gesture-Controlled AR Health Security What if your hands were the interface?
What if your body were your password? SOVEREIGN is an AR health experience
built entirely in the browser, no app,
no installation. Just your camera and
your hands. ✋ Show your palm → health aura activates
☝️ Point and hold → deep biometric scan
✌️ 2 fingers → full health dashboard
🤟 3 fingers → secure call with your doctor
✊ Fist → session ends, data leaves with you No tapping. No typing. Pure gesture control. Built with Figma Make.
Best experienced on a desktop with a camera. 🔗  https://remote-camel-7692"

author: Alexis Balinoff
body:
"I built an interactive identity mapper with  @Figma  Make. 
It's called: Map Your Universe. Try it here:  https://modal-safari-44756507.figma.site/ The idea is simple: Who are you really? Beyond your job, where you're from? What makes you, you? The music you couldn't live without, the food you can't get enough of? The artists you'd love to see live or could listen for hours? You start with your name, then you click anywhere to start liking things. You name them. You can resize them if they mean a lot to you. You can merge things you like and they become clusters. If you merge clusters, they be"

body:
"Planetary Explorer

Built a 3D Planetary Explorer in Figma Make — a dark-themed galaxy interface where users can navigate the solar system and zoom into individual planets. 

The experience allows system rotation, planet selection, and interactive 3D exploration with smooth transitions and depth-based navigation between overview and detail modes.

Try it here

author: Ian
body:
"Here is my submission for the Figma Makeathon! I've created a ASCII design tool. 

My goal was to create unique ways to select tools. The Quick Ribbon [x] allows you to quickly swap between tools with your A & D keys. The toolbox is a quick way to swap between characters.   

I also incorporated a personal feature, CRT support. A quick way to swap to a 4:3 ratio to easily fit the application on any CRT. For those who want that extra level of digital grunge. 

Start designing!  https://flower-azul-67033652.figma.site/  

 Community Profile"

author: Maxence Atzori
body:
"Sing Them Home 🧜‍♀️  What if you could use your voice to guide marine creatures through the depths and protect them from fishing nets? 

The game is simple Sing or Hum to make them rise, silence to let them dive.  Play:  https://gecko-roast-28422327.figma.site/   Inspired by the atmospheric art of Hollow Knight, I built this game to blend my passion for marine conservation with technology.  I wanted to create a unique experience that is as educational as it is enchanting.  I hope you’ll dive straight in and help lead them home! 🌊💙"

author: Jhan Piere Torres A.
body:
"After a 10 years of designing interfaces, I'm exhausted by standard e-commerce. We are still forcing users to doomscroll through rigid lists, clicking uninspired ""Add to Cart"" buttons. It kills product discovery and limits Growth Design.  So, I built something completely different: Cluster. 🔥  It’s an infinite spatial canvas that ditches the traditional product card. Instead of scrolling, users curate.

We shouldn't just design pages anymore; we should design spaces. Check out the interactive prototype and let me know what you think of the vibe: Interactive Prototype:  https://cluster-ecommer"

author: Jasmine Gordon
body: "My last submission is voice notes reimagined for spoken journals and daily reflections, taking inspiration from dictaphones and cassette players for both the interactions and UI design. The goal of this concept was to explore how I could emulate the aesthetics and functionality of old school technology and bring it into the digital world, while staying true to its original purpose. When the user presses record, the film, represented as wheels, starts to spin. The user can rewind by pressing and dragging the left wheel in an anticlockwise circular motion. To fast-forward, they can do the same w"

author: Muhammad Yousaf
body:
"I decided to join the  Contra  x  Figma  challenge!

What if design feedback was instant, with no waiting and no “Thoughts?” left hanging?

Pixlume lets you upload your work, get structured insights, and refine immediately. Less waiting. More refining. Just clarity when you need it.

It’s not just feedback — it’s an experience.

So I built interactions around it. Pixlume interactions include swipe-based navigation, click-and-hold analysis triggers, dynamic score animations, live feedback transitions, and responsive UI states that react in real time.

It doesn’t just respond.
It feels like it’s"

author: Abdullahi Adetunji
body: What are you carrying? I built an experience that asks the questions nobody else does and shows you that you're not carrying them alone. 5 questions. No wrong answers. Every answer joins a global living canvas of what humanity is feeling right now. Anonymously. Beautifully. Honestly. Try it →  https://dwarf-deer-54826685.figma.site Built with Figma Make ✦

author: Anjali kumari
body:
"Rebuild — Do You Really Know Your Tools? Post Copy: Design isn’t just creativity.

It’s system literacy. Rebuild transforms familiar creative software into a live competitive arena. Two players. One interface. 60 seconds. The UI explodes. You rebuild it. Built in Figma Make with real-time multiplayer, physics-based interaction, and atomic scoring. Design as performance."

author: Anastasia Moccia
body:
"Here's my concept!
Wildlife Hub connects citizens and volunteers to protect urban wildlife using AI Core Features: AI Scanner: Instantly identify species and get expert care advice. Volunteer Alerts: One-tap emergency updates to notify nearby rescuers. Neighbor of the Day: Daily educational facts to foster urban coexistence.  https://www.figma.com/proto/uu7uJUaBywQZ6QGXGyhICq/Wildlife-Hub?page-id=0%3A1&node-id=15-1170&viewport=599%2C-1134%2C0.12&t=3WGJYQorbhXdnwsv-1&scaling=scale-down&content-scaling=fixed&starting-point-node-id=15%3A1170"

author: Haseena Khan
body: "Just entered the #FigmaMakeathon! 🏠🔐 Settlr — an interactive escrow experience designed to protect newcomers from rental scams. Move through a verified listing, perform a live ""outside the house"" match check, and release funds only when you're safe. I even added a feature to automatically notify parents back home once you've settled in. 🙏 Built with procedural sound, 3D house models, and smooth transitions in Figma Make. Site:  https://yew-sling-52944137.figma.site   @Contra HQ #FigmaMake #Makeathon #WebDesign #PropTech"

author: Jeroel Padilla
body:
"Collective Pulse reimagines the ""Reaction"" buttons with a new interaction primitive: a continuous, anonymous 2D gesture where you locate yourself in emotional space. One placement creates a living, real-time map of how humanity feels — no content, no identity, just presence. Features:
- New emotional gesture:  self-location on a sea of emotions
- Anonymous by design:  no accounts, no identity, no attribution
- 24-hour mood decay: every pulse fades like weather, not a permanent record
- Draggable live update: your pulse becomes a handle; mood is a position, not a selection
- Nearest-first commu"

author: Prakash Anand Kolkundi
body: "🚀 What if interfaces disappeared — and technology just understood you? I built Futuristic Interaction 2050 for the Contra | Figma Makethon 2026 — a 5-scene cinematic prototype that reimagines how humans will interact with technology 25 years from now.No screens. No taps. No clicks. Just intent. This is a fully interactive, — a cinematic journey through a day in 2050 where biometric sensing, spatial computing, emotion-aware environments, and zero-navigation AI replace every interface we know today. You live it — walking through smart doors, watching rooms adapt to your stress levels, and colla"

author: sakshi ranglani
body:
"The Last Analog
The more you try to perfect it, the faster it dies. Draw in a classic MS Paint canvas.
Your first imperfect stroke is honoured.
Try to fix it — watch everything decay. Lines bleed. Pixels fray. The page consumes itself beautifully. In a world obsessed with optimization, this refuses to help. If you can resist...
a unique wabi-sabi relic waits. Try it : https://liter-resize-02262472.figma.site/"

author: Fatik Owais
body:
"I let my baby guide me on this one. I observed how she is using the app, told Figma Make about it. And boom I had the next iteration till my daughter actually started learning Alphabets. She spoke her first Alphabet 'D' and I am glad I got to record while doing my Baby Centered Design research :D 

I think this is the future of software is right here, custom built and iterated for your own needs rather than one size fit all. Thank you Figma for making it accessible, and thanks to Contra for the push to try new things :)

Try:  https://award-slip-15609525.figma.site/  
Remix:  https://www.figma"

author: Fatik Owais
body:
"Why lists with dense information items can't have some fun. How about we squeeze in more items in a single fold where all the primary information is visible at a glance, and the secondary info reveals itself as you scroll up. I am calling this ""Spatial Compression Progressive disclosure thing."" There is some complicated maths going behind which Claude Opus 4.6 took care gracefully.

author: Aniket Raut
body:
"For the Figma × Contra Makeathon, I built FunBooth - an interactive, neo-brutalist photo booth where you can click button and also you use your hands. Pinch to pick stickers. Drag to place them. Flash a ✌️ to capture the shot. No UI clutter. Just bold visuals, playful motion, and natural gestures turning interaction into expression. Built with love for interaction, experimentation, and a little bit of chaos. 🎥 Watch the demo.
🧪 Try it yourself here: [ https://funbooth.figma.site  ]"

""drift well"" — a prototype where content and people orbit you based on proximity, not algorithms. 

Built in Figma Make

author: Shreya Bane
body:
"justbreathe  
A breath-controlled interaction built using Figma Make. Blow into your mic — an autumn leaf moves. The harder you exhale, the further it drifts. The longer you hold, the more the silence speaks. After 30 seconds, it reads your breathing pattern and tells you what it heard — your emotional state, a score out of ten, one honest line about you right now. Then it becomes an instrument. Your breath plays pan flute notes across a pentatonic scale. Short breath hits the high pipe. Long exhale drops to the lowest. Add a drum loop. Add a hi-hat. Create remix that sounds cool :)  Just air "

author: Victor Abayomi
body:
"CANVAS — Multiplayer for the Entire Internet
Figma gave designers a canvas. CANVAS gives everyone the internet. Every collaboration tool stops at the edge of the app. CANVAS changes that. Click one button on any webpage — draw on it, annotate it, move elements, share your layer. The next person adds theirs. Two humans. One webpage. Infinite layers.
Five working tools. Real shared annotations. Shareable links. Recipient view with layer toggles. Built entirely in Figma Make. This reimagines multiplayer collaboration — one of Figma's iconic interactions — and puts it on the entire internet for th"

author: NH__ Nayan
body: "Neighborhood Resource Sharing Platform Built a community app for neighbors to share tools, games, and household items. Features a warm neumorphic UI with earthy tones, conversational onboarding, and an interactive map for discovering nearby resources. Stack: React • TypeScript • Leaflet • Tailwind CSS Focus: Trust, accessibility, human connection Making neighborhoods more connected, one shared power drill at a time. https://www.figma.com/community/file/1609841421860931738/neighborhood-resou"

author: ASHWATH A - AA design
body:
"If it has ever been done by a human, it is humanly possible. Anything Humanly Possible explores how human achievement, AI mentorship, and immersive learning come together to turn belief into action. History shows what’s possible.

AI helps you move toward it.

Real projects turn learning into proof. Built in Figma Make.

Designed to feel alive.

Made for builders. Explore:
 https://anythinghumanlypossible.figma.site/ #FigmaMakeathon #AnythingHumanlyPossible #HumanPotential #AI #Design #Learning"

author: Jonathan Wijaya
body: "We judge what we don’t understand. But could you survive a day in their shoes? I built POV: SoulSwap, an immersive empathy simulator that forces you to inhabit the invisible struggles of others. Built with Figma Make, this project uses a ""No-Win Scenario"" logic and a unique OLED reflection feature to turn your screen into a mirror for your own soul. Trade your privilege for their reality, just for a moment. Try the Empathy Machine:  https://lunch-wired-60509254.figma.site #FigmaMakeathon"

author: Farjana Khatun
body: "Vivid Vault: AI Cinematic Thumbnail Suite I built Vivid Vault to empower mobile creators to generate high-end, 3D cinematic thumbnails instantly. This project was designed 100% on a mobile device using Figma Make to push the boundaries of on-the-go creative workflows. Key Interactions: Voice-to-Design Engine: Hands-free thumbnail generation via voice prompts. Liquid Glass Style Gallery: A high-fidelity UI that makes selecting styles intuitive and tactile. Success Celebration: Animated feedback loops to make the creative process fun and rewarding. https://relax-fresh-09690095.figma.site/"

author: Don Precious
body:
"I reimagined the colour picker for the #FigmaMakeathon.
The colour picker hasn't really changed in 20 years. It's usually the same spectrum, the same slider, and the same hex code. But that's not how many people actually experience colour. Colour can come from everyday things — a photo, a place, a fabric, or even a moment. EXTRACT lets you drop real images onto a canvas, pull the colours that catch your eye, and watch a full brand identity build itself from your choices. Logo, typography, persona, tone of voice, business cards — all from what you noticed.

Try it here:  https://text-sepia-3491"

author: Arijal Ibnu Jati
body:
"The future of diagnostics is interactive. 🧬🧪 My entry for the Figma Makeathon: A Digital Micro-Petri Dish. I used Figma Make to create a ""magnetic lens"" interaction. Drag the droplets into the central zone to trigger real-time medical analysis. It’s all about making complex data feel tactile and intuitive. Experience the lab here:
 https://report-oats-20748393.figma.site/"

author: Ria S
body: "What if I digitized the collection and decoration of a photocard using Figma Make? These are collectible cards featuring a photo of an artist, available as inclusions found in albums & other merch. As someone who's been listening to K-pop for years, it's a huge part of fan culture. But, not everyone is able to afford or get their hands on merch that would help them build collections, especially since the card included is often random.  Enter ""Photocard Customizer""— users can upload images of scans (or their own photos!) that they have saved into a canvas and play around with different tools to"

author: Peter Perez
body:
"MADE — Six Interfaces, Thirty Years, One Story Every interface is a document of its time. Not just how things looked, how people thought, what they believed was possible, what they were afraid of and excited about. 1995 → 2002 → 2008 → 2012 → 2025 → 2026. Each era is built to feel like it actually lived there. Some things are obvious. Some things you have to find. There are easter eggs hiding in places you wouldn’t expect. Explore it.

 • Here:  MADE  

Also available on: • Figma Community —  Figma Community

 • X thread —  X"

author: Soham Shirke
body:
"Built a mini interactive OS time machine: the Evolution of OS

From 1984’s black-and-white Macintosh System 1.0 (entire OS on one floppy 🤯) → Windows 95’s Start Me Up era → XP’s iconic Bliss hill (most viewed photo in history) → macOS Big Sur’s vibrant redesign

If you're into UI history, nostalgia, or just want to feel old-school clicking again:  https://dress-lend-08638783.figma.site  

Would love feedback: which OS hits the hardest for you? 👀"

author: Angela Andreea Paralescu
body: "Have Fun everyone!!! 🙌 😍   Imagine  25,000 glowing particles morphing into a DNA helix, a butterfly, a beating heart, all floating in bloom lighting and YOU controlling all of it with nothing but your bare hands. Particle Universe turns your webcam into a musical instrument and a particle sculptor. Move your palm to sweep particles across the screen. Spread both hands to explode a galaxy. Pinch your fingers to drop a deep, chest-rumbling bass note. Raise your hand to swell a flowing pentatonic melody that you compose just by moving left and right. Clap to shatter one shape and birth another."

author: Sneha Saha
body:
"This is a LEGO-inspired game concept. The idea came from a simple frustration: I’ve always wanted the time and space to build something with bricks, but I often lose pieces or don’t get enough uninterrupted time to complete a build. So instead of waiting for the perfect moment, I decided to create Brickify, a virtual building experience inspired by the creativity of the LEGO Group, where anyone can design and build without worrying about missing blocks or limited time.

p.s : It's not perfect but I'll work on it to make it real production level application🔥 

 https://record-thank-47519750.fi"

author: Copees UX Design Firm
body: This offers a completely new perspective.  Don’t miss it.

author: Osagie Eghe-Abe
body:
"ATTENTION HAS WEIGHT In the attention economy, engagement appears effortless.

But nothing online is truly free. Attention Has Weight reimagines the social feed by visualizing attention as a finite, physical resource. Instead of tapping a “like” button, users must press and hold to invest attention.

Energy transfers visibly from the user to the content. Scrolling drains attention.

Rapid consumption increases friction.

When attention reaches zero, the interface shuts down. This project explores: The cost of infinite scroll The psychology of engagement Friction as a design tool Interfaces tha"

author: Kofi Wood
body:
"What if we stopped designing the internet only for
scrolling?

My answer to that is ""Trace Muse"", my submission for the 2026
#figmamakeathon. It is my attempt to reimagine what a break on the internet
could feel like. Instead of doom scrolling & passive consumption, Trace
Muse invites you to pause, reflect, trace, and learn. You enter, share how you are feeling, choose a color, pick a
topic, and then begin revealing knowledge by tracing through soft biomorphic
shapes.
So instead of scrolling to read, you trace to reveal. Trace Muse is designed to help people relax, unwind, and
learn in short b"

author: Kelechi Chijioke
body:
"The comment box needs a rethink.

Same blank rectangle. Same typed replies. So I built MARK for the #FigmaMakeathon.

A comment thread where every response is a canvas. Pick a background color, write, draw, drop stickers or images, post!

No two comments look the same. Try it out:  https://fawn-sleep-69369062.figma.site/"

author: Chiara Cerretti
body:
"https://celestia.figma.site  

Celestia — Songlines of the Sky ✨ What if you could explore the night sky using only eye-tracking? Celestia is a Figma Make prototype that turns constellations into sound and haptic patterns, with text and transcripts always available. Its core, the Haptic Sky Compass, translates direction and “presence” into gentle cues — chimes, rhythms, pulses — and a Dream Library lets you save thoughts as personal constellations. You don’t need to see the stars to feel them close."

author: Hanna Nyzhnia
body: "An oddly specific scenario - camera lenses and landscape photography. To make everything in your photo entirely in focus, I made visualized hyperfocal distance calculator. The mobile version made to look like you're playing with the lens. And you can observe real time simulation in digital viewfinder!"

author: Jasur Osimi
body: "Adaptive Interface [Concept] The interface quietly adjusted itself to where you are: bright office, dimmed room, late night. A small prototype exploring context-aware UI."

author: Bruno Del Monde
body:
"Infinite scroll was just the beginning. But like gaming's evolution to open worlds, a linear path can't hold human curiosity forever. For the #FigmaMakeathon, I developed the 2D Feed. It shifts users from passive observers to main actors, driving happiness and engagement. Users gain a new sense of control and discovery, seamlessly integrated into a familiar interface. Try it here:  https://lnkd.in/dSiym3XR 🛠️ Prototyped in Figma Make Figma Community:  https://lnkd.in/d-nmci2v
 LinkedIn:  https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7434504511198515200/"

author: ishita gupta
body:
"What if your entire website could redesign itself from a single image??

I built🎨  ChromaShift  ✨using Make, a palette engine that lets users upload any image and instantly generate a complete 5-color theme system, applied across the interface in real time. It demonstrates how design systems can become dynamic.

- Upload an image 📸
- Let the system extract a 5-color theme 🎨
- Click Apply Palette to update the interface instantly ⚡
- Copy the generated CSS for your own website 💻

The next step is transforming this into a deployable tool for designers and developers.🎯

Do check it out😃"

author: Farwa MK
body:
"Design the next great interaction that shapes culture. My concept: Time Capsule Messaging A mobile experience that allows users to write messages, seal them, and unlock them in the future.In a world obsessed with instant replies, what if we designed for patience instead? Time Capsule Messaging lets you write a message today and unlock it in the future.
 https://www.figma.com/make/7BltgypWemI42zAIZym55s/Time-Capsule-Messaging-Prototype?
 No edits. No early access. Just anticipation. This project explores how delayed interaction can create deeper emotional impact and reshape digital communicatio"

author: FAITH AKIROR
body: "LinguaLink is a real-time translation app that enables seamless text and voice communication across different languages. It automatically detects and translates conversations, eliminating the need for external tools. Designed with accessibility and simplicity in mind using Figma Make, the app offers an interactive prototype with chat, voice, and history features. LinguaLink connects people globally by making communication easy, natural, and inclusive. Here is the live site  https://lingualink.figma.site/   https://www.linkedin.com/posts/faith-akiror-44a9a6139_figmamake-uiuxdesign-productdesign"

author: Ina Yosun Chang
body:
"(First Draft - 15 minutes left, waiting for fixes to gen, submitting this just in cases)

Swipe right to add dimension. Swipe left to flatten. Prismatica reimagines the iconic left/right swipe as dimensional density—a core HCI for generative AI media. Input an image and shine it through an Image Prism: it refracts into controllable visual components, then recomposes through reversible AI edit nodes. I joined the #FigmaMakeathon to test whether we can design a new interaction language for generative AI 3D using Figma Make. Grateful for the unlimited generations, I kept pushing and soon it becam"

author: Prue Steadson
body: "Every hour, somewhere in the world, someone pauses for tea. STEEP is a real-time interactive map that lets you join them. Explore tea rituals as they happen across cultures, from gongfu to çay to shai, and listen to local radio while they steep. A small reminder to slow down, make a cup, and share a moment with someone on the other side of the world. https://thin-santa-86449499.figma.site/"

author: Harsitha A
body: This one is inspired by my most recent hobby. A digital embroidery app that lets you create your own thread art right in your browser. Draw inside a wooden hoop using a variety of classic stitch styles from running stitch to chain stitch with a vibrant palette of thread colors. Cross device friendly and you can export your design once you are done! Link:  https://embroideredly-yours.figma.site

author: Bruno Del Monde
body: "Great UX improvements aren't always flashy; they are the practical ones that blend seamlessly into the interface, making every use a bit more rewarding. Managing an overwhelming amount of chats causes cognitive overload and visual fatigue. To fix this, I created Glance and Seek: a single gesture for distraction-free navigation. Just hold a conversation to see a preview, then slide your finger to scrub through dozens of chats instantly. This large, movement-free window saves time, reduces visual distress, and makes finding exactly what you need effortless. Try it here:  https://lnkd.in/d7eTRNYC"

author: Milind Bhalala
body:
"What if apps didn’t need fixed menus? I built OmniLayer — a voice-driven AI overlay that generates working buttons and workflows inside any app. Say:
“Generate a button to reply to all unread chats apologizing for delay.” It creates a persistent Smart Action, adapts the message based on delay duration, and reflects real UI changes after execution. Intent → Generated Action → UI Transformation. Exploring what happens when interfaces become dynamic instead of predefined.

Figma make link:  https://matter-dove-76180248.figma.site"

author: Leoni Christakou
body: "The Reality Check  AI generates ""perfect"" code in a vacuum, but your project exists in the real world. When you try to force that AI logic into your existing work, things usually break. Instead of reading a boring list of errors, Code Cracker lets you slam the new draft against your project to see where the structural integrity fails.  The Fix  Why hunt for invisible bugs when you can see the debris? By turning an abstract review into a physical audit, you instantly locate where the AI lost its way. Just snap the broken fragments back into the original project to heal the logic. It turns a fru"

author: Ahmad Fawaid
body:
"I’ve been exploring a new interaction pattern inside Figmamake called Stash. 

Instead of the traditional copy–paste flow, Stash acts as a temporary visual layer that collects what you copy (files, folders, or text) and lets you choose exactly what to paste next. 

It lives at the bottom of the screen, appears only when needed, and keeps up to five recent items. You can trigger it with a shortcut, select what you want, and paste intentionally, not blindly. 

It’s a small shift, but it changes how copy-paste interaction feels.

Preview here:  https://use-stash.figma.site"
