author: Muhammad Ichwan
body:
"ACSI:  https://sans-thread-39316348.figma.site   ACSI is a social media feed simulation that detects compulsive scrolling and progressively intervenes to restore cognitive control. Inspired by dopamine-driven anticipation loops and the Variable Ratio Reinforcement Schedule by B. F. Skinner. 1–11s: feed normal.

12–23s: saturation drops, animations slow 30–40%.

24–35s: one post locked for 2s.

36–48s: screen pauses. Note: Timings are simplified; production would calculate them dynamically. Social post:  LinkedIn ,  Instagram"

Not just UI. Explore it here: https://aurashift.figma.site/ #FigmaMakeathon #FigmaMake #InteractionDesign #CreativeCoding #UIUX

author: Gabriel Stevens
body:
"Hmmmmm - take a moment before you fire off that rage post.

Ever sent a message in anger that you immediately regretted? hmmmmm is designed to prevent that. Using real-time audio analysis to detect your humming frequency and providing visual feedback to help you cool down. Once you complete the exercise, you've had a moment to calm down and can proceed more mindfully.

There is real science behind it. Humming is more than just making noise—it's a powerful physiological tool that: •Activates the vagus nerve
•Slows your breathing
•Creates a mindful pause
•Releases nitric oxide

hmmmmm.figma.site"

author: Mohammad Yahia
body: "WearWise  is an experiment in bringing texture to life through audio. Texture isn’t only visual—it has a sound, a rhythm, and a “presence.” So the core interaction is simple: you rub a fabric swatch (mouse hold + move / touch + drag) and the audio responds to your motion, shifting from soft to energetic so you can sense the difference between quiet, crisp, and swishy materials. The goal is to help your brain connect sound → texture → imagined feel, even through a screen. At its heart, this is an interaction design idea: using one sense (hearing) to support another that’s missing (touch), creat"

Plan Mode redefines AI thinking as: Intent → Constraints → Resources → Reasoning → Execution 1. Plan Builder (Node-Based Logic) In Plan Builder, you wire your system like n8n. You drag nodes onto the canvas and connect them. When the structure is ready, you activate the plan. 2 Constraint Knowledge Graph Time,Budget,Technical ,Complexity,Risk Level 3.Final  Plan Activation When you hit Activate Plan, the interf"

author: Rashid Iqbal
body:
"OpenSwiz is a concept for a visual AI workflow builder. Connect nodes on a canvas to design automation pipelines with LLM calls, API requests, code blocks, payments, and more. https://ankle-void-05433614.figma.site  Still a prototype. 

The canvas is fully explorable, but you'll need to bring your own LLM API keys for anything to actually run. No keys, no magic (yet). 25 node types across 8 categories: AI agents, integrations (Slack, Gmail), logic, code, media, memory, and payments. Plug in OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI, or a custom endpoint.

The long-term vision is a fully open-source alter"

author: Neha Tetarwal
body: "AgriSmart integrates AI crop diagnostics, weather forecasting, and market intelligence into a single digital platform, enabling farmers to make smarter and data-driven agricultural decisions.  #agritech #digitalagriculture #smartfarming #aiinagriculture #agricultureinnovation #farmtech #sustainablefarming #agriinnovation #uxdesign #figmamakeathon #productdesign #startupidea"

author: Furqan Kissana
body:
"I built a cinematic, interaction-first wedding invitation where the mouse replaces buttons, scroll, and clicks. Every screen is unlocked through intentional cursor actions — hold, drag, scratch, hover-dwell, proximity, right-click charge, freehand drawing, and speed-based movement — turning a static invite into a story-driven experience. Built with React, TypeScript, Tailwind, and Motion, this project explores how mouse interaction alone can create emotion, pacing, and narrative on the web.

Check the experience:  https://volume-coral-44812255.figma.site"

author: Osarenkhoe Iyamu-Noma
body:
"The biggest painpoint with TV remotes isn't missing buttons, it's FRICTION. Finding the remote, navigating endless menus, typing with a D-pad.

Here's where MOTE comes in:

1. It gives you complete control, using Natural Language. Just type or speak, and AI does the thinking & handles the navigation.

2. Show ""just the essential"" buttons - minimal UI, no clutter.

Link to the site 👉 -  https://moteapp.figma.site/  

For this demo, I already created sample TVs to choose from. Have fun 😊  ."

author: Edward Lucky Obi
body: "Introducing Shorts Pulse — a new way to interact with YouTube Shorts. What if Save and Share didn’t live inside a menu? Shorts Pulse is a new interactive gesture system that reimagines how billions of users worldwide save and share on platforms like YouTube Shorts — replacing taps, menu hunting, and long-presses, and redefining short-form video interaction. In this demo, I show how it works: double tap, then slide up to share or down to save. Built with Figma Make for the #FigmaMakeathon. • Double tap → Like • Double tap + slide up → Share • Double tap + slide down → Save Prototype: https://gl"

I enjoyed the process a lot and learned a lot while building this. Let me know your thoughts 🙂 #FigmaMake #Makeathon"

author: Akhil Saxena

When you physically flip, choose, and race against fate — the words stay. Built with Figma Make for the "

author: Takhirzhan Yussupov
body: "Imagine an app that tracks your eyes to tell you exactly when it's time to rest. And yes, it’s entirely possible with Figma Make. 👁️✨ We stare at screens until our eyes burn. To fix this, I built EyeSync for the #FigmaMakeathon — a digital wellbeing dashboard that heals your focus instead of stealing it. How it works: 👀 Smart Tracking: It simulates eye-tracking to monitor your blink rate and screen fatigue. 🍅 Focus Assistant: A custom Pomodoro timer interrupts your work before you get too tired. 🧘 Guided Recovery: It guides you through audio-visual eye and neck exercises to release tension"

author: Chidiebere Peter
body: "Decide Together - Makeathon Submission The Problem: ""Where should we eat?"" - the eternal group debate. The Solution: Decide Together reimagines the iconic Tinder swipe as a real-time multiplayer experience. Everyone swipes simultaneously on restaurants, sees live reactions (""Alex liked this too!""), and gets instant match celebrations when the group agrees. Key Innovation: Transforms a single-player gesture into collaborative decision-making with velocity-based swipe physics, 3-card stack effects, and joyful animations. Demo Flow: Full multiplayer simulation with mock participants that auto-joi"

author: Ayaneshu
body: "#2 While building flows in   Make & referencing multiple frames in a single prompt, I’m often unsure if I’m pointing to the right frame. Sometimes AI misunderstands & generates something else, & in the best case I end up breaking the task into multiple smaller prompts to get the right output. Overall, it increases the number of prompts needed & reduces confidence while prompting. What if I could directly mention or attach a specific frame, image, or file exactly at the point in the prompt where I’m referring to it? This removes ambiguity & make the interaction feel more precise, while also giv"

author: Eric Sangcap
body:
"What if your computer remembered your life the way you do? Introducing MemoryOS — a new interface concept where your digital life is organized by moments instead of apps. Zoom through time and explore your life like a constellation of memories. Year → Month → Day → Moment. Click a moment and everything connected to it unfolds: • files

• conversations

• ideas

• tasks

• collaborators No folders.

No apps.

Just a living map of your digital life. Built as an experimental prototype for the #FigmaMakeathon using Figma Make. Curious what computing looks like after apps. 👉  https://memory-os.fig"

 Using latitude, longitude, date and time, the app determines the current celestial energy (mood, intensity, rhythm). The energy profile is translated into musical parameters (genre, tempo, emotional tone). These parameters are used to fetch Spotify songs. The result is a playlist that feels cos"

author: Gustavo Gadea
body:
"We do not usually lose people on purpose.
Life gets busy and, without noticing, we slowly drift away from loved ones. To mitigate this I've created ""Orbits"": a visual system that maps your relationships as a ""solar system"". The people closest to you stay near your center, and those you have not connected with in a while slowly move farther away. The goal is simple, help you notice the distance and gently trigger a change in behavior: Reach out Try it here:  https://orbitsapp.figma.site/
 This is a demo, but In a fully built version, ""Orbits"" could recognize when you communicate with the people"

author: Neel Walse
body:
"I realized I don’t trust the “show password” button. https://note-wheat-67630147.figma.site/   Whenever I wanted to double check my password I would really quickly click the show/hide eye icon because it felt unsafe to see my entire password on my screen. 

So I thought why not repurpose the scratch gesture typically used in lottery tickets and gift cards to hide information?

For this years makeathon I wanted to keep my submission simple and focus on the feeling behind the eye icon. Specifically the difference between flashing your entire password on screen vs revealing only what you need. 

"

author: Nathan Zerafa
body:
"Introducing Catalyst, a focused AI brainstorming app. Catalyst transforms a single prompt into an evolving chain of structured concept cards. Instead of typing follow-ups, users guide ideas through simple gestures: Refine to deepen, Remix to explore new directions, and Reflect to pause and think critically. Each idea becomes part of a living session flow, allowing focused exploration without clutter. Catalyst reimagines swipe interaction as a creative steering mechanism, shifting it from fast binary decision-making to intentional idea shaping.

Published Project:  https://stone-proxy-25875452."

author: Shivangi C
body:
"TL;DR A small interactive bonsai experience about patience and watching form emerge over time. 
Check it out here:  https://grow-with-bonsai.figma.site  
&  Link to the Figma Community published file I’ve always admired bonsai from a distance. Tried growing one once. Failed. We’re used to shaping things instantly: Click. Undo. Reset. Perfect. Bonsai doesn’t work like that. Sometimes what grows is what you intended. Sometimes it isn’t. So I turned it into pixel-style, bonsai shaping experience. Maybe it is informative, maybe it is meditative, maybe it is confusing—I'll leave that to you.

P.S I"

author: Helo Pera
body: "I designed the scroll itself as a tool for self-control. This year I’ve been trying to step away from screens, but screen-limiting apps only pause me, one click later I’m scrolling again. I created a new interaction where the longer you scroll, the heavier it gets. You set a normal usage time, and after that the feed becomes progressively slower and harder to scroll, as if it were physically heavy. Check it out:  https://genius-format-42210391.figma.site Prototyped this as an example of use on a social feed, but it could work with any infinite scroll. The experience adds weight, slows interact"

body:

author: Joe Balich
body:
"Every AI conversation navigates the same way — you scroll. And scroll. And scroll. No search, no jump-to, no landmarks. The scrollbar tells you where you are. Never what's there. My interaction changes that. Move toward the scrollbar and a contextual layer wakes up. Move away and it sleeps. No button, no mode. Proximity is the trigger. What it surfaces are anchors — landmarks that give the scrollbar semantic meaning. Here, they're your own messages, which you recognize faster than anything else because you wrote them. 

But the trigger doesn't care what it's anchoring to. 

In an article, head"

author: Atif Abbasi
body:
"Taskjar : Your day summarised in a jar.

Inspired by the pickle jar theory, you can add tasks in a jar in the form of Rocks, pebbles and sand.

This involved a lot of experimenting with Matter.js inside Figma Make to define physics for the jar and bodies inside it.

author: Linda Ojo
body: "This year, I decided to read more books, and found that authors describe faces in ways that sound vivid but… what does ""she smiled with her lower teeth"" even look like? So I built Between the Lines  https://between-the-lines.figma.site , a visual dictionary for classic literary expressions that shows you exactly what they look like. For fun, it uses AR facial recognition to see how close you can match the expression in real time. Try it out on desktop or mobile!"

author: Surriya Zahra

Each frame is hand drawn.  ✅Published as a Figma Make project  ✅Editable file shared with  makeathon@figma.com  ✅Posted Walkthrough video on X  🔗Published Prototype Link  https://www.figma.com/make/JqodRE3TESlT08ajSfpjZ5/Father-s-Love?t=3VYfzgmreq8gUEHZ-1  🔗X Post Link https://x.com/i/status/2028072691778093122"

author: Alex Mensah Tenkorang
body:
"🍲🥁 OkraSynth — Cook Your Culture Into Sound What if the next iconic interaction paradigm didn't come from Silicon Valley? OkraSynth is my answer. Drag West African ingredients into a cooking pot and hear your dish compose itself as music in real time. Kontomire becomes soft strings. Plantain becomes bass. Shito becomes a snare crack. Every combination sounds different. Every dish tells a story. When your pot is full, hit Serve — and get a Kente-pattern album cover of the composition you just created. ──────────────────────
🔗 Live demo:  https://stage-judge-51028461.figma.site/  
📁 Editable"

author: Steven Okosieme
body:

author: Chayti Ghosh
body:
"The night before the exam, the ceiling had become your enemy — your notes useless, your mind trapped somewhere between tomorrow's question paper and every mistake you'd ever made. 

At 1:13 AM, you remembered the app, opened it, and pressed the mood that had been pressing on your chest all night — Anxious.

 A deep violet washed over the screen, and for thirty-five seconds, something that sounded older than music held you — rain finding its rhythm, a low hum that knew the shape of your worry. When the sound faded, the room was the same, the exam was the same — but somehow, you were different. "

author: Abubakar Gill
body: "Decido - Train clarity in 60 seconds. We make thousands of decisions every day — most of them reactive. Decido is a behavioral clarity app built entirely in Figma Make. It turns decision-making into a fast, repeatable training system. In a 60-second session, users navigate rotating psychological scenarios across themes like Self & Pressure, Risk & Control, and Identity & Growth. The system evaluates patterns in how choices are made — speed bias, risk tolerance, avoidance tendency, and focus consistency — and generates a dynamic Decision Style with visual insights. Unlike static quizzes, Decido"

author: Aymen Rouabehi
body:
"Hey , this is my submition to the makeathon

I built a hands-free text editing interface using real-time hand tracking. The idea is simple: instead of clicking, dragging, and using toolbars, you navigate and edit a document using gestures in front of your camera. The system allows you to: Scroll through a document continuously without touching the screen Enter an edit mode using a dedicated gesture Select text in real time using both hands Trigger AI-powered transformations on the selected content Compress information (summarize) Expand and enrich text with additional context and research This"

author: Garima Singh
body: "The digital world is built on scrolling & reacting. What if we designed for mindfulness instead? 🌿 For #FigmaMakeathon, I built ZenPond—a sanctuary turning interaction into a meditative ritual. Using Figma Make & Claude Opus 4.6, I pushed the boundaries of prototyping: ✨ Real-World Physics: Reactive logic creates a multi-sensory experience. Move with intention to invite fish to leap to the sound of nature. ✨ New Interaction Pattern: I designed a gesture for pause. When your cursor is idle, fish drift toward your presence. Drawing a circle disperses food, turning idleness into an act of care. "

author: Soham Shirke

The Riddler corrupts every file with snack-themed UI riddles. Mr. Freeze freezes all prototypes solid. Only Batman can save the day one tap at a time. Everything built 100% in Figma Make.

author: Charlotte Iselin Lockert
body: "Stuck designing the same thing over and over? I built Uncanny UX to break that loop. Uncanny UX    The name comes from the “uncanny valley” — that strange feeling when something feels almost human, but not quite. Today, we design for synthetic personas, algorithmic behaviors, and systems that shape how people think and act. The result often feels polished — but slightly artificial. Using Figma Make, I created a constraint-driven case generator rooted in accessibility, environment, and behavioral contradictions. In a culture of infinite tools and instant generation, constraint becomes radical. "

Users can generate hanko stamps for things they’ve done (or create them themselves!) and physically place them onto a monthly passport spread, creating a visual record of effort. It focuses on interaction as ritual: tactile placement, subtle rotations, and overlapping layers that make each month feel handmade. Rather than optimising productivity, Konseki elevates small wins into a daily practice so you can build your own book of milestones. You c"

In an era of instant gratification, we treat objects as replaceable. But memories—like a child’s toy or a grandfather’s watch, can't be easily reordered online. Lost+Found reimagines the community board to foster altruism, creating interactions that feel ""safe"" even in moments of panic. How I Used Figma Make

Leveraged responsiveness to ensure the shift from ""Panic"" (Losing) to ""Action"" (Reporting) feels seamless and grounding. No"

author: Saee Deshpande
body:
"Motion as a Language  What if motion wasn’t triggered… but drawn?
For Figma Makeathon, I explored gesture as input — turning directional strokes into intentional motion.
 The interaction is simple:
- Select a body part
- Draw a direction
- Watch it respond The project explores animation as a responsive system — blending interaction logic with motion design principles like constraint, articulation, and controlled return. Motion isn’t cosmetic here; it’s functional, intentional, and driven by user input.

Try it here:  https://glass-chip-43094673.figma.site"

author: Akhil Desai
body:
"Ideas aren't linear. They collide, mutate, combine.

That thought became ""Forge"".
An infinite canvas where AI generates app ideas and lets you combine them into something entirely new.

Select 2-3 cards and they fan behind the panel like cards in your hand. 
Hit Forge Together.
AI synthesizes. Not summarizes.
Connection nodes draw your creative lineage.
You can literally see your thinking evolve.

I think the way we generate and combine ideas IS culture. Every product, every company, every movement started as two or more ideas and someone was brave enough to smash'em together.

This isn't a de"

author: Fabio Campora

In an era of constant comparison, this app empowers you to embrace the present: today is enough. P.S.: The name ""PositiveWide"" refers to a hypothetical final goal for the app: sharing these text-based sparks via Bluetooth, creating a ""wide"" local network of support without the need for a heavy data connection. 🚀 Figma Community file"

author: Fashola Tunji
body:
"The law was built for a physical world. But today, DAOs get hacked, AI creates art, and people own virtual land. When disputes happen in Web3, who do you sue? I built a VR courtroom. Put on a headset. Stand at the podium. Argue your case before an AI judge trained on legal precedent. Present evidence. Object. Get a ruling. Win and earn XP. Lose and learn. Disputes over smart contracts and virtual property are resolved through reputation and community consensus. The law shouldn’t just happen to us.

We should help shape it, in VR.  https://odd-hatch-29866517.figma.site"

author: Abdulmajid A.
body: "Hey guys meet  inkMood  a playful, handwritten-style note app that reacts to your emotions. Choose or autodetect a mood - Happy, Sad, or Angry - and the entire canvas transforms. Notes bounce, rain falls all depending on how you feel. There are no boring buttons. You scribble to crumple a note into a paper ball. You peel corners to reveal hidden thoughts. You smash notes in half, stack them, or fold them into bookmarks. And so much more, enjoy. Inkmood"

Is it calming? Overwhelming? Premium? Trustworthy? You can also use the History tab to compare websites and see which one creates a stronger emotional impact. Because design isn’t just what users see.

It’s what they feel.
Here is my X:  https://x.com/Juweriya_A/status/2028200080013209945?s=20  
LinkedIn :  https://www.linkedin.com/posts/juw"

author: haneul park
body: "This project began from the idea that while the Latin alphabet unfolds linearly, Hangul (korean alphabet) functions as a grid-based structural system assembled within a defined space, shaped by an interest in the beauty of its structural order. Hangul combines initial, medial, and final elements within a spatial block, and a change in one component affects every syllable that contains it. The site presents this contrast and introduces a generator that lets users deconstruct and recombine these elements, transforming their names into graphic sentences. Meanwhile, the infinity-shaped scroll cont"

Designed and prototyped using Figma Make to explore immersive environmental mapping experiences inspired by platforms like Google Maps. try it:  https://client-manual-84770007.figma.site"

author: Puja Agarawal
body: "🌫️ Unhaze  We show people climate charts and numbers. Nobody shows them their city disappearing. Unhaze does. Open it and your city appears with real location, real air, real haze driven by live AQI data. Not a simulation. Your city, right now. Click any pollutant and understand what's actually in your air. Then drag the slider and watch your landmark physically disappear under thickening haze. PM2.5 climbing. O3 turning the sky yellow. Explore any city on Earth. This was never just one city's problem. Built in under 24 hours with Figma Make using live OpenWeatherMap API, real geolocation, tu"

author: Wu Ricky
body: "heylo :)) 🌟 💫  Counting stars  ✨ ⭐️ I'm a toddler parent, so we’re learning to count these days and have been spending some time counting stars together. author: Erik Nardai
body:
"Interactive Blob Interface  is a bold rethink of how we navigate and consume information. Instead of static screens and rigid menus, content lives inside a fluid, responsive blob. Drag horizontally to deepen detail.

Pinch to simplify into sharp summaries.

Rotate to shift tone — analytical, creative, critical, playful.

Press and hold to generate content, triggered with a subtle glow pulse. The concept is exploratory by design. The use cases are limitless. This project challenges conventional chat UI patterns and pushes toward a more intuitive, tactile, and dynamic way of interacting with dig"

author: Manu M
body: "Collective Hold The internet has always assumed content is one click away. Collective Hold challenges that. This is a spatial canvas where everything is hidden by default. Content only reveals itself when 100 people, from anywhere in the world, hold their attention on a single piece — at the same moment. Not a paywall. Not a login. Just collective human interest as the key. It reframes how we think about access online — turning attention from a solo act into a shared one. Content belongs to those who sought it, together. Collective Hold is a new interaction primitive. One that cannot be comple"

author: Jeroel Padilla
body: "Collective Pulse is a minimal emotional check-in tool that allows users to express their feelings on a shared canvas, fostering community connection and emotional awareness. No text. No followers. No algorithm. Just a gesture. After you place your dot, you sit alone with your feeling for a moment. Then the community appears — and you realize you're never as alone in a feeling as the internet makes you think. The collective field becomes a living map of human emotion. Real-time. Anonymous. Profoundly human. Inspired by Figma's ""Software is Culture"" — and my entry to the Figma Make-a-thon 2026. "

author: Tatiana Papastylianou
body:
"I just shipped my Figma Makeathon project: Era Engine ⏳✨ It’s a scratch-to-dive interactive experience where you “enter” four worlds: 🖼️ Museum 💸 Time is Money 🎭 Poster Generator ✍️ Write as You Feel The fun part: switching decades changes the entire interface — visuals, motion, and interaction language. Design history as a living prototype, not a slideshow 🌍🎛️ Try it here 👇 https://good-runny-41548589.figma.site/ Published to community https://www.figma.com/make/HYbtatOWxNxeMz1O3Isj4g/the-era-engine?t=rrZHzxiMdVMrlrcf-1  
 and Instagram reels🎉 
 https://www.instagram.com/reel/DVV8cyejL"

author: Mohammed Makrane
body:
"I spent about an hour in Figma Make and built something for fun 😅 ⚡ StickMotion Studio You just drag the stickman limbs around… Click play… …and it actually moves: Arms bend Legs kick Head turns Frames are magically recorded No fancy animations.

No templates.

No rules.

Just messing around and seeing what happens. Built it for fun and a quick challenge, but it kinda works! Try it here:   https://dandy-camp-78192161.figma.site"

author: Nikola Milovanovic
body:
"Hi everyone, This is my submission for the Figma Makeathon challenge. I’m excited to introduce Nodify, create any effect you want, test it live, export JSON, and use it for anything you need.

 https://nodify.figma.site   Feel free to try it out! I’ve included a few presets so you have a ready product to see how it works. Of course, there’s still a lot of work that could be done, especially on the outputs, but this is what I’m submitting for the challenge. The app itself is very well structured, so we can add pretty much anything you can imagine, just let me know! That’s it, I hope you like it"

author: Vladlena Cravcenco
body:
"Built during the FigmaMakeathon.

I rethought the traditional vision board and turned it into a commitment system.

Dreamboard is a private space where dreams aren’t just visualized — they’re structured, funded, and completed.

Interactive prototype available here: [ DreamBoard ]

Designed and prototyped in Figma Make.

 #Makeathon   @Figma   @FigmaMake   @contra"

author: Vahid Lenjanzadeh
body:
"Hit the play button: What if your music equalizer had a body? Instead of equalizer bars, a 3D character dances to the music on a concert stage with LED lights and a disco floor, all reacting to the audio. Built entirely with AI tools: UI concept with Nano Banana with create image tool in Figma, PRD with Claude, music generated with Gemini, and the final app built with Figma Make (React + Three.js + Howler.js). 4 switchable characters, 2 original songs, JetAudio-style design using Figma Make.

Source:  Github
 Try it live:  https://trust-adjust-40491506.figma.site"

author: Fotis Pastrakis
body: "Commanding the Grid: AETHRA for #FigmaMakethon The city isn't a database, it’s a living organism.  Inspired by  data.europa.eu , AETHRA explores how open data can be utilized across public services for the common good. For #FigmaMakethon, I’ve envisioned a ""Tech-Noir"" command center that synthesizes the potential of 1M+ data points across 36 countries into a cinematic operations platform.  AETHRA transforms open data into a high-fidelity ""Mission Control."" Use it to orchestrate emergency services, optimize transit, and synchronize city-wide events—turning abstract numbers into immediate, life-"

author: Dimitrios Arapis
body:
"One of my favorite retro games of all times is Transport Tycoon. I remember as a kid wishing I could play it with my friends.

This memory came back once I came across the Figma Make Hackathon, so here it is!

Try out the Web Multiplayer version of TT, exclusively made with Figma Make and Supabase:  https://transporttycoon.arapis.de/  

Supports up to 50 people at the same time (not entirely tested 😅), each session can last up to 1h (but you can re-login) and please be kind (always build a road for someone else to continue)! Enjoy!"

author: Aditya Gupta
body: "Updated submission with live link included below 👇 AI Taste Agent 🍔 Built this during the Figma Makeathon using Figma Make AI. The idea was simple: what if you could build a burger based on your mood instead of clicking through filters? You type how you’re feeling — and the burger forms accordingly. Ingredients adjust, and the protein, carbs, and fat update in real time. I focused mainly on making the interaction feel smooth and responsive, keeping the UI minimal while letting the mood switch drive the experience. 🔗 Live Experience:  https://revel-envoy-29468737.figma.site/"

author: Purva Patel
body:
"The Adaptive Archive - A Living Website Made with Figma Make It is a digital museum that changes its layout based on where users move their cursor. Figma Make helped me turn a simple idea into a live working prototype. With my design taste and creativity, I did not just make a website. I made a living interface that changes based on user attention. Using Figma Make, I created: - Dynamic Auto Layout that grows and shrinks
- Smooth 600ms soft animations
- Color that appears when users show interest
- Heat bars that show engagement This project shows how design can reflect what people care about."

author: Héléna Ly
body:
"I watched one Christmas movie in July. Netflix has never forgiven me.
What if one click didn't define you forever?
Ricochet breaks algorithm bubbles. 

Try it:  https://ricochet.figma.site/  

(Built in 3 days — mostly by Figma Make and Claude, honestly)

Posted on  LinkedIn"

author: Artem Svitelskyi
body:
"For #FigmaMakeathon, I built Stellar Atlas an interactive Solar System orrery shaped by motion and physics.

This isn’t a page you read. It’s a cosmos you explore. Planets orbit in real time. Moons trace their paths. Comets sweep past with glowing tails. Zoom to reveal hidden details, activate Time Warp to watch centuries unfold, or toggle overlays to track constellations and deep-space probes. Click any world to open a glass-panel with orbital data, mythology, and facts. Even light travel time can be measured between planets.

I wanted to see what happens when UI behaves like gravity instead "

author: Megan Hsu
body:
"District Aquarium: A Living Interface for Complex Systems

District Aquarium reimagines data dashboards as living ecosystems.

Instead of charts and static reports, complex systems are visualized as dynamic environments where data literally breathes. Each student is represented as a fish. Progress affects movement, brightness, and depth. System-wide trends influence water clarity and ecosystem health.

Built entirely in Figma Make, this project explores how metaphor-driven interfaces can transform how we interpret complex information. Originally inspired by education systems, the concept can e"

author: Neo | Bryan Hermoza
body:
"Gallery Infinite Canvas

This is my design for the Figma Makeathon!

Showcase your projects on an infinite canvas with smooth, freeform navigation. Explore content without limits, pagination, or interruptions through drag interaction and vertical scrolling. Live Preview:  Gallery Infinite Canvas"

author: Kaleem Mohammed
body:
"Swipe has become one of the most dominant interaction patterns in digital products — yet it remains binary. SWIPE IQ re-imagines swipe as a multidimensional signal, capturing dwell time, filtering behavior, and decisive commitment to reorganize intelligence in real time. Gesture becomes data.

Swipe, quantified. Built with Figma Make for the #FigmaMakeathon.
Website:  https://undo-flint-16558639.figma.site/  
Community:  https://www.figma.com/community/file/1610203193915807859/swipe-iq-reimagines-swipe-as-a-multidimensional-signal"

author: Yuliia Prykhodko
body: "I want to present a social network where users can customize their main page to suit their needs. One of the key features is the ability to increase or decrease text size. Users are not always able to read comfortably in just one fixed font size (due to fatigue, illness, or other reasons). In this app, text adjustment is quick and simple. It also provides an anonymous mode — something that is greatly lacking in today’s social networks. Inspired by Threads and its lively, free-flowing communication. However, people sometimes need the option to remain anonymous in order to share their thoughts w"

author: Michael Vurghese
body:
"MOOD MORPH — An Interface That Feels What You Write
Built entirely with Figma Make | #FigmaMakeathon

The UI is not a container for your emotions. 
It IS your emotion.

Features:
• Cursor Empathy Engine (cursor shape + trail changes per emotion)
• Physics particle system (different physics signature per mood)
• Procedural mood art generated from your session
• Ambient Web Audio soundscapes per emotional state
• Emotion memory — the app carries over your last session's feeling

All transitions: 1.8s cubic-bezier. 
Because emotions don't snap — they shift.
check it out:  https://class-agency-849"

author: Lukas Behringer
body: My submission to the @figma x @contra #figmamakeathon RoloDesk: Re-imagines scrolling through your contacts and notes with a rolodex. Scroll through it or press the tab to run your finger through all cards. Try it out. Add and edit it yourself! 🎨 https://hex-blob-69973331.figma.site

author: Allison LiTing Dai
body: "Need an extra 15 minutes of doomscrolling?  Hold the app for 10 seconds first. Dorsal vagal drift (one of the phenomena that occurs when doomscrolling) requires grounding sensory input.  Which is why Mindful Moments allows you to press and hold something (for ten seconds) while calming colors and grounding questions are asked, to break you out of the loop... ... unless you really need the extra 15 minutes. Try Mindful Moments today:  https://www.figma.com/community/file/1610571019190837558/anti-doomscrolling-app"

author: Kartikey
body:

Check it out here:  https://whole-clever-94229159.figma.site/"

author: Sahil Malgundkar
body: "For the Figma Makeathon, I built something I actually needed. I was great at setting goals but terrible at staying consistent. So I turned productivity into a fantasy basketball league. You draft weekly goals, compete with friends, earn points, and climb a leaderboard. Turns out I didn’t need more discipline. I needed a scoreboard. 🏀 #FigmaMakeathon #BuildInPublic #UXDesign #Productivity Link:  https://chain-tall-44645726.figma.site   Linkedin:  https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7434506437365641216/"

author: Angela Andreea Paralescu
body: "FaithConnect Where Faith Meets Understanding. In a world that often divides along belief lines, FaithConnect brings people together. Whether you're Christian, Buddhist, Hindu, Jewish, Sikh, Agnostic, or simply spiritually curious - this is your space to ask real questions, share honest perspectives, and discover what connects us all. Engage in respectful forums, real-time chats, and thought-provoking debates with people who believe differently than you - and walk away richer for it. Earn badges, climb the leaderboard, and build a reputation as a bridge-builder. Faith doesn't have to be a wall."

author: Martin Studio
body:
"We had a lot of fun building this concept on Figma Make.

Have you ever come home ready to relax… but instead you get stuck debating where to eat, what movie to watch, or what to do with friends or family?

The Problem We set out to solve a universal frustration: the wasted time and friction that comes from trying to decide what to eat, what to watch, or where to go with friends, partners, or family. These small moments of indecision often drain energy and derail plans. The Solution

ShakeIt reimagines decision‑making as something effortless, playful, and beautifully intuitive. Instead of tapp"

author: Tyson Le
body: "In our increasingly busy world, it’s getting harder and harder to stay in touch with the people who matter most. To help solve this, I designed Ties for the #FigmaMakeathon challenge! Ties is an app prototype built to act as a personal CRM—helping you intentionally manage, nurture, and grow your personal relationships. Core features: 🏷️ Smart Organization: Manage your network with custom relationship Tags. • 📅 Events & Birthdays: Track important milestones with automatic reminders. • 📝 Contextual Notes: Save the specific, little details from your catch-ups. • 💡 Activity Ideas: Get fun, ran"

author: Arshia Sandhu
body:
"What if you could cook without ever touching the screen? Introducing CulinAiry — a touchless recipe experience built in Figma Make. CulinAiry uses simple air gestures to control the cooking flow:
• Scroll through recipes with a finger motion
• Air click to select
• Thumbs up to confirm ingredients and move step by step
• Open palm to pause timers, air click to resume Designed for messy hands and real kitchens, this project reimagines how we interact with digital interfaces in physical spaces. Instead of tapping glass, we use intentional movement.

 https://culinairy.figma.site"

author: Jacqueline Guo
body:
"🐰  Rabbithole  is an exploration on how we can make LLM interfaces less linear, and how they can help us learn and build a knowledge base rather than getting buried in a long list of past chat threads.

Built fully in Figma Make over 2 days with ~200 prompts, using Supabase and the Gemini API. So many things I want to add and improve, but here's what I have for now. Please let me know if you run into any bugs!

Happy rabbitholing :)"

author: Suman sarmah Sitoula
body:
"The Accent Weight is a response to Figma's ""Software is Culture""
theme — approached from an unexpected angle.

Figma's brief catalogues 10 iconic interactions that shaped a
generation: pinch-to-zoom, infinite scroll, tap-to-like. But
before any of those existed, there was a more ancient and more
powerful interaction: the moment a person speaks and is judged
by how they sound. Accent is the original cultural algorithm.
The Accent Weight encodes it in mathematics and puts it behind
a slider.  Check it here:  https://tacky-gothic-07246400.figma.site"

author: Mahum Samar
body:
"I designed and built Aera, an interactive, phase-aware wellness experience centered around a dynamic dial that lets users seamlessly switch between cycle phases.  Each phase adapts the UI, workouts, and guidance in real time, supported by a cohesive soft 3D visual system and swipeable exercise cards.  The result is an immersive, emotionally intelligent interface that blends storytelling with functional interaction. https://www.figma.com/make/DSpQKsRN054V1SxjNrwd0i/Aera?t=B7e3VP7RDgaFk5y5-1  

 https://bubble-banner-93864527.figma.site/"

author: Jan Harold Diaz
body:
"Introducing BilliART! Play billiard and make art. You can choose colors, change the brush trails and submit it in the gallery for other users to see. A fun way to make art!

Test it here!  https://billiart-contra-figmamakeathon.figma.site  

Figma Make has been a game changer since it was released. You can now make your design more interactive as it can handle more than just click areas and basic prototype. Or the other way around where you create a functional prototype and design it the way it should be!

BilliART has an admin where I can upload frames and use the colors from  https://coolors"

author: Jeannie Tse
body:
"Morse Moments.
A new way of feeling - Tapping.
Try it here:  https://cake-flex-83301788.figma.site  

Before words, there was rhythm.
A mother's heartbeat. A child's first cry. The drum of rain. The silence of snow.

We type. We swipe. We scroll.
But we've forgotten how to feel through rhythm. Morse Moments is a small rebellion.
- Lock a memory in the rhythm of how you felt.
- Someone, somewhere, will have to match it. Not with a password. Not with a code. With their fingers. Their patience. Their feeling.

Suddenly, they're not just seeing your day. 
They're matching your pace.
Your excitemen"

author: Soumen Sengupta
body:
"The Stealth Shield The ""Why"": As a former Bengaluru delivery rider, I know the ""Risk"" firsthand. In India, 2-wheelers account for 45% of road fatalities, yet they lack the safety tech found in cars. This project brings ADAS to the common rider. Thanks to Figma Make I’ve turned my experience as a delivery rider into a life-saving AI concept for Indian roads. From a 'To-Do' list to a 'Stealth Shield' reality. 

Check out the link below:  https://warm-verse-67659673.figma.site/  

 https://www.figma.com/community/file/1610552417997881310  

 https://www.linkedin.com/posts/soumenprofile_figmamakea"

author: Mehul Chauhan
body: This project broke more times than it worked. Every feature added created new conflicts. Every fix required rethinking the architecture. But iteration is the process. From 5 instruments to 3. From broken sync to shared tempo engine. From chaos to Studio Mode. Built entirely in Figma Make. https://studiosync.figma.site/

author: Yoona Lee
body:
"LinkedIn lets you say you're a founder.
Your calendar lets you say you'll study at 7.
Neither one holds you to it.

I kept seeing the same pattern everywhere: ""CEO"" in the bio with zero proof of execution. Students saying ""I'll start tomorrow"" for the 47th time. Founders posting day 1, day 2, day 3 threads on x then going silent by day 8.

Commitments are free right now. There's no cost to making them and no record when you break them.

CommitMate is the execution layer to LinkedIn, to X, to your own self-talk. A social app where your commitments are tracked publicly, your reliability score up"

author: Paulina Ficner
body: "Aura Snapshot is a generative “emotion poster” that helps you pause and name what you’re feeling right now. Pick emotions, set their intensity directly on the wheel, and personalize their colors—because anger isn’t always red, and everyone experiences emotions differently. The app turns your unique mix into a WebGL artwork you can export, add to a public gallery wall, and use as a gentle starting point for reflection, empathy, and conversation. Try it:  https://wasp-menu-15915384.figma.site"

author: Sanyam Kohli
body:
"I discovered the Figma Makeathon late, but I couldn’t ignore the opportunity to build. Drip reimagines the wardrobe as something personal and intelligent — from Magic Upload to AI-powered outfit suggestions and Mirror Mode. It’s not as complete as I envisioned, but I’m proud of what I created in a short time. Can you believe this was made in just 4 hours Loved the Figma's Make Tool : Grateful for the chance to participate, and I hope to return with an even stronger version.
 https://bubble-hacks-02853187.figma.site"

author: Vladimir Khablov
body:
"L’Écume des Jours (The Foam of Daze)
Most task managers treat time as a rigid, digital grid. The Foam of Days reimagines your schedule as a fragile, living ecosystem of physical objects — iridescent soap bubbles. Using Figma Make, we’ve transformed abstract to-do lists into a dynamic 'foam' where tasks float, merge, and react to the pressure of reality. Each bubble’s size and surface tension represent the task's weight and urgency. As a deadline approaches, the bubble becomes unstable, vibrating and distorting until it reaches its breaking point. If the task isn't completed, the bubble pops — "

author: MADCAT GD
body:
"The infinite scroll was built to trap us. So I built a way out. 🛑📱

Introducing Current—a social app prototype with two brand new digital reflexes:
👉 Scratch-to-Dislike: A tactile upgrade to the hidden ""Not Interested"" button. Physically scratch the screen to erase toxic content from your algorithm.
👉 Shake-to-Reset: Shake your phone to shatter the UI and trigger a custom cooldown timer to get your headspace back.

You can on and off the features and also set a cooldown timer in the settings.

Taking back the steering wheel, built entirely with Figma Make. Watch the demo! 👇

Social Media "

Built in Figma Make

 ekovoice.com"

author: Bennett Ben-Ebong
body:
"I built an AI playlist generator because I was tired of spending more time searching for music than actually listening to it. I wanted something that understood simple things like “late-night drive” or “happy but in a calm way.” 
So I built a tool where you just describe the vibe, and it turns your words into a playlist that actually feels right.

Create your own playlist here  https://ream-shift-45040850.figma.site"

author: Anup Kotur
body: "Design is more than just aesthetics; it’s about behavior. For the #FigmaMake challenge, I created Small Steps—a habit tracker built on the philosophy of marginal gains. Most tools focus on the end goal, but I wanted to celebrate the ""pebbles"" along the way. I integrated a ""Lessons"" section alongside ""Wins"" because growth isn't linear. It’s about turning stumbles into catalysts. 💎 Key Feature: The interactive Jar of Progress provides immediate visual satisfaction for every micro-action taken. Because big changes are just a collection of very small wins."

author: Anya Kishida
body:
"""Sent with love,"" I scrapped my original idea 12 hours ago to build a digital card sending platform instead. 

I love writing cards (especially physical ones) but sometimes it just isn't feasible. I created ""withlove,"" a platform where you can create, share, and send out digital cards that hopefully feel just like the real thing. 

You can sign your message with your trackpad for a personal touch, and share out the card with an easy invite-link.  Learned a lot, would change a lot, and enjoyed it a lot :) https://seam-circus-68549507.figma.site"

author: Elizabeth Tam
body:
"⚡ Excited to share my first Makeathon creation — Spark Board! ⚡ 

Using  hashtag#FigmaMake , I built Spark Board — turning everyday tasks and goals into boards you can play, win, and celebrate. Whether it’s chores, self-care, productivity, or even networking, SparkBoard makes reaching your goals fun!

This has been such a rewarding challenge and a lot of fun to push myself and bring this idea to life.

You can try it out yourself here:  https://ash-apron-09696421.figma.site  

Social Links: 
 LinkedIn  
 Figma Community"

author: Anna Macherkevich
body:
"(Peaceful) Posts
Reimagines always-on culture. Trade endless notifications and inbox pile-up for 3 prioritized postcards on unlock. Momentum with breathing room. Mindful batches that keep connections alive. 
The Problem
Always-on culture forces overwhelm or guilt. Either glued to our phones, keeping up - or ignoring texts, feeling guilty. How do we stay connected sustainably? 
The Solution
No. More. Lock screen. Notifications. Unlock → 3 postcards. Light-weight, engaging. AI starters match your voice. Discard junk-mail, save for next batch, or jump to live chat.  Caught up, present, peaceful. "

author: Angela Andreea Paralescu
body: "EcoScan - your pocket eco-bestie 🌍✨ Scan any product, get the tea on its carbon & water footprint, and find out if it's a slay or an oof for the planet. Track your impact with gorgeous dashboards, unlock streaks, save your faves, and level up your sustainability game, all in one app. Shopping smarter has never looked this good. The planet called — it said you're that person. 💚   https://spiral-dash-57801235.figma.site"

author: Milind Bhalala
body:
"What if you didn’t have to navigate apps at all? I built OmniLayer - a voice-driven AI overlay that generates working buttons and workflows inside any app. Instead of digging through menus, you say: “Generate a button to reply to all unread chats apologizing for delay.” The system:
• Compiles a Smart Action
• Creates a persistent Quick Action chip
• Adapts the message based on delay duration (2 hours ≠ 2 days)
• Reflects real UI changes after execution
• Updates dynamically every time the app opens The button doesn’t disappear.
It becomes part of the app — intelligent, contextual, reusable.No "

author: Atul Ranjan
body:

 “What if scrolling didn’t need touch?” 👋📱

 https://air-swipe.figma.site/"

author: Martin Leon Refi
body:
"Here's my submission!

I reimagined how we rotate our screens, proposing a new interaction pattern: drawing a circle over the screen.
→ Draw it clockwise and the screen will rotate in that direction.
→ Draw it counterclockwise and it will rotate in that one.

I put together a UI to showcase it, but this interaction isn't limited to my design. It's something we could all adapt to make every product flexible to the needs of different users and different contexts.

→ Check my prototype on:  https://circular-motion.figma.site  

→ Download my file from Figma Community: https://lnkd.in/dHdRXUvY
 → "

author: Stacey Uhm
body: "Draw your goals. Fill the board. Make this year count. Build Your Year is an interactive goal-setting experience that transforms your ambitions into a playable bingo board. Instead of writing a traditional resolution list, users draw their goals directly into each square and watch their board come to life. As goals are completed, stamps appear on the board—and when a row, column, or diagonal is filled, the site celebrates with a satisfying BINGO moment, giving users a sense of progress and motivation to keep going. The board automatically updates to the current year, making it a timeless tool "

author: Srishti Verma
body:
"WhisperCode : A secret language you can see. And hear. Live Project:
 https://www.figma.com/make/SZGZ2aKCmzK8AvlzEMk6g8/WhisperCode?t=TLG2pMomEah5XWTr-1 Figma Community File:
 https://www.figma.com/community/file/1610320518678549437/figma-make Concept WhisperCode transforms typed text into abstract symbols in real time. Interaction Type → Encode → Upload → Decode A simple loop.
A controlled reveal. Built With • Figma Make

author: Valerie Therese
body:
"I’ve created a concept project called ""SHELTER — Fund a Night. Change a Life"".  https://speech-syrup-81321399.figma.site/  

A real-time map of available rooms, where anyone can help fund a safe night for someone in need. Every donation creates a ripple across the map, showing the collective impact of small actions in real time. 

I also experimented with a “raid” feature (inspired by co-op gaming eg. Pokemon Go) where people can rally together to join a ""Shelter Raid"" and quickly fund rooms that are in urgent need.

Hope you like it! Had heaps of fun in FigmaMake :)"

author: Godwin  Okechukwu
body: "We have yes. We have no. We have maybe But nobody ever asks how close to yes your maybe actually is. Every decision we make gets forced into a binary. Pick a side. Check a box. Move on. But real positions don't live in boxes. They live somewhere on a spectrum between two extremes. Where Do You Stand is a cultural calibration interface that finds exactly where you are on that spectrum. You drag a card. The entire interface transforms around you continuously in real time ; color, typography, spacing, motion, atmosphere, all shifting under your hand based on exactly where you are. Not after you r"

author: Naomi Jayesh Shah
body:
"What if inspiration tools intervened? Designers don’t need more references. We need to absorb the ones we find. 
Exploring how friction can be a creative feature. After 15 minutes: Saved 5+ images? You’re cut off and sent to reflect.
Saved less than 5? You’re nudged to search differently. Every save asks: why this? A slow feed for designers. Because taste isn’t built by scrolling."

author: Charlene  Chi
body: "I want to reimagine story writing. For centuries, we have written stories for timeless reasons: to create a lasting legacy, to forge a connection with others, and to make sense of a chaotic world by crafting a perfect, orderly narrative. We write to be remembered, to be understood, and to control the ending. This led me to a question: What if a story is written with the risk that it cannot be finished? What if stories are not meant to be perfect, but are instead defined by the beautiful, messy, and shared attempt of their creation? The Babel Chronicle  is my answer. Built with Figma Make in ju"

author: Swarali Pednekar

"Deztract Built for social media users navigating constant digital notifications.  This game turns notifications into physical distraction blocks.

They move toward your 3D glass tower - your focus. Click to destroy them.
Drag to rotate the tower in 3D.
If blocks hit it, the tower tilts and absorbs their color. The clearer your tower stays, the clearer your focus is. Deztract  transforms digital interruptions into a playable 3D interaction, pushing Figma Make into real-time motion, impact, and spatial control. A familiar digital pattern. Rethought."

"Bloom is an ambient AI writing companion that responds to how you work, not what you ask.

Every tool we use goes silent the moment you're stuck. You pause. You undo. You rewrite the same sentence three times. Nothing responds to any of it. So I built Bloom. A writing surface that detects human hesitation and responds to it. When you pause, Bloom notices. When you strike something out, Bloom suggests. When you keep rewriting the same line, Bloom understands. No prompts. No commands. Just your behaviour and software that finally speaks it. Site:  https://relay-asset-48853317.figma.site

body:
"Vibra: Designing Stateful AI Interaction
 https://gray-shelf-35651771.figma.site/  

Most AI tools are stateless.
They generate, replace, and forget. Vibra explores refinement as a first-class interaction pattern, preserving creative memory, adapting to user intent over time, and visually narrowing possibilities as decisions converge. Instead of resetting direction, the system evolves with it. Built entirely in FigmaMake as a study in state-driven, memory-aware AI interfaces."

When you scroll fast, the story becomes concise. When you slow down, it expands with additional detail and context. The experience is centered on behavioural feedback:
• Real-time depth indicator reflecting reading intensity
• Scroll behavior determines content density

author: Saumo Pal
body:
"Introducing VibeCam 🎉 

Smartphones made everyone a photographer. Instagram made everyone use the same filters. But we still take bad photos. You see a perfect full moon.
You capture it.
It looks like a blurry dot. You photograph fireworks.
They look flat and lifeless. The problem isn’t your taste.
It’s that the camera treats the click as the final output. We built VibeCam for a simple reason: the shutter should be the beginning - not the end. Now, when you take a photo, instead of saving a mediocre image,
you get a strip of intelligent permutations powered by AI They’re scene-aware, intent-a"

author: Chinmay  Zinjal
body:
": Meet The Bidding War — a real time 2-player strategy card game where every card you play counts. 
Read your opponent. Outbid them. Win. The rules are simple. 
♠️ You get 13 Spades. They get 13 Clubs ♣️. 
🟥 Every round, a red prize card (diamonds & hearts) is revealed. 
🤫 Both players secretly bid one black card. 
👑 Higher card wins the red prize card. Ace = 1. King = 13. 
🔥 Tie? The prize carries forward. Stakes get higher. 
Highest total prize value wins. No luck. No bluffing. Just pure strategy & reading your opponent.

🎮 Play it yourself →  https://fling-clone-39670569.figma.site/   "

author: Xavier Fernandes
body: "We've been told to ""take better notes"" our whole lives — but no one fixed the tools. You write in one app, link in another, and lose the thought that connected them somewhere in between. The problem was never your memory. It was that your notes were dead the moment you saved them. Your brain doesn't store memories in files — it stores them in connections. Desnote works the same way. Every page you write becomes a node. Every link you make becomes a visible connection. A neural graph grows in real time — showing you not just what you know, but how it all fits together. This is what note-taking "

Building this with Figma Make brought me back. A raga is an ancient melodic framework, a set of ascending and descending notes called swaras. Each one evokes a specific emotion, at a specific time of day. In Raga, you choose your swaras one by one as you ascend, then decide how you descend. Your choices reveal which raga you've built, and the colors shif"

author: Masaaki Taberi
body:
"New Interactions — Human-Aware Interface Prototype Live demo:
 https://clip-column-08152281.figma.site What if interfaces could respond to human presence, distance, and attention — automatically? It introduces three human-aware interactions: - Privacy Guard
When another person looks at your screen, sensitive information is automatically blurred — and restored when they look away. - SoftFocus Text
As you move farther from the screen, the text scales up to remain readable, adapting to your distance naturally. - Attention Audio
When you look away, the interface begins reading a summary aloud, all"

body:
"Sound on the web became synonymous with annoying autoplay ads, so we killed it entirely. Our interactions went mute and nobody noticed. 

For the Makeathon I used Figma Make to design a delightful interaction: drag the card down to reveal card information. Drag-to-reveal has been on mobile for years, mostly for refreshing social feeds. I wanted to take that gesture somewhere more intimate. Figma Make made that happen  #figmamakeathon  

Try it here:  https://card-drag-interaction.figma.site/  

 Link to the Figma Make community file

 Twitter post"

author: Hariharan JK
body: "Hello everyone, 👋 Friction Swipe - Designed for Second Thoughts This is my submission for the Figma Makeathon. It reimagines the iconic swipe interaction by adding intelligent friction based on how you swipe, fast, hesitant, or impulsive. Swipe through the cards. Try swiping quickly, slowly, or aggressively, notice how the interface stretches, resists, and adapts. Not every decision should be effortless. Friction Swipe explores a future where interfaces sense intent and add gentle resistance, helping users pause, reflect, and make better decisions instead of acting impulsively. Try it here:  "

author: Bianca Ursaru
body:
"https://stony-heart-90307000.figma.site/   This project explores a duration-based interaction model built with Figma Make. Modern interfaces treat most gestures as equal. A quick tap and a deliberate hold often trigger the same response. This limits expressive interaction and reduces behavioral depth. This framework proposes a layered interaction system where gesture duration communicates user intent: • Tap → Lightweight action

• Medium Hold → Enhanced state

• Deep Hold → Full contextual transformation This project aims to explore how temporal input can reshape digital interaction patterns a"

My first smartphone was the iPhone 2G, and it truly amazed me, from its software experience to its hardware craftsmanship. That moment had a lasting influence on me as a designer. I incorporated scrollytelling and iconic micro-interactions such as slide to unlock and swipe.

 We tap too fast.

Intent Hold™ transforms impulse into intention a pressure based commerce interaction built entirely in Figma Make. 
Hold to reveal key features.
Hold longer to unlock reviews and deeper insight.

behind every frame. ↳ Zoom → ""Step Back"" becomes a mental health lever

author: Xavier Fernandes
body:
"We've been told to ""take better notes"" our whole lives — but no one fixed the tools. You write in one app, link in another, and lose the thought that connected them somewhere in between. The problem was never your memory. It was that your notes were dead the moment you saved them. Your brain doesn't store memories in files — it stores them in connections. Desnote works the same way. Every page you write becomes a node. Every link you make becomes a visible connection. A neural graph grows in real time — showing you not just what you know, but how it all fits together.

This is what note-taking"

author: Kritik Sharma
body: "18 hours ago I was staring at a dustbin full of crumpled paper balls. Every idea I had was either too expensive, already existed, or fell apart technically. Then I got one that felt different. Blind — a collaborative drawing app where you never see the whole picture until it's done. You draw one tile at a time. Then send to your friend. They draw theirs. Neither of you sees what the other made. When the last tile is drawn, the canvas reveals itself for the first time. What emerges is entirely yours. No AI made it. No template shaped it. All human creativity. Built entirely in Figma Make for #F"

author: Dattaprasad Mete
body:
"Imagine an interface that doesn’t rush to react. It waits.
It gathers.
It responds only when you truly mean it. For #FigmaMakeathon I built Commitment-First Interaction in #FigmaMake — a fullscreen prototype where state changes only when intention surpasses threshold. Movement becomes energy.
Energy becomes tension.
Tension becomes decision. Slow, directionally stable motion builds visible commitment across the surface. The ambient field tightens. Lines align. Structure firms. When the threshold is crossed, everything releases inward — a quiet, decisive settle. Rapid motion never commits.
Hesi"

body: "BIODOME - Keep It Alive. A living, breathing ecosystem sits under glass - and it's slowly dying. Spin the dome, watch birds circle snow-capped mountains, fish dart through winding rivers, and fireflies glow between willow trees. But look away too long and the water dries up, oxygen fades, and the whole world collapses. Water the soil. Purify the air. Balance the temperature. Every second counts. Can you keep an entire world alive?  https://resize-bring-28193287.figma.site"

body:

author: Iman El Sayed
body: "I’ve always been drawn to automation and AI agents. I love that we can design systems that extend how we think and work.  Most AI agents are powerful, yet purely functional. They don’t feel like anything...or do they? As a designer, that gap became my focus. If AI is shaping how we create and make decisions, then its personality isn’t cosmetic, it influences collaboration. Lore explores that idea. It lets you choose between four distinct AI moods, each with a unique behavioral presence and interaction style. I also expanded interaction beyond keyboard and mouse to include voice and touch, maki"

body:

Not a PDF. Not a slide deck. A full interactive storybook — with page turns, halftone textures, and a Spider-Verse aesthetic. Why? Because I'm a designer. My portfolio shouldn't just show my work — it should be my work. Every spread tells a chapter of my journey with quantified impact, not just pretty pixels. If it doesn't make you flip the next page, I haven't done my job. How it adapts: 🖥️ Desktop — 3D perspective book with camera zoom, page-turning, and hidden Easter eggs. Go explore. 📱 Mobile — Swipe-bas"

author: Vadzim Pateshkin
body:
"Nexus PM IDE: The first IDE for Product Managers. Because translating ""make the button pop"" into a 10-page Jira epic is a waste of your life.

 https://badge-skid-34358896.figma.site   ⚙️ Pull: Ingest the chaos. Feed it stakeholder tantrums, messy Slack threads, and vague emails, but don't limit yourself
🤖 Compile: Let the AI translate human input into strict, engineering-grade contracts.
🚀 Push: Auto-route PRDs to your Wiki and epics to Jira, New Components to Figma, and much more. Killer feature: The ""SYS_AUDIO"" toggle. It plays aggressive mechanical keyboard sounds."

author: Alvis @ Designlabb
body: "This is Ink It. The first online Chinese Calligraphy practice platform, built entirely with Figma Make. Calligraphy is one of the oldest art forms in Chinese culture. It's meditative. It's expressive. And for most people outside of Asia, it's completely out of reach. 2 ways you can go about using Ink It:  1) Mouse mode - write strokes using your mouse or trackpad 2) Hand mode - use your camera to write in the air, just like real ink on paper Strokes not looking right? Toggle on ‘Accurate mode’ and the ink ‘snaps’ into the correct calligraphic path as you follow the stroke order. It's forgiving"

author: Melanie Owubokiri
body:
"Meet ""The Invisible Gesture""
 https://decor-snake-93474862.figma.site The Invisible Gesture is an interactive 3D flipbook exploring the cultural reinterpretation of universal hand movements. A ""thumbs up"" in Baghdad can be a deep insult; an ""OK"" sign in Rio, a serious offense. We move our hands instinctively, but the world fills those shapes with diverse meanings. TIG transforms this cultural friction into design-led empathy. Instead of dry facts, users physically ""handle"" meanings through a stacked-card interface. By visualizing the gap between intention and perception, the app turns simple g"

author: Michelle Cheng
body:
"Real doors have rituals. You knock. Someone peeks. You wave. You enter. Virtual meetings replaced all of that with a loading spinner. For this reimagine interaction challenge, I brought knocking back to the meeting. You knock on the door, it sees you through a live peephole using your front camera, you wave, and you’re let in. It’s an inherited motion your hands have done a thousand times at apartments, offices, and hotel rooms. We just never translated it into software. - Try it out 🎉  https://shirt-juice-72274487.figma.site
 -  Social  &  Figma community #FigmaMakeathon"

No engineering background needed. No government connections. Just your phone, your street, and your voice. I designed every screen in Figma Make; from the glassmorph"

body: """Infinite Feed"" is a reimagined scrolling experience that challenges the way we consume content. The feed lures users with an illusion of endless posts. Attempting to go further triggers a SYSTEM LIMIT popup, delivering a playful, conceptual commentary on endless scrolling and digital consumption. https://violin-tamp-19145011.figma.site/"

author: Emily V
body:
"CueOS
Draw the cue. Speak the action. CueOS is a new interaction layer that pairs gestures (to target what you mean) with voice (to say what you want to do). Instead of hunting through tiny menus, you cue actions directly on the UI. Ava’s Journey guides you through 4 mini experiences: Social: dismiss an ad instantly and DM a creator right from the feed

Shopping: jump to reviews, save to wishlist, and open checkout

Reading: start text-to-speech, save to a reading list, and share

Browser: open sites, bookmark, and clean up tabs fast Imagine the possibilities! Try it!"

author: Andrea Fabian
body:
"Depth: A Slow-social experiment The Problem: Social feeds prioritize ""clout"" over nuance, creating a ""Reaction Economy"" where we respond before we understand. The Solution: The Context Gate
Using Figma Make, I created a feed that rewards presence over mindless scrolling: The Gate: Articles are blurred by default. Users must ""Hold to Reveal,"" creating a physical commitment to the content. Active Focus: As you hold, the text clarifies and a progress ring fills, simulating a mind focusing. Mandatory Nuance: Unlocking reveals a ""Perspective Shift"", a mandatory counter-argument to break echo chambe"

author: Sarah Oyervides
body: "Hello Everybody! I designed AutoGuide, an app built to reshape the relationship between mechanics and vehicle owners while giving owners the confidence to repair their own cars. I was inspired by two-sided platforms like DoorDash and Instacart, where both sides (Dasher and Customer) create value within one system. I started with the idea, studied top competitors to understand where their “how-to” communication fell short, and used those gaps to shape a clearer, more supportive guidance through multiple instructional formats in one app.  This software is powerful, and I am excited to learn more"

author: Yanny Cheng
body:
"The Vision Club — The Year You Play ♥️♠️
(See it live:  Click me! ) Vision boards are everywhere right now, but let’s be honest, most of them are static, easy to forget, and can feel a bit daunting to even start. Staring at a blank page without a clear direction can be quite overwhelming. So, I thought, why not turn it into a fun game? Welcome to The Vision Club, a poker-inspired, interactive vision-setting experience. Pick your vision themes, deal your hand, and flip cards to reveal actionable steps. It’s a way to turn big dreams into something achievable, playful, and easier to commit to. I "

author: Santosh
body: "I built ""OnlyFame"" to explore an uncomfortable idea: what happens when attention becomes identity. Using Figma Make × Contra , we designed an interaction where validation isn’t hidden in numbers—but felt in real time. This project isn’t about growth or features. It’s a mirror for how digital culture behaves when being seen matters more than being known. try this out , you are gonna love it :  https://onlyfame.figma.site/"

author: Sumit Singh
body: "Every AI chat lets you talk. None of them let you think. For the #FigmaMakeathon I built Droid Chat. An AI chat interface where conversations become a spatial workspace. Highlight any part of a response, and it becomes a sticky note on an interactive canvas. Drag it, arrange it, and send it back as context for your next prompt. A closed-loop thinking environment, designed for how you actually work with AI. Try it here:  https://shush-east-10244644.figma.site/"

Using Figma Make's prompt‑to‑app capabilities, I generated the interactive timeline, the iconic era navigation (🔥⚔️🏰🌍🚀), and the ""warp"" interaction that defines the experience. The published prototype lives at  here  and is fully inte"

It is about courage, clarity, and confidence. It is about helping a child believe their voice matters. Because communication shapes leadership and opportunity and every child deserves to be heard. As simple as th"

author: Orkhan Jafarov
body:
"I built FigMold for the #FigmaMakeathon — a browser-based 3D sculpting tool built entirely in Figma Make. It reimagines how we interact with 3D — instead of being locked to a mouse on a desktop, your phone becomes a spatial companion device. What it does:
- Sculpt a 3D sphere with real brushes
- Scan a QR code to connect your phone as a live controller
- Rotate the model by tilting your phone, switch brushes from your palm
- Enter AR mode and see your sculpture placed in the real world
- Share your creation when it's done The idea was to rethink 3D sculpting — traditionally a heavy, desktop-on"

author: chris dacs
body:
"Introducing Liquid Wood.

Had a fun time with this app design.
The Mid 90's saw the rise of Liquid Metal.
The Mid 2020's saw the rise of Liquid Glass.

When working with organic textures and materials especially in AI there are definitely certain protocols for language that the AI will understand better. 
Learned a lot about organic and nature focused language and translating it for AI model. 

An app feature that could be used for companies introducing wood stains, hardware brands, and overall brand interaction to give users and customers a fun way to interact and match wood stain colors. 

T"

body: "Synthwave is interactive way to produce your own lo-fi music... and it was built in 1 prompt and 5 minutes. I’m a content creator who uses “copyright free” lofi music in the background of my videos... Disappointingly, not all “copyright free” music is actually free, which means some of my videos have had to be taken down :( So, I decided to take matters into my own hands and create an app that let me quickly create my own music! Feel free to give it a go for yourself!:  https://www.figma.com/make/BXjXAe7NnlbsnDViYYnGxs/Interactive-Lofi-Synthesizer?fullscreen=1&t=0OC9YghFzWPzLazQ-1"

This question inspired me to build IMBUE, a digital tool that turns places into living archives. 

IMBUE reimagines how we explore cities by connecting digital interaction to physical place. Walk to a location and unlock stories shared by the community — from local recipes to hidden histories — revealing the meaning behind the map.

author: Muyideen Jimoh
body:
"In a world optimised for frictionless engagement, what would it mean to design for pause? Most digital wellbeing tools rely on restriction.

Block the app. Start a timer. It works — briefly. Then users disable it. So I explored a different pattern: What if the intervention wasn’t a wall, but a door? A voluntary 5-second micro-pause before opening selected apps.

Complete it or skip it — you proceed either way. Not less engagement.

More conscious entry. Duolingo-inspired: lightweight, friendly, identity-reinforcing. Demo made with Figma Make LinkedIn:  https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/green-door"

author: Ettouzany Abdelkader
body:
"I turned weekly planning into a puzzle. You solve your time. 

Your week is a grid. Your tasks are blocks. You only get 3 blocks at a time. Goal: fill the entire week by placing blocks into the schedule. Rules: - Blocks have different durations (so they take different space) - Some blocks are one-time, others must be placed 5× (weekdays) or 7× (daily) - You can’t place the same block twice on the same day - Place all 3 blocks to unlock the next set of 3 Built in Figma Make for #FigmaMakeathon.

 https://relume-wad-90397426.figma.site/"

author: Karen L. Calderon Q
body: "Beyond Voice , A Gesture-First Spatial Prototype What happens when voice is no longer the primary interface? Sign language depends on multiple parameters, including facial expression and spatial context, that wearable AR devices may not reliably detect. This creates a design challenge: interaction must rely on simplified gestures and clear confirmation feedback. This prototype explores a gesture-led AR experience where a sign activates a cultural spatial transformation. Built in Figma Make for the #FigmaMakeathon https://www.figma.com/make/AO1DJkBDQxQiYajgmAtyB7/Cinematic-AR-Glasses-Interface?"

author: Anadil  Raza
body:
"I made OVERLOAD  an interactive high-fashion editorial experience built entirely in @Figma Make.
The layout reacts to scroll speed.
Scroll slowly → calm, elegant typography.
Scroll faster → fragmentation, distortion, controlled chaos.
Slow down again → it gradually heals.
It’s an exploration of how speed affects perception — and how digital interfaces can visually respond to attention.
Built fully in @Figma Make using components, variants, and scroll-speed driven interactions.                                                                         https://splash-circus-50299399.figma.site"

author: soban usman
body:
"PRISM - Refractive Perspective Engine 
PRISM addresses digital polarization by turning static headlines into interactive, multi-dimensional objects. It shifts users from passive consumption to active critical thinking. The Interaction: Users drag a digital glass prism over a headline to ""refract"" the content. This action reveals three distinct layers of context: Analytical, Empathetic, and Historical. Technical Execution: Built with Figma Make and Claude 4.6 Opus. The system utilizes a variable-driven state machine for real-time text morphing and custom spring physics for tactile refraction. C"

author: Stephanie Wang
body: "Count It Out reimagines the payment keypad - the most used and least questioned interface in digital finance 💸 Traditionally we designed friction out of spending, and it broke our relationship with money. Venmoing $1 or $100 feels identical. A number on a screen has no weight. It replaces the numeric keypad with a physical counting interaction, watching your money pile up like cash on a counter. Bigger payments take more taps, more time, more intention. Key interactions reimagined: - The denomination grid replaces the 0-9 keypad, sitting in the keyboard zone - Bills stack in poker-chip column"

author: Jacob Brown
body: "SIGNET 🚀 I built Signet. A document signing experience rooted in 500 years of seals, stamps, and wax. 🧠 We've forgotten something in the shift to digital. Signing a document used to mean something. The weight of a pressed seal, the deliberateness of ink on parchment. Now it's a click. Frictionless, forgettable, weightless even when what you're signing is anything but that. Signet brings the tactile feel back to truly reflect your identity the emotional weight/importance of this signing. Have a play::  https://write-series-58255787.figma.site/"

author: Said Brjanov
body:

Check it out here:  https://gentle-queue-80590095.figma.site/"

author: Yizhang Li
body: "Have you imagined adaptive AI education? Artverse is an immersive AR learning prototype built with Figma Make. Artverse is not a traditional mobile app. It is an interaction model that merges artificial intelligence, augmented reality overlays, and gamified progression into a unified spatial learning experience. The prototype demonstrates how digital interfaces can respond intelligently to physical exploration. Check it out and let me know what you think:  https://most-modem-51585288.figma.site/"

author: Sean Mazan
body: "https://flex-debug-22515725.figma.site   I built Depthfeed to challenge how we consume information. Instead of a mindless scroll, the interface responds to your pace. Key features explored: Velocity-based Depth: Fast scrolling stays on the surface; slowing down reveals hidden layers of content. The Depth Scrubber: A physical-feeling vertical rail that replaces the traditional progress bar. Immersive Reading: Articles aren't just text; they are atmospheric experiences that shift perspective as you dwell on them. The Constellation Map: A spatial way to visualize your reading ""fingerprint"" and in"

author: Faraz khan
body:
"My creative take with desk companion.

Smile Buddy is a tiny creature that lives in your browser and reacts to your face in real time — it bounces when you smile, sulks when you don't, and throws confetti when you're on a streak. Earn points. Level up. Go from Grumpy Gus to Smile Deity. It's silly. It's science. And it's really hard to stay stressed when a little guy is celebrating your smile with floating hearts. ✨

Demo:  https://smile-buddy.figma.site"

body:
"An AI-powered personal assistant dashboard that orchestrates your day through three input modalities: touch, voice, and gaze tracking. What it does Live weather + geolocation. Real-time OSRM routing. Hands-free gaze tracking (MediaPipe) and voice commands (Web Speech API). Cinematic driving mode. Bilingual EN/KO. WCAG accessible. Built with React, Tailwind v4, Motion, Figma Make. 
(Check in browser for the best result.)

 https://context-aware-orchestrator.figma.site   https://www.instagram.com/p/DVMfgN5EpFZ/"

body:

I built Zendoit in #FigmaMake, an app that helps you take any goal and turn it into actionable steps while tracking your progress. Perfect for learning new skills or finishing projects without losing track.

Try it out here:  https://palm-degree-58569854.figma.site/   I wanted a way to try out Figma Make and create something that actually helps me get things done. Along the way, I learned a lot about designing with AI-powered workflows and fast iteration, and I’m excited to keep experimenting with it. Add a goal, let Zendoit figu"
