body: First Figma makethon Submission Untangle it  https://untangleit.figma.site

author: Edward Lucky Obi
body: "What if Save and Share didn’t live inside a menu or you don't have to look for the icons ? Shorts Pulse is a new interactive gesture system that reimagines how billions of users worldwide save and share on platforms like YouTube Shorts — replacing taps, menu hunting, and long-presses, and redefining short-form video interaction. Built with Figma Make for the #FigmaMakeathon. • Double tap → Like • Double tap + slide up → Share • Double tap + slide down → Save Prototype: https://glaze-self-20304204.figma.site/ Social Posts: Full Walkthrough Video: https://lnkd.in/ewPp6VnM Full Breakdown & Design"

author: Marco Pzz
body:
"Memora is a small experiment on how an interaction can bring us closer to the way we perceive our memories. https://memora.figma.site I’m building Memora for the #FigmaMakeathon: an experiment on how our eyes activate memories.
It’s a website where you navigate through photos using only your gaze: eye tracking follows your movements and brings images into focus, just like when you try to recall a specific moment. I wanted to explore what happens when your gaze becomes the main interaction gesture, and not just another metric: in Memora, looking is already remembering. Built with Figma Make 🔗 "

author: Ash Wesley
body: "Netflix, but just for hobbies and handcrafted stuff."

author: Aleksandra Burshtein
body: "Hi everyone! I’ve recently finished my design studies, and I’m fascinated by how AI can transform our workflow. For this Makeathon, I decided to build a site for my design studio, UX42. What usually takes weeks took me just a few days thanks to Figma Make. My favorite part is the interactive Hero section — I wanted to create a playful ""dialogue"" between the user and the interface through cursor-reactive elements.  As a beginner, it feels like magic to turn a prototype into a live site so quickly. Check it out! https://brick-done-95050177.figma.site/"

author: Anais Devereaux
body: "What Do You Sacrifice? transforms financial survival into an interactive system of tradeoffs. Users don’t just earn money: they exchange time, stress, identity, and freedom. In an era of layoffs and instability, this experience asks a harder question: is this a game, or is this real life? Built in Figma Make to push interaction beyond swipes and into consequence. https://pastel-lurch-43692465.figma.site/"

author: Shambhu J
body: "CAN YOU GET THE HIGHEST SCORE!!, I know you guys are already challenging yourself for the   x   competition, but if you have time, try playing and beating all the other players in Mythic Arcanum. Mythic Arcanum is a Web app game where you can play trivia with special cards that grant you special powers. I don't have a $100k give away for the winner, but I will give you mad respect for trying out the game.  This is not my usual style, but I had so much fun with Figma Make! Game Link -  https://mythic-arcanum-49433631.figma.site"

author: Till Janek
body:
"Night shift to get my #FigmaMakeathon submission in before it’s too late! Meet “Rapid Read” — a simple, easy-to-use app that helps you fly through big blocks of text using proven speed-reading techniques. After 42 versions, it’s finally at a point I’m excited to share. I designed everything manually in Figma, then used Figma Make to bring the interactions to life. The workflow was surprisingly smooth—and genuinely fun. You can now literally build anything you can imagine ✨

 https://rapid-read.figma.site"

author: Dishaant Gilda
body:
"I built a Wild West sniper game for the #FigmaMakeathon. 🤠🎯 I didn't want to build another boring UX tool. I wanted real stakes. So I built Dark Bounty—a game where you hunt manipulative ""Dark Patterns."" It’s completely unforgiving:
🎯 Blind Hunting: No hints. Just a crosshair. Use raw instinct to spot hidden fees and shoot the deception.
🃏 Loot: Collect Wanted Posters explaining the UX laws violated.
⏳ The Twist: You get 20 seconds to build the most evil checkout possible. I’ve attached a sneak peek video below to show how the blind reticle hunting actually works. 👀 👇 Play the full proto"

author: Mohammed Bin Ejaz
body: """Infinite Feed"" is a reimagined scrolling experience that challenges the way we consume content. The feed lures users with an illusion of endless posts. As they scroll, the feed slows, posts fade, and colors dim, creating a tactile sense of resistance. Attempting to go further triggers a SYSTEM LIMIT popup, delivering a playful, conceptual commentary on endless scrolling and digital consumption. https://violin-tamp-19145011.figma.site"

author: Olha Ratinova
body: "What if the sky remembered you? Starborne is an interactive web experience where a simple gesture becomes a personal constellation. Draw your path — and the sky responds. 🔗  https://starborne.figma.site/   Each constellation reveals a spirit, and each spirit carries a unique message — generated once, never repeated. The project explores personalization beyond data: no forms, no prompts, no inputs. Only movement. Gesture → constellation → spirit → artifact. Every result becomes a downloadable poster — a captured moment of transformation. Just motion. Just response."

author: Sajid Munir
body:
"Pet Academy is a friendly and intuitive platform designed to support pet parents at every step of the pet-care journey. It offers easy-to-follow guides, expert tips, and interactive tools to help you learn essential skills for raising happy, healthy pets. 

Whether you’re training your pup, understanding nutrition, planning wellness routines, or connecting with a community of fellow pet lovers, Pet Academy makes caring for your furry family simpler and more joyful — all in one place.

It also has a AI agent to help pet parents discuss about their pets and get relevant info.

Here is a demo sho"

author: Elene Chekurishvili
body: How I built  emotional archive

author: Marie Spreitzer
body: "While I was doing my master thesis, genAI was just starting out to be a thing. I’m happy I could recreate one of my concepts for the #figmamakeathon. I wanted to rethink music interfaces & how, by drawing from the club, we could make listening a more collective experience. “Digital dancefloor” is packed with interactions that are based on real behaviour: moving around, expressing your opinion, dancing, switching floors if the bop isn’t bopping and most importantly, hanging with your friends. on the web ,  figma community ,  linkedin"

author: Jordan Sinclair
body: "Born from a prompt to create a never-before-seen interaction, Cosmic Fabric leverages Figma Make to replace static states with a rigorous, physics-driven ecosystem via a custom Image-to-Physics Pipeline. Rather than hovering, your cursor physically displaces a molecular cloud of tethered pixels. Clicking and holding triggers a ""Singularity Event,"" transforming the cursor into a gravitational black hole that pulls the entire image into a dense, high-energy vortex.  As particles accelerate, a Kinetic Chroma-Shift simulates friction, glowing cyan before a non-linear spring engine snaps the digita"

author: Kar Villard
body:
"I'm thinking of the celestial sky where stars live. So often when we lose someone, we wonder where they go & I think they take a place in the stars. So often that I long for those who had already departed & wanting to talk to them. In this platform, we'll give a chance for those who are grieving to send a message to those who have passed on. 

Driven by my own grief of losing my nephew to depression at a young age & parting ways w/ my grand folks who lived a full life, I created a communal space where we can give the living a place & time to grieve together, which so often has been reduced by "

author: Carlos Henrique Evangelista
body: "I built from scratch an app where you can create visual benchmarks and page analyses, adding sections, filters, and best of all, collections where you can even create your own curated sets for clients or leads. Test your own to see the magic! Links Social post:  https://www.linkedin.com/posts/carlosevans_i-built-from-scratch-an-app-where-you-can-activity-7434410296842014720-qa7V?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAACc_FaEBkmWkH6BlvPNkHAwa8o1Oq2uPHgc   Figma community:  https://www.figma.com/community/file/1610476019063483339"

author: Eurey Noguchi
body: "Modern phones got bigger. Our thumbs didn’t. Warp Tapping rethinks interaction for oversized interfaces. Instead of demanding precision, it responds to intent. A long press activates an adaptive lens that scans the DOM at runtime, builds a visual hierarchy of interactive elements, and enables intent-based focus traversal without hardcoding layout structure. #FigmaMakeathon  Figma Site:  https://vivid-gizmo-26157745.figma.site/   Figma Community:  https://www.figma.com/community/file/1610470380617118596   X:  https://x.com/eureyuri/status/2028644516282912829?s=20"

author: Stanley  Okeke
body: "Design has always been fixed. What if it adapted to you? This is Programmable Taste: an interactive system where Authority, Fluidity, Compression, and Energy reshape an interface in real time. Instead of choosing a theme, you shape the philosophy of the design itself. Built entirely with Figma Make for the #FigmaMakeathon."

author: Charlotte Strohmeier
body: "Hey! I used figma make to create a tool that helps educators create course concepts.  Most course design happens across scattered folders, files, and bookmarks. The connections between what students should know, do, and practice exist only in the educator's head. CourseMap brings it all into one place. Using a mind map or process view, educators can link real-world actions, knowledge, and learning activities — and actually see how the layers of a course relate to each other. https://model-invite-53326945.figma.site"

author: Jasmina Skrbinjek
body:
"I built an interactive prototype of Social Decoder App.

It’s a communication translation tool designed to decode subtext, hidden needs, and emotional expectations in conversations - empowering users to understand social norms and consciously decide how to engage with them, without framing different communication styles as wrong.

I built the full interactive prototype using Figma Make’s AI app builder and structured it around needs-based analysis and Nonviolent Communication principles.

🎥 60 sec walkthrough below.  
🔗  Live prototype   🔗  LinkedIn Post"

author: Damean Rittmann
body:
"I built Glow! A shared pixel art night sky where your messages become stars that slowly fade unless someone keeps them alive. Go add a wish, a gratitude, or an encouragement and watch the sky grow! Hover over fading stars to bring them back. Let's see how bright we can make this galaxy together.

Built with Figma Make for #FigmaMakeathon

 https://stylus-kiwi-78642359.figma.site/"

author: Nikhil Afonso
body: "Interwoven Text is a website that explores the connections between books through shared themes and parallel ideas.  It reveals how different authors approach the same central concept from unique perspectives, allowing ideas to interact across text. By tracing these thematic links, the platform builds a deeper layered understanding of one core idea.  Built using Figma Make and Claude.  https://grab-magma-32888583.figma.site/tree  #claude #figmamake"

author: Katharina Pilz
body: "Pinch to zoom was the gesture of 2007. Make things bigger. Get closer. The problem in 2026 isn't size. It's complexity. We already have AI that can explain anything at any level. But the interaction is broken — copy text, open a chat, type ""explain this simply."" You've already lost the thread. Semantic Pinch reimagines the most iconic touch gesture for the AI era. Same two fingers. Same motion. But instead of resizing what you see — you resize how complex it is. Pinch in. It distills. Pinch out. It expands. No context switching. No prompting. Just a gesture you already know, now carrying new m"

author: Piotr Jarkowski
body: "My entry for the Figma Makeathon. Contra-Boy OS is a specialized freelance management interface designed with a aesthetic inspired by the Fallout Pip-Boy. This project recontextualizes the professional dashboard into a tactical, mission-oriented environment built for independent workers. Key Features Freelance Command Center: A mission-based dashboard where projects and leads are managed. Wasteland Radio: A functional audio module integrated into the OS to provide an atmospheric background for the workspace. Try it here:  https://contra-boy.figma.site Figma Community post X post"

author: Riley Jones
body:
"Sending my submission in.

A fun little passport app that you can add images/destinations/notes etc in a fun drag and drop canvas.

 https://flee-basil-80419237.figma.site  

#figmamakeathon"

author: Sudeep Sharma
body: "2014 called. They want their UI patterns back.  The idea was to use the Mac time-machine interaction to show older messages. I quickly realised it only made sense for thoughtfully written messages. So I came up with the concept of ""DayLetters - a letter a day"" Send beautifully designed virtual letters to your friends. Once a day. Delivered at 9am local time. Smoothly scroll through old letters when you think of them. Write back when if you miss them. I had a bit of fun and a lot of nostalgia making this thing. An almost working app with a new (really old but great) interaction pattern brought "

author: Maryam Alakoso
body: "Imagine being able to still spend a nice time with your loved ones even when they are far away.  Near is shared space video call app that replaces the standard call screen with an intimate 3D environment. Avatar representation with real voice and facial expression mapping, built-in games with persistent score history, and a minimal dark UI designed for emotional connection. Designed and prototyped end-to-end using Figma Make. The flows, design system, interactions, and full prototype. Use the link below to use the site:  https://sift-draft-89729945.figma.site/ #figmamakeathon"

author: Prodigy Dan
body:
"Designers often joke that client feedback feels like a game of survival. 

🎱 Pixel Perfect Pool: turns that metaphor into a high-fidelity, multiplayer reality. Players compete as The Designer or The Client in a physics-based battle to either ""ship the assets"" or ""bury the project in feedback.""

Play Beta -  Link Here>>   or https://ux.pool.figma.site The game is built on a modular ""middleware"" architecture and uses professional-grade rigid-body physics to ensure every ""Pixel Perfect"" shot counts. Showing a re-imagined use of the iconic design interaction & collaborative feature in figma and a"

author: Amanulla A
body:
"Make Try-On  A virtual try-on app built with Figma Make that lets users upload a garment and instantly see it on a virtual model — no physical fitting needed.
-Open in desktop for better experience.   -Model used: IDM-VTON via Replicate AI -Challenge we faced:
Figma Make runs inside an iframe which blocks direct calls to external APIs (CORS restriction) so calling Replicate's API directly was failing with a network error. -How we solved it:
Deployed a lightweight proxy using Vercel server less functions to route API calls safely, bypassing the iframe restriction entirely. Link :  https://earth"

author: Hannah Conrad
body: "When was the last time a ""connection"" actually led to a conversation? Standard networking is broken. We’ve all felt it –the endless scrolling through generic posts, the vanity metrics, the surface-level small talk at events about “what do you do”, or even having a great conversation with someone, then never speaking again. That’s why I created 6 Degrees, a CRM (Community Relationship Management). The refreshing “vibe"" of 6 Degrees isn’t just a UI choice – it’s baked into the foundation. Using Figma Make x Calude Opus 4.6, I was able to transform an idea into a fully functional MVP without a si"

author: Parash Rautela
body: "https://camera-fifth-71711992.figma.site/  An immersive 3D virtual temple where users navigate and interact using natural hand gestures detected via webcam: Core Features: 👊 Fist Gesture → Move forward through temple ✋ Open Palm → Stop and observe 👏 Clap 3 Times → Join live bhajan/kirtan (music intensifies, devotee avatars appear) 🙏 Namaste → Offer prayers at deity (triggers aarti ceremony) 📹 Real-time Hand Tracking → Red skeleton overlay shows users exactly what camera sees Here is the explainer video  https://www.loom.com/share/9523b42fc7fa40fdbf2f35fc8951469a"

author: Irene Porro
body:
"I spent 3 days building a cultural time travel
experience for the Figma Makeathon (my first one btw) and I'm
kind of obsessed with how it turned out. ""Nostalgia isn't a crime"" is an interactive
cultural archive covering the 80s, 90s, 2000s
and 2020s. Each decade has its own visual identity,
sounds, and atmosphere. What's inside:
— Cinematic intro with animated globe and starfield
— 4 decades with unique typography, colors and sounds
— Spotify playlists embedded per era
— Easter egg facts on every card
— A soul decade quiz with 6 archetypes
and a concert ticket result you can share
— Birth year"

author: Roman Rashid
body:
"ContentFlow is an AI-powered content repurposing dashboard that transforms long-form videos and podcasts into platform-optimized social media posts. Upload once, generate dozens of posts for Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram & TikTok—all with AI-driven insights and analytics.  Upload content — drag & drop videos, audio files, or paste YouTube links
AI processing — automatic transcription, key moment detection & highlight extraction
Generate posts — platform-specific content for Twitter threads, LinkedIn posts, Instagram captions & TikTok scripts
Smart editing — adjust tone, length, hashtags & call-"

author: Xavier  Fernandes
body: "158 million elderly people in India manage their medicines with a plastic pill box and hope. The box works — but it can't notify anyone, can't track what was missed, and can't be refilled without a caregiver present. PillPal bridges that gap: a digital twin of the pill box they already own, designed and built with care so that a first-time smartphone user can take their morning medicines with a single tap. Every screen was crafted for delight and clarity — Photo carousels for medicine identification, a week calendar for daily tracking, and a step-by-step refill guide that walks caregivers thro"

author: Jiny Song
body:
"I built a high-conversion landing page for an indie thriller author, using advanced prompting to bridge the gap between moody design and robust functionality. Figma Make was easy to use and I had a lot of fun building this project.

 https://case-white-06444760.figma.site/

 Figma Make file on Github:
 https://github.com/jinysong/Shewriteswebsite

Figma Community Page

author: Atabay Kuliyev
body: "Nexus — A Spatial Interface for Life Balance For this Makeathon, I explored a new interaction model for personal productivity. Productivity has been designed as lists for decades. I wanted to explore what happens when we design it as space. Instead of dashboards and lists, Nexus places the user at the center of a connected system. Health, Work, Learning and Finance exist as dynamic nodes that evolve and influence one another. The interface visualizes balance — not just output. I also integrated an AI layer that observes cross-node patterns and generates calm, system-level insights focused on a"

author: Amisha Parmar
body:
"🎮 Learning the Design Process Through Gamification

I created an interactive Snake game that teaches the importance of following the proper design process sequence. 

The Challenge:
• Navigate through 8 design stages in correct order
• Skip a step? You fail (just like in real projects!)
• Each stage: Research🔍 → Ideate💡 → Sketch✏️ → Wireframe📐 → Prototype🔧→ Design🎨 → Test🧪→ Launch🚀 

It's a fun reminder that there are no shortcuts in good design – every step matters and builds on the previous one.

Try it out :  https://lnkd.in/emxDDZaf
 Made for the Contra x @figma Makethon"

author: Ashish Soni
body:
"Introducing Aura — a voice AI orb component built entirely in Figma Make. Voice interfaces are becoming a defining part of how we interact with technology, and I've always wanted to craft what that moment of connection actually looks like. Custom GLSL shaders, fBm noise, Fresnel lighting, smooth state transitions — all running in real-time. Configure every state, tweak every color, and export a production-ready React + Three.js component in one click.

 https://www.loom.com/share/ef5f89a371454993ab092360612a8a84"

body: "https://camera-fifth-71711992.figma.site  An immersive 3D virtual temple where users navigate and interact using natural hand gestures detected via webcam: Core Features: 👊 Fist Gesture → Move forward through temple ✋ Open Palm → Stop and observe 👏 Clap 3 Times → Join live bhajan/kirtan (music intensifies, devotee avatars appear) 🙏 Namaste → Offer prayers at deity (triggers aarti ceremony) 📹 Real-time Hand Tracking → Red skeleton overlay shows users exactly what camera sees"

author: Josefina Poventud
body: "A little late to the party, but I made it! Here's my Figma Makeathon submission ✨ SpellBoard is an interaction experiment that turns spelling practice into a fun, playful adventure. It started with a simple need: helping my son practice spelling in a way that actually felt enjoyable. English isn't my first language, so I also wanted something that would help me practice alongside him with more confidence and accuracy. Here's how it works: parents enter custom words, choose a theme, and kids drag letters to build each word as they move through a gentle, game-like world. It's a prototype explori"

author: Praneet Gupta
body:
"For my #FigmaMakeathon entry, I designed a landing page experience for an architecture studio focused on clarity, structure, and visual hierarchy. The goal was simple:
Create a digital space that reflects how architecture feels — calm, intentional, and timeless. Key focus areas:
• Editorial-style layout inspired by architectural portfolios
• Strong typography and whitespace to highlight projects
• Minimal UI that lets visuals carry the narrative
• Clean, modern interaction flow built using Figma Make Live project:
 https://yoyo-drone-14849612.figma.site Would love feedback from the community �"

author: Ayo Ayo
body:
"This is the first official experiment within the DADI project, built entirely using Figma Make. I created an interactive experience where users track DADI’s project progress in real time. This is my official entry for the Figma Makeathon. If we win, this becomes the spark that kickstarts Phase One. The website is live.
The journey starts at $0.
Everything will be documented publicly, daily updates, active projects, strategy, and financials. From zero… upward. #FigmaMakeathon https://truce-elude-65046250.figma.site/"

author: Angela Andreea Paralescu
body: "Ok, I think all of us wanted to play with Jarviss (extras s). 😍  So now I got the chance to try and build something at least similar. Have fun!!! It really is a nice trip down memory lane. (still working on some updates, use help for commands list please) )  https://bagel-app-22221374.figma.site/"

author: Plamena Peykova
body:
"4, 000 weeks  . That's roughly how long you get. Not as a metaphor - as math. I couldn't stop thinking about that number. So for the Contra × Figma Makeathon (my first), I turned it into something you can feel. The experience starts with 4,000 dots drifting across your screen like dust - aimless, scattered. Move your cursor and they pull toward you. Stop, and they drift away. That's the metaphor: time scatters unless you're intentional about it. From there, the project walks you through your own life:
- Your weeks, visualized as a grid
- Seven questions that paint your past in color
- A flip t"

author: Melanie Owubokiri
body:
"Meet  Oni , my #FigmaMakeathon submission. ☁️✨ What if the next world-changing idea is trapped in your sleep? The Periodic Table, the sewing machine, and even Google started as dreams. But dreams are fragile—they fade the moment we wake up. I built Oni to bridge the gap between your subconscious and reality. With Oni, you can:
- Immerse (concept): Experience a fluid dreamscape UI that follows you even after you close the app, subtly transforming your device's theme Capture: Record fleeting dream details before they vanish. Visualize: Turn those fragments into instant AI-generated imagery. Brid"

author: Paavan Buddhdev
body:
"For years, I've had an ever-expanding list of interesting words saved on my phone.

I add to it when I stumble upon a new word in a book, but I never go back to look at the list, so these interesting words never stick in my head.

So I wanted to see if I could make a super quick (and extremely stylised) simple app in order to (buzzword warning) 'gamify' this process.

So I made INTERESTING: a quick quiz that gives me a five-round-challenge, testing me on some of these words I've picked up. Something to do instead of doom scrolling.

 https://interestingwords.figma.site/"

author: Jeroel Padilla
body:
"100+ pulses! 🙌  A huge thank you to everyone who has anonymously shared their emotional state on Collective Pulse during the Figma Make-a-thon challenge. We just hit our first major milestone of 100+ shared dots. Collective Pulse is designed without an object on purpose. The gesture is the physical act of locating yourself in emotional space — where the movement of finding your position is the expression, not a label you select after the fact. How do you feel right now?  Add your pulse here:  https://collective-pulse.figma.site/  

Here's my entry post:  https://on.contra.com/DpRcKH"

author: Devanshi Sharma
body: "As designers, we often face decision paralysis, wondering how users will experience our choices. For #figmamakeathon 2026, my submission introduces tools that help designers understand the impact of decisions as they make them. Impact shows a user’s emotional journey, cognitive load, and live deltas for suggested changes. It highlights sensory outputs to design better for neurodivergent users and previews which elements matter most to different user types- like anxious users. Health snapshots file’s design hygiene, flagging UI inconsistencies. Brief summarizes a project’s frames, context, and "

author: madhesh P
body: "AETHER is a futuristic mission control suite that bridges the gap between raw astronomical data and a intuitive human interaction through a stunning , glass morphic visual interfaces                                                                                                                                                          This  is my design created using figma make with an new innovation"

author: Luxix Uman
body: Short-Form Video Editing | Reels TikToks & YouTube Shorts free 15 sec simple avalable #VideoEditing #MotionDesign #ShortFormVideos #ReelsEditor #TikTokEditor #YouTubeShorts #ScrollStoppingVideos #EngagingContent #ViralEdits #CleanCuts #FastEdits #ContentCreatorTools #SocialMediaVideo #EditingMagic

author: Michael Almiroudis
body: "art(i)physical - A Vision Test for the Algorithmic Age Originally developed through my MFA and presented at  Paris Photo 2025  alongside the AI image series I Was Made in My Motherboard, art(i)physical reimagines the traditional eye exam as a posthuman interaction system. Users draw a trace on a crosspad, transform AI-generated images by closing one eye and navigate halftone shifts & light/dark modes through blinking. Eye charts unfold as cultural anagrams. The project treats software as an intertextual field where perception, language & code continuously reshape one another positioning vision"

author: Said Brjanov
body:
"I built The Global Supply Chain Constellation to reimagine supply chain transparency as a collaborative, 3D spatial experience where every ""illuminated"" node represents a real-world ethical choice. By weaving hidden production webs into a shared galaxy of intent, this prototype pushes the boundaries of digital creation to transform how we collectively shape global culture.

Project Link:  https://ozone-clone-49845730.figma.site/  
Social Link:  https://x.com/brjanov/status/2028616692109365371?s=20"

author: Wiwenid Chunky
body:
"I’ve always wondered what it would look like if systems built for efficiency felt more human. So I built Human CAPTCHA, where verification happens through shared drawing. Each verification leaves a trace, and someone else continues it later. Over time, these traces grow into a collective CAPTCHA shaped by fellow humans who never meet. It’s security built by humans, for humans.
 https://theme-turn-06411622.figma.site   Social Link"

author: Jane framer
body:
"Most mood boards are graveyards. Beautiful references, zero output. Mood Board OS is different it reads the vibe of your images, names your colours (think ""Shadow Ink"", ""Pale Gold""), and exports them as real design tokens your codebase can use. The gap between inspiration and implementation has closed. Fun with it. 

App link:  https://churn-boar-02711597.figma.site"

author: A9 Studio
body:
"Dance to Paint 🎨💃 Turn your body into a paintbrush. This website uses your webcam to detect movement and translates your dance into abstract style paintings in real time. How it works: Move in front of your camera — every gesture, wave, jump, or spin becomes flowing paint on an infinite canvas. The app tracks your body's motion and maps it to expressive brushstrokes.

Have fun!

 https://body-brush.figma.site/"

author: Shreha B

"📰 What if a newspaper could print itself?  The Daily Detach is Design's newspaper, built entirely in Figma Make — styled as a classic broadsheet, powered by live data. It fetches the latest stories from Product Hunt, Shortcut (Figma's blog), Muzli, and couple of Substack accounts for now.

author: Safia Alif
body: "REACH, when words aren't enough - send what you feel.  https://reach.figma.site   You know that feeling when Siri lights up with that glowing pink gradient? It feels like something is listening. Like something understands. What if we could send feelings like that to each other? This concept reimagines digital communication as an immersive emotional signal — combining color, motion, and sound to transmit feelings in a way messages can’t.  Inspired by Siri’s gradient glow, the experience allows users to send emotions through a shareable link, transforming communication from something read into s"

author: Roberth Dudiver
body: "Zapatito Cochinito is a fast social party game: players join a room and choose a shoe. When the host starts, a rhyme chants while a selector hops between shoes. When the rhyme ends, players are resolved via: Classic (Switch) marks players to eliminate on the second hit; Number/Count picks based on a chosen number; or Hybrid. The game continues automatically through rounds until the win condition is met (typically last player remaining), then celebrates the winner and generates a shareable result card.   https://dark-brave-98730655.figma.site/"

author: Gen Chang
body: "I built a gesture-based autofill system for the Figma Makeathon. Instead of typing repetitive text, you can long-press inside a text field to activate drawing mode and sketch a shortcut that instantly inserts predefined text. The system detects direction changes in the stroke and maps them to customizable strings; enabling faster, more accessible form input without relying on traditional typing. This is an exploration of gesture-based autofill as an OS-level accessibility concept, built in Figma Make."

body: "Lit  reimagines digital reading with frictionless notetaking—drag to highlight text, release the highlight to create notes, and organize AI-generated study guides without the hindrance of tiny menus on mobile or extra integrations. I designed and prototyped Lit in Figma Make, prioritizing simplicity, readability, mobile usability, and learning-centered UX. Click  here  to check out “Lit.” in Figma Community. Click  here  to drop a comment or like on LinkedIn."

Pick a weak spot. Scratch the foil. A canvas where AI doesn’t generate outputs, but co-creates in real time, reacting to: Your cursor movement
Your rhythm

author: Baka Diop
body: "🖤 BAK — A SaaS with a Soul The tech industry uses AI to build tools that make us faster and more productive. For this hackathon, I wanted to ask a different question: Can we use Figma Make to build a SaaS that makes us heal? Bak is a fully functional generative visual board, but I deliberately took off my ""cyber"" hat to let the artist drive. I didn’t just build an solution; I built a AI sanctuary. Its only metrics of success are joy, hope, and the relief of letting go. Bak is an AI SaaS with a soul. Fully functional engine using Figma Make and Supabase for our Community Vault. I’ve used Figma"

author: Tamires Segata
body: "What if every story was made for your reader? StoryLab turns anyone — a parent, a teacher, a big sibling — into a storyteller. No writing experience needed. Just answer a few simple questions about who the story is for, and we build a fully personalized book or comic around them. Pick the age, the character, the art style, the language. Add accessibility needs.  Instead of generating random AI content, StoryLab guides creators first — ensuring each story is intentional, age-appropriate, and emotionally aligned with its reader."

author: Mahmoud Hanafy
body:
"Infinite scroll was never designed for your well-being.
It was designed for retention.

I built Intentional — a prototype that redesigns infinite scroll into a friction-based, awareness-driven interaction.

Instead of endless passive consumption:
• Posts are blurred until you press and hold for 3 seconds
• A circular progress indicator reinforces conscious choice
• After every 5 posts, the UI pauses and asks:
“Why are you here right now?”
Learn.
Connect.
Escape.
Not sure.

The goal isn’t to slow users down.
It’s to give them back control.

Built entirely in Figma Make.

🔗 Live prototype:  htt"

author: Priyanshu Gupta
body: "THE LIVING INTERNET The Living Internet is a generative, interactive prototype that treats human creativity as biology. Every idea in the system is a node — a living cell with energy, ancestry, relationships, and a natural lifespan. The ecosystem responds to everything you do: scroll forward through time to grow the network, hover to reveal invisible energy flowing between connected ideas, click to enter a node's world, hold to charge a fusion, walk away and watch ideas fade This is not a dashboard. author: John Justice
body:
"Inspired by the classic aesthetic of 80s Atari titles, Kaiju Rumble is a high-octane arcade experience that task players with one simple goal: Destroy Everything. 

Featuring a vibrant, retro-pixelated art style and a scanline-heavy display, the game captures the nostalgic feel of a smoke-filled arcade cabinet. Players take control of a towering monster, using WASD to navigate urban landscapes, the Spacebar to jump on other monsters, the J key to unleash devastating physical attacks, and the Q key to fire a signature Laser Beam. 

To achieve ultimate victory, you must survive 7 intense levels "

body: "🚀 Contra x Figma Makeathon Submission  — Contra x #FigmaMakeathon Proud to submit my experimental prototype for #FigmaMakeathon with Contra An experimental prototype exploring interfaces that respond to human presence instead of clicks. I designed three original interaction models — Attention Field, Interface Temperature, and Interface Gravity — that allow the system to reorganize itself based on focus, usage patterns, and behavioral clustering. Together, they form a unified adaptive interface that evolves in real time. Grateful to be part of this exploration with Contra x Figma Makeathon. We"

body: "Let's reimagine the chat interface. Every AI conversation has an invisible layer. You say something. The AI decides what you meant, then responds to its interpretation, not your words. Then it sculpts that interpretation into a parametric shape from 11 parameters: geometry, presence, energy, color. No templates. Every shape is unique. Your message and the AI's response orbit the interpretation like satellites, connected by thin tethers. The interpret"

author: GOURANSHI CHOUDHARY
body:
"A gravity canvas about letting go and making a mess 🎨 We’re used to making things perfectly.

Click. Adjust. Undo. Refine. Paint doesn’t work like that. Sometimes it spreads the way you expect.

Sometimes it does something better. So I built Let It Spill - a small interactive canvas where you drop paint and tilt to let gravity take over. No precision tools. No pressure. No undos. Just motion, colour, and a bit of chaos. P.S. Turns out describing “messy paint” to a machine using physics rules is its own kind of experiment :)

author: sagar ghuge
body:
"AURAX - Human Insight Scanner.

AURAX simulates a facial analysis AI that scans a portrait and generates a psychological intelligence dashboard - trust scores, dominance probability, archetype classifications - all delivered with 94% confidence. Then it collapses. The confidence drops. The interface fractures. The system admits every metric was randomly generated. No biometric analysis was ever performed. For a moment, you trusted it. That moment is the experiment. AURAX doesn’t analyze people.

It analyzes our instinct to trust machines that appear certain.
 https://relink-heavy-82710743.figm"

author: Maryam Ghaznavi
body:
"It’s been intense building this in 48 hours for the #FigmaMakeathon. Most productivity tools let you start more than you can finish.

This one doesn’t. OneLoop is a constraint-based execution environment.

You get one mission. You can’t start another until it’s closed. No stacking tasks.

No silent abandonment.

Abort requires a reason.

Completion requires proof. The idea is simple: constraint builds discipline.

Instead of tracking tasks, it forces behavioral closure. Still early. Still raw.

But the core question is live: Does forcing loop-closure actually change execution behavior?
Check i"

Users must ""Hold to Reveal,"" creating a physical commitment to the content. Active Focus: As you hold, the text clarifies and a progress ring fills, simulating a mind focusing. Instead of swiping people, users swipe between bold and safe life choices — and watch their future identity evolve in real time. Each swipe dynamically reshapes the interface: backgrounds morph, typography adapts personality, and a live dashboard visualizes happiness, stress, financial growth, and life trajectory. The experience transforms a familiar gesture into a reflection on how small decisions shape long-term identity. Built entirely using Figma Make during the official challeng"

"We've spent decades designing apps that demand users adapt to them, learn the menus, find the buttons, read the docs. Vektr asks a different question: what if the app adapted to you instead? Vektr is a concept design tool that understands natural language.
Tell it what you want to do, export your design, invite a teammate, apply a drop shadow, and it walks you there, step by step, highlighting exactly what to click and why. No onboarding flows. No tutorial videos. No hunting through settings. Just a conversation. This is a glimpse at how human interaction with software could change where the g"

author: Zakria Asim
body:
"Most productivity tools start with your tasks. State starts with you. Before you see a single task, you do a 5-second ritual — drag a glowing orb across four energy states:

Scattered, Energized, Focused, or Burned Out.

The entire interface restructures itself around your answer. Burned out? Three easy wins. Breathing UI. No pressure.
Focused? Deep work only. Timer on. Silence.
Energized? Full list. Stretch goals. Confetti.
Scattered? One task. Just one. I've built tools that optimize for output for decades.

State optimizes for the human behind the work.
Built with Figma Make.

Tool Link:  h"

body: "Retailers often rely on guesswork when designing store layouts, even though customer movement and product visibility play a major role in sales performance. I built RetailFlow, an interactive store layout simulator that allows retailers to reposition fixtures, simulate shopper traffic, and visualize heatmaps, impulse zones, and dead space across the store environment. As layouts change, the system updates performance analytics in real time to highlight opportunities for improving traffic exposure and product placement. Built using Figma Make for the #FigmaMakeathon challenge."

With Figma Make, I was able to bring it to life in just one evening.

Drift prototype reimagines swipe by turning decisions into a spectrum of intent. It lets users express curiosity, interest, or strong intent, and aligns matches based on actual human hesi"

author: Lynn Zeng
body:
"Deathline — A Mirror for Our Weeks 

Time feels infinite, until suddenly it doesn't. Every app I open is a planner, screaming at me to do more. None of them ever made me feel how little time we actually have. So I built Deathline. 
It shows our whole life as a grid of weeks, one for every week of a 90-year life. The weeks we've lived are filled deep red. 
Then it asks us to commit to one meaningful thing and do it with one of the weeks we have left. 
Mark it done, and that week turns gold, one week, well spent. 
We can keep adding them, slowly filling a life with weeks we chose to make count. "

They arrive through lived experience, already packaged with a thousand tiny feelings and nuances we carry without thinking. So what if an interface tapped into that? One glance and you know exactly how much you're holding. Create, save, and track your goals right in your browser, the same way you'd hang, dry, and fold your laundry. "

author: Ken Shew
body:
"Meet Dear.

We've made saying ""I love you"" disposable. A text, an emoji, a $4 card that looks like everyone else's. Dear makes one small act of love feel worth keeping. You answer five small questions about someone you love: a color that feels like them, a place you think of, a small thing they do, one line you'd want them to know. Dear then composes a one-of-one keepsake: a short letter written from those exact details, over a soft painting generated from the details of your answers. Because it's built from the little things only you know, no two are ever alike. Built with Figma's suite: the "

author: Supreet @rlvnt
body:
"Your design mentors need this, and honestly, you do too.

I surveyed and spoke with 20+ designers, motion designers, and animation experts, as well as everyone working with us at  The RLVNT Studios .

One recurring pain point kept showing up: having the right visual, but not the right style. ""Hey GPT, add a new effect to this image, but don't change anything else.""

I heard variations of that request over and over again. It made me realize that AI has fundamentally changed how creatives approach their workflows. People know what they want, but getting there is still frustratingly inefficient.
"

author: Gabe Cagara
body: "🧲 FRIDGIT — A Shared Fridge for the Internet’s Memories Excited to share my submission for the Figma × Contra Makeathon! Growing up, I moved between countries and collected magnets everywhere I went. At first they felt like souvenirs, but over time I realized they were really memories—places I lived, trips I took, and moments I never wanted to forget. That made me wonder: what memories are other people holding onto, too? So I built FRIDGIT, a shared fridge for the internet’s memories. FRIDGIT lets people transform meaningful moments into digital magnets, pin them to a communal fridge, and dis"

author: Mesai Memoria
body: "Meet ScribblePrompt. It started with a moment at work. I was whiteboarding with one of our engineers, and the conversation felt faster, clearer, and more productive. That made me realize I wanted the same kind of experience with my coding agents, without sending screenshots, typing long explanations, and still not quite getting what I meant. So me and   built ScribblePrompt in Figma Make: a tool that lets you whiteboard with an agent, so ideas start as sketches, not walls of text. If you like design tools that make ideation feel more natural, we’d love for you to check it out. Try it out:  scr"

body:
"DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN VERIFICATION  🗳️

warning: for full experience do the test in the link above, before reading or watching explanation below. No data is stored.

--
I worked in digital privacy for years, and it was hard because the issues are invisible, - you don't feel or see your privacy being violated. That's part of the trick.

Every CAPTCHA watches how you move - your hesitation, your timing, the way your cursor drifts. It just never tells you. This is the same checkpoint except with a narrator. I wanted to make the invisible visible, - just how much data we give away unknowingly.

But"

author: Alexia Kai
body:
"I built a tool to help people learn to draw. 
It's called  ✨Deconstruct✨
 https://deconstruct.figma.site/   Built it in FigmaMake Learning to draw is mostly about training your eye. Knowing what to look for before you even pick up a pencil. But that part is hard Deconstruct breaks down any reference — face, body, hand — into its basic construction. The shapes that hold everything together. And if you want to go further, there's a camera mode. Set your phone above your paper and trace directly. The structure comes to you. Nothing fancy. Just the foundations, made visible. ❤️ Made for the @contr"

author: Lisette  D. Brown
body:
"The hardest part of creative work isn't creating. It's deciding what to create next. As creatives, we make hundreds of decisions every day: • What should I work on next?
• Which idea is worth pursuing?
• Where did I leave off?
• What's the next step? That mental load adds up. So for the Config Makeathon, I built Ezzi; an AI-powered creative operating system designed to reduce decision fatigue and help creators spend less time figuring out what to do and more time actually creating. The feature I'm most excited about is the Memory Engine. Ezzi remembers context, projects, priorities, and how yo"

author: Utkarsh Dhairya Panwar
body: "What if digital creation felt as natural as working with real clay? Mitti is a gesture-controlled pottery experience that lets anyone sculpt virtual clay using hand movements. Instead of learning complex tools, users simply shape, narrow, widen, and refine pottery through natural interaction. Start with different pottery styles, adjust wheel speed, and save finished creations to your personal collection. Mitti explores how creative software can become more intuitive by replacing menus and controls with direct physical interaction. Built using the Figma ecosystem for the Config Makeathon. Links"

body:
"Flickers, The Disco Light You Already Own

Check it out here -  Flickers

 Most of us experience music the same way. Headphones in, screen off, scrolling something unrelated while a song plays in the background.

I wanted to build something that pulled people back into the music itself and not just hearing it, but feeling like the room was part of it. 

So I made Flickers. Upload any song, hit play, and your entire screen be it laptop, phone, tablet, doesn't matter it turns into a full-room light source that flickers in real time to the actual beat of your music. No app, no hardware, no DJ lig"

This is my entry for the Config Makeathon. I built a 3D brick simulator where you can create and play with LEGO-style bricks using only your hand gestures. The goal was to make building feel more natural, playful, and interactive by bringing the physical experience of playing with bricks into a digital world.

author: Carlos Fernandez
body: "I present Doodle Fonts, my entry for Config Makeathon. Making a custom typeface can be very complex and time consuming. Nearly every designer who wants a weird little display font for one project ends up licensing something generic, begging a type designer for a favor, learning a new complex pipeline or quietly giving up. Custom type is gatekept — not by talent, but by tooling. The most personal element of a brand is the one most people aren't allowed to touch. Doodle Fonts fixes that. It's a font foundry that fits inside a canvas. You draw each glyph — freehand pencil, shapes, a dot grid, wha"

"My submission for the Config Makeathon is one I WISH I spent more time working on but nonetheless, meet GB Maker. 

GB Maker is a small game engine built right from Figma Make. It includes a tileset manager, object manager and logic customisation. 

It was built with a single workflow:

Design Brain Dump, to then scaffold in Claude, and finally build in Figma Make.

From the ground up, the UI and background functionality were built out separately, ensuring one doesn't break when the other does.

This proves that tools like Figma Make can help us build complex programs, leveraged by people beco"

author: Mohammad  Rafat
body:
"Workflow Quest - An Instrument for Independents. The Problem
Independent creators and freelancers are overwhelmed by Tool Sprawl, Dashboard Fatigue, and AI Anxiety. Late-night questions like “Am I using the right stack?” or “Is my workflow future-proof?” go unanswered because traditional audits feel like soul-crushing admin work. The Solution
Workflow Quest transforms dry workflow auditing into a ritualistic, joyful performance — like playing a synthesizer with your operational DNA. I designed and prototyped a fully interactive, skeuomorphic instrument inside Figma that mimics a high-end Rolan"

I asked myself one question: ""What feeling am I uniquely equipped to digitize?"" The answer came from a childhood memory. Growing up, I was fascinated by Rajasthani Kathputli — traditional string puppetry that's been alive for over 1000 years. Not just by the puppets, but by the puppeteers. How they gave life to something lifeless using only their fingers. I wanted to bring that feeling into the browser. So I built Digital Kathputli — an interactiv"

Voice recordings are rarely preserved. As generations pass, family history becomes fragmented, leaving future generations with names and dates but little understanding of the people behind them. Traditional family tree tools focus on genealogy and structure, b"

author: Maksim Lomachenko
body:
"Slay or Fail — a Y2K tarot oracle that reads your design's fate. every designer ships with a pit in their stomach: deadlines, clients, the fear it'll flop. 

So we cope with rituals — this one's just prettier. Pull 3 cards, the meter calls it (certified slay or glorious disaster), get a cursed-but-true prophecy, let go, hit send. 

The recipe: 80% Figma Make, 20% content gen + prompting across ChatGPT, Claude & Gemini, and a pinch of love.

Cosmic RNG, not a perf review. 
 Pull a card → 
 Try on Community →"

People can upload everyday moments and turn them into reusable roots: motifs, palettes, textures, weaves, and embroidery ideas. What It Does Design System Gen turns a client moodboard in"

Well, this app reimagines the digital nomad journey in the city I know best, Lisbon, through the lens of a local friend. author: Evelyn Evie
body:
"Are you slouching? What if music could change your posture?

'Tale' is a music player that helps you build better posture using classical conditioning through music.

Neck pain affects over 200 million people worldwide, including many children and adolescents. Tale has a fairy theme, making it just as suitable for children and teenagers as adults. 

Using your webcam, Tale tracks seven key points on your face and shoulders every frame. Slouch → Music pauses → Sit up → Music resumes.

Over time, your brain associates good posture with uninterrupted music, naturally building healthier habits. A "

author: sakshi ranglani
body: "I built something for my mom. We talk every day. But we've never made anything together. Not once. So I built KINSHIP — a place where two people across any generation gap build a private visual language that only they share. No typing. No reacting. Just drawing. You arrive. Type your name. That's the door. A prompt surfaces — ""the kitchen table"" — and you both draw blind. Neither sees the other's version until both submit. Then the screen goes dark. Both drawings develop slowly, like photographs in a darkroom. Hers. Mine. Same word. Different worlds. Over time the archive grows. Every word you"

First Frame is a digital museum of beginnings. Users share their past and present, and the system generates a personalized frame with growth scores, skill insights, and a unique artifact identity. Every story becomes part of a shared, explorable canvas. Process and Figma Suite:

author: Mya Nguyen
body: "We scroll for hours and feel nothing. What if that same impulse could take you somewhere? That's the question behind Art Universe — an infinite art discovery experience built entirely in Figma Make. Most museum apps show you art. Art Universe pulls you through it. You start with one painting. Hold to explore. Suddenly you're looking at every artist who worked in the same decade, used the same technique, painted the same subject on the other side of the world. One artwork becomes a portal. Then another. Then another. Van Gogh leads to Monet leads to a Japanese woodblock print from the same era."

author: Facu Salinas
body:
"Trazo - a small workout for your creativity: It's just a stone. But it's also a crab, a snail, a happy face.
Depending on who's looking! We all have AI tools at our fingertips. What changes the result isn't the tool - it's the creativity you bring to it. And that can be trained. The idea:
Trazo gives you an ambiguous shape: a stone, a leaf, a crumpled ball of paper.
You rotate it, find something in it, and draw it out. You name what you saw.
Then it reveals what everyone else saw in that exact same shape. Same shape, a dozen different things.
Being creative isn't about drawing well. It's about"

This is Cocoon. Cocoon is an AR sensory filter concept. Featuring a single dial that controls how much of the world you want to let in. Visually, aurally, atmospherically. You stay aware, you stay social, you stay safe. And you get to dial down the world a notch.

The concept works like this. You arrive at Times Square, and before you even step out of the subway, you can start to feel the intensity. Lights, billboards, cars honking, chatter like a roar. 

Activate Cocoon, and a dial appears. As you turn it, two things"

author: Katarzyna Gąska
body: "What if you could feel a photo? Millions of blind and low-vision people can't - the images we love stay locked behind a screen. So we built Felt: drop in any image and it becomes a tactile relief with braille, ready to 3D-print. Felt redraws your picture into clean, raised lines, writes a braille caption and turns it into a real 3D file you can hold. Felt was built in Figma. Claude shaped the idea how a flat image becomes a raised relief, and how to map each shape into readable braille - and through Figma MCP it worked right on the canvas with us. In Figma Design we built the whole product: th"

"💃🏻 Dance the Emotions 💃🏻 turns a dance into a wordless diary.

author: Kashvi Goel
body:
"What if you could reach into a moment and capture its colour forever?

That specific orange the sky turned at 6pm. The blue of a wall in a city you'll probably never go back to. The green of the season just before it changes. These are moments worth noticing and preserving - but a photo buries everything in a single frame you passively snapped. 

So I built Pluck, a gesture-based mobile web app, so you can just point your camera at the world and literally pluck the hues out of the scene in front of you with a pinch - like picking a flower. Each colour you gather fills a region of a small illus"

author: Dylan Weicker
body: "There's a trip you keep meaning to take. A person you keep meaning to call. A version of your life you assume you'll get around to — later, when things settle down. We all do this. We treat time like it's infinite, because the alternative is uncomfortable to look at. ""Someday"" stretches out in front of us, soft and endless, so we move the things that matter into it. There's no rush. There's always next year. But almost none of us actually know how much time we're working with. We picture ""the rest of my life"" as a vague, generous cloud — we've never seen it counted out. The Rest of My Time cou"

author: sohum Bhatnagar
body: "REWIND Every music app today feels the same. Skip, volume, EQ. All the same flat tap on the same flat screen.  REWIND brings back the physicality of real hardware. Six disc slots. Three EQ knobs. Two faders. One click wheel. Each control does one thing. Upload your tracks to the shelf, load them into a slot, and play. The knobs and faders process audio in real time through the Web Audio API. The VU meter shows live frequency data. This isn't a music player with a hardware skin. It's a functional audio interface that happens to run in a browser. The design language is intentionally brutalist. M"

"Undertone: an animated background generator that exports to Next.js and drops onto your website in seconds ✨ I built Undertone, a website background generator. They all end up looking the same, and tiny details like the speed and the pauses are hard to change. 

So I built Undertone: pick a feeling, customize everything, draw your own, then export straight to Next.js and drop it into your site in seconds. It's just"

author: Aarna Taparia
body: "As textile production gets infinitley more mechanised,  traditional forms of textile making often get forgotten. Leaving these centuries old cultural pillars at risk of extinction.  With my entry for the #configmakeathon, I aimed to tackle this porblem by digitally preserving 3 of India's textile crafts, in an interactive gameplay tutorial that thakes users through the pre-production, weaving, dyeing and detailing processes of creating these crafts. By removing the barrier to entry to produce these crafts, it allows the indigenous knowledge of these textile crafts to be preserved and and enjoy"

author: Samara Al Badri
body: "For #ConfigMakeathon, I created Someone Left This For You 🪭 A tiny paper-crafted digital experience where strangers leave little worlds behind for one another. The idea came from two things I love: creating thoughtful digital experiences and the paper craft and origami I spent hours making as a kid. I'd fold cranes, boats and tiny creations for hours, amazed that something so simple could become something magical. I wanted to bring that same feeling into a digital experience. So I imagined a place where people could quietly build tiny handcrafted paper worlds, hide anonymous notes of kindness"

You can even share your stamps with friends and family, making it a fun way to relive your journeys/memories together. How I built it: 1. Brainstormed a couple of concepts in FigJam before gathering inspiration from Pinterest. 1. Wrote an initial prompt, then refined"

author: Durgadevi R
body: "In India, we celebrate with light. Every occasion, big or small, a wedding, a festival, a new shop, a street during a temple procession, gets strung with serial lights. Bulbs draped over doorways and rooftops, glowing into the night. It's been part of how we mark joy for as long as I can remember. So to celebrate this Config Makeathon, I built something in that same spirit Serial Lights: a tool for painting with light. You draw strings of bulbs onto a dark board, give each strand a rhythm, chase, twinkle, breathe and then flip the switch and watch the whole thing come alive, glowing like a cel"

body: "Found Type - A living alphabet, found in the wild Found Type is a collaborative web app where people photograph letters of the alphabet found in the real world - on walls, signs, buildings, packaging, pavements - and together build a living typeface made entirely by the world. author: Devon Bulgin
body:
"For the Config Makeathon, I've built Interdimensional Snake, an experimental game that blends beautiful mathematics with design. 

I built it to expose more people to mathematical ideas, and to show they can be experienced visually in a way that's genuinely fun. 

It's a reimagining of the classic game Snake, but in 3D, with two modes:

Escher - crawl across all six faces of a cube, slipping through wormholes as gravity reorients around you.

Metamorph - survive on dynamically morphing topologies (sphere, torus, Klein bottle, Möbius band, etc), it's tougher, but the more visually striking of t"

author: Hang X
body: "I designed and built a new NYC subway map that changes and reroutes itself automatically in response to service changes. Instead of reading about confusing service updates, the map shows the updates graphically by rerouting the lines. I used Figma Make to build a custom map drawing and viewing tool and then used that to hand draw all 423 stations and 588 route segments for all the permutations of potential route changes. All the stations and routes are connected to Google transit feed data from the Metropolitan Transit Authority (MTA) so the map is live and updates on its own. The MTA text-bas"

body: "As a brand designer, I'm always looking for ways to speed up the final stretch of a project, and delivering logo guidelines is one of those steps that tends to eat up far more time than it should.  So for this Makeathon, I wanted to test whether Figma Make had the power to fix that, and it does. Guidelines Studio 1.0 is a faster way to hand off logo guidelines to a client. You fill in the information, upload your existing brand assets, and it generates a clean document for you. I was impressed by how much is possible. And this is only the beginning. I'm planning to grow it into a fuller brand "

author: Aleyna Çatak
body: "Motel Blues — build rooms out of your memories.  motelblues.com Every room you've ever loved still holds a memory. Motel Blues lets you build those rooms — and walk back into them. The idea. A photo is flat. A memory is a space — it has a layout, a light, a feeling. We're great at losing memories and terrible at keeping them. Motel Blues turns a memory into a room you can actually stand inside: you sketch a floor plan, generate it in 3D, fill it with objects, and tuck a memory inside each one — a song, a photo, a video, a link. Then you share it: public rooms have a number, private ones a 6-di"

body: "Kickoff, the companion that helps you start. We don't struggle to do tasks so much as we struggle to begin them. That first step is the hardest part, especially with ADHD, burnout, or plain overwhelm. Kickoff takes whatever you're stuck on, breaks it into one tiny, doable first move, and a friendly character talks you through it, one step at a time. No streaks, no countdowns, no guilt. It rewards the bravest part of any task: starting. How I built it: I leaned mostly on Figma Make and designing directly on the Figma canvas. I started by building a small design system with an iterative approach"

Upload a mobile screenshot and see your design through the conditions real users face every day: bright sunlight, color blindness, low vision, a shaky commute or a scratched screen. It is you checking your phone outside.

author: Surbhi Newaskar
body: "I built a digital tool rooted in two real craft traditions. It's called Indikon. The first: hand block printing  centuries-old Indian craft where artisans carve wooden blocks, dip them in natural dye, and press them onto fabric. The second: texture hunting , a practice where people press soft clay onto surfaces, lift the impression, and carry a pattern home with them. Like a rubbing, but tactile. Like collecting, but physical. Indikon is the digital version of both. Built entirely in @Figma Make, with the motif library generated in Figma Weave  real carved Indian wooden block stamps translated"

body:
"Unmade A receipt for every idea that almost disappeared. Ideas are strange. They arrive without warning during a shower, a midnight scroll, a conversation with a friend, a walk home, or a random moment in class. For a few seconds, they feel important. Potentially life-changing, even. Then they're gone. Most ideas never fail because they're bad.
They fail because they're forgotten. Today, our thoughts are scattered across notes apps, screenshots, voice memos, sticky notes, WhatsApp messages, and half-finished documents. The more ideas we have, the harder they become to revisit, connect, and act"

author: Md Mahdi Hasan
body: "Hi everyone 😊 Really excited to share FigmaCrews — a multi-agent AI studio I built for the #ConfigMakeathon (@figma x @contra 💙) 🔗  https://figmacrews-agentstudio.figma.site/ FigmaCrews is basically my proposal to Figma — what if Figma had a built-in AI team? Right now, designers leave Figma constantly. Research, strategy, content, planning — all happening in different tools, then coming back to design. That's a broken workflow. So I built a working prototype to show what it could look like. You get 6 AI agents inside Figma: Manager, Researcher, Digital Marketer, Designer, Figma Designer, a"

author: Guadalupe Badallo
body:
"TONO 🎨
Live color tracker and palette generator

The problem:
Color inspiration doesn't happen at your desk. You see it in a café wall, a piece of fabric, a golden hour out the window — and by the time you're back in Figma, it's gone. There's no way to pull a palette from the real world in real time without leaving your design tool, bouncing between apps, and losing the feeling entirely.

The idea:
Tono connects your camera to your design workflow. Point it at anything — a physical object, a photo, a scene — or upload a picture and it extracts a live color palette in real time. The colors upd"

author: Vikiiing CQ
body:
"🏮Sensoji Omikuji | Sensoji Fortune Slip 🎋: An interactive mobile prototype that recreates the on-site omikuji experience at Sensoji Temple. From walking through Kaminarimon Gate and shaking the omikuji canister, to drawing a number and opening the fortune slip, I wanted to bring that small travel ritual into the interface. Sakura petals, dark gold tones, and temple-inspired scenes create a subtle sense of actually being there.

 https://pants-folk-20252265.figma.site"

author: Said Brjanov
body: "Proud to share my submission for the #ConfigMakeathon 🦋 Every designer knows the feeling: you finally nail the logo — and then comes the part nobody talks about. Building the bento board. Mocking it up on a t-shirt. Laying out the colors and the type. Re-rendering it for the client, for Instagram, for Dribbble. Hours of manual work in Illustrator, Photoshop and After Effects… just to present work that was already done. So instead of inventing a problem to solve, I built the tool I kept wishing existed. Meet Reveal — it turns a logo into a complete, animated brand presentation in minutes. I we"

author: Chintan N
body:
"Hello everyone I'm excited to share my submission to the #ConfigMakeathon 

 The Motion Notebook , a calmer place to learn motion design, where complex animation becomes simple to feel and understand. When I was learning motion, the knowledge was everywhere and nowhere. Locked in expensive courses, scattered across blog posts, or buried in docs written for engineers. 

So I built the place I wish I had. Nothing is just text. You watch each motion, mess with it, then read what is going on in plain words. The main motion types, how timing changes the feel, and what triggers each one, all in one "

author: Aghna Wasim
body:
"There's this beautiful tradition of floating lanterns out across the water. Small lights, let go together. Lumière is a quiet digital echo of that, for the times you just need a peaceful corner to slow down in. Here's how it works: you light a paper lantern, decorate it, leave a thought, and set it adrift on a still, dark pond. The water is shared, so you can drift past other people's lanterns, see what they made, or read what they left behind.  There's also a soft ambient soundscape running under it all. By playing around with the water lily, you can shape the sound.

You can try it here:  ht"

author: He Gao
body: "Like all desk workers, I spend all day with my hands married to a keyboard and mouse. Hours of computer use keep the wrists static and the muscles in the fingers tight and overworked. We all know we should take breaks and stretch, but it's boring, easy to forget, and nothing pulls us back to do it again — me included. So I built  Grow  - an app that turns hand and wrist stretches into something interactive. Using just the webcam, it tracks hand movement in real time to grow flowers. These movements are mapped to four simple finger and wrist stretches, each targeting a different muscle group. T"

author: Amr Khalaili
body:
"PETWORKING 🐱 - a pixel art gamified networking platform where your cat does the networking for you. The problem: remote work is lonely. Connecting with people from the industry is cold. There's no organic way to meet people. The solution: a cat daycare where your cat represents you. Drop your cat off, and while they wander around, AI finds you 3 daily connections based on your skills, tools, and goals, and writes you a warm intro message to send them. What I built: - Live pixel art canvas with animated cats and real time interactions
-AI vision analysis that matches your real cat photo to a p"

You can activate perks using ""/perk-name"".  Don't rush, check the perk list because it may cause damage as well. spend 4k of credits on failed ideas. The tank war was great but the art was not good. I bought a subscription in third party products to handle the art for me and brought them to figma make through custom mcp (connectors) but they didn't work. The art is not good at all. Disappointed on myself and on those products. I simplified the "

author: Harsh  Upadhyay
body: "I never thought this would ever be possible, seriously.. I built a 3D zombie survival shooter inside Figma Make, a tool that is meant for designing websites. It is called DEAD ZONE and it runs in the browser. You drop into an open apocalyptic city with houses, abandoned cars, dead trees, and thousands of blades of grass moving in the wind. A full day and night cycle runs over it, so the sky moves from daylight to dusk to moonlight to dawn while the fog and shadows shift with it. There are six types of zombies, and they do not charge you on spawn. They wander until they see or hear you. Gunshot"

author: Alice D.
body:
"Terra: Civilian-First Mutual Aid terraapp.figma.site Concept
Terra is a community resilience platform that operates across two temporal phases: - Before (Calm): Functions as a reciprocal local marketplace. Neighbors trade land-clearing, tool loans, or transport in exchange for communal meals, firewood, or future favors: building a trusted social network before a disaster strikes. - During (Crisis): The interface transforms instantly. Non-essential features suspend, the community log closes, and a prominent red ""Call 112"" banner pins itself to every view. Forms strip down to four critical utili"

author: Sebastian Moreano Mesa
body: "submission time for Desk Doodles ✏️ I built a shared public desk where you can draw the little objects around you, restyle them through a hand-drawn rendering system, flip them between 2D and 3D, and leave them next to everyone else's doodles. The part I care about most: your hand doesn't get erased. Most tools clean up your sketch until it stops feeling like yours. Desk Doodles tries to keep the wobble, pressure, hatching, messy little mark energy — then carry that same character into 3D. Under the hood, there’s a smart rendering system looking at the drawing and making decisions per region: "

author: Ivan  Galdík
body: "We're excited to submit our project to the Figma Makeathon. We started with a simple question: how does AI actually design? What's happening between the prompt and the final result? Is the process consistent? Can it be reproduced? And what happens if we change just one small part? NODY is an experimental real-time design interface that makes the AI's creative process visible. Instead of generating a finished design behind the scenes, NODY translates an abstract prompt into its own visual language: a network of interconnected nodes, each with a role and effect. Watch the design get built in rea"

The idea started from a simple question: What if health tracking didn’t feel like punishment? Most wellness apps focus on numbers, calories, weight, perfect streaks, or rigid routines. But for people managing chronic symptoms, fatigue, mood changes, medication, supplements, and emotional burnout, consistency rarely looks perfect. Luma turns care into someth"

What idea was it trying to communicate? Which element was worth keeping? As a result, the inspirations were still there, but the analysis and reasoning that made them useful had disappeared. I would then have to dig through scattered notes or sta"

body: "Yearbook - We built Yearbook, an all-in-one website for university students, orgs, and faculty to discover and track campus events. Students can chat and upload photos directly on each event's page, all designed and built in Figma Make."

author: Sameer Gaikwad
body: "Breathverse: Aqua Ring A childhood toy, rebuilt at planetary scale. Breathverse reimagines the nostalgic handheld water ring-toss game as a cosmic, microphone-controlled experience. Players select a left or right air jet, then exhale into their microphone to propel floating rings toward celestial targets. Each captured ring transforms into a glowing planetary orbit, gradually building a small solar system through breath. The hidden objective was to make controlled breathing feel playful rather than instructional. Instead of presenting another meditation timer or wellness dashboard, Breathverse"

body: "Introducing Hobbyverse 🌌✨ I have a feeling I'm not the only one with way too many hobbies. 😅 The problem is that every hobby seems to live somewhere different. So I started wondering... What if every hobby had its own planet... all in one shared universe? 🌍 That's Hobbyverse. A playful social platform where every hobby has its own interactive world to explore. In"
