author: Aarna Taparia
body: "By Hand helps preserve the knowledge and craft of India's endangered heritage textiles. Through a series of interactive, game-like interfaces, you learn how these textiles are made, step by step, becoming part of a digital textile workshop. You don't just read about the craft; you practise it Three interactive, game-like makers guide you through the real process, step by step: block-printing Ajrakh, weaving Muga silk, and stitching Banjara embroidery. Every piece you complete joins one growing collective quilt. Every piece you make is sewn into one growing collective quilt - a shared archive s"

author: Sugeng Priyanto
body:
"Last minute submission. So happy to finally submit this project for Config Makeathon.

Project Name: Toolbox: A Local Micro-Grid Utility Share
Category: Building with Purpose Award & Innovative Workflow Award The Problem:
Modern neighborhoods are paradoxically connected yet isolated. We suffer from ""resource silos""—where one person buys a $200 pressure washer they use once, while three neighbors rent theirs. This leads to financial strain, environmental waste, and a lack of genuine community interaction. Existing platforms are too transactional, broad, or unsafe for true neighborhood sharing. "

author: Chisom Aniefuna
body: "Hi Creatives,  My entry for Config Makeathon Some words never get sent.Not because they don’t matter, but because we were too afraid, too late, too proud, too hurt, or too tired to say them. The Unsent Museum is a digital museum for those words. A space where people can write the message they never sent, choose the emotion behind it, and watch it transform into a living animated artifact inside a public museum of human emotion. Love. Regret. Grief. Hope. Closure. Each artifact is generated from the visitor’s own words, meaning no two pieces are ever the same. Every message becomes something vi"

author: Mohammed Alyazji
body:
"🧵 Introducing  StitchLab  — where traditional embroidery meets modern design tools For the #configmakeathon, I built StitchLab: a browser-based cross-stitch and tatreez design studio created entirely with Figma Make. Cross-stitch carried stories through geometric patterns for generations — but many digital embroidery tools still feel outdated, complex, and disconnected from how designers create today. I wanted to imagine: 
What if creating embroidery felt as natural as designing in Figma? ✨ Inside StitchLab you can: 🎨 Design on a real stitch canvas
Draw with X-stitch, half-stitch, and back-s"

author: Guadalupe Badallo
body:
"BE PRESENT 
Live project:  https://b-prsnt.vercel.app/  

We've all been there. You're at dinner with friends and somehow, without deciding to, everyone's on their phone. The conversation just... stops. I wanted to build something that actually changes that — not by lecturing anyone, but by making it a game. What if the person who checks their phone the most has to buy dinner?

The idea: 
B.prsnt tracks phone usage during social gatherings, automatically, through the browser. The host sets the stakes, everyone joins with a code, and the app does the rest. Every time someone leaves the window, "

author: Fred Austin
body: "We just shipped Stickies. 🎉 Journaling has always felt like a chore. Stickies flips that. Instead of writing about your day, you sticker it. 📸 Take a photo → ✂️ cut it out → 🎨 style it → place it on your journal sheet. That's it. Your day, captured in seconds. We built Stickies around one idea: journaling should feel tactile and expressive — not like homework. Every interaction is designed to feel like something you've done with your hands before. Peel. Place. Flip. Share. If you've ever started a journal and abandoned it by day 3, this one's for you. 👀 Link:  https://tilt-queue-76154881.f"

author: Scott DS Young
body: "Tusk & Quill: An AI-Assisted Editorial Content System Independent publications often struggle to create consistent, high-quality social content. Every new post requires design decisions, formatting, and visual exploration, making content production slow and difficult to scale. Tusk & Quill needed a  repeatable framework  that could support thoughtful editorial content while maintaining a recognizable visual identity. To solve this, I created an editorial content system consisting of reusable templates, typography standards, graphic elements, and content structures that can be used across futur"

author: Kelsie saison
body: "Hi there! This is my submission for the #ConfigMakeathon.  You can check out the live site at  aporia.figma.site . I’ve been exploring ways to use fine art practices to deepen empathy and creativity in design. For this particular project, I focused on interface design and asked the question, “How can we rewild digital interfaces by using art?”  Challenge: Many of today's digital experiences can feel a bit clinical or automated, sometimes missing that warm, personal touch that makes technology feel human. Solution: An experimental web application built by translating physical, analog mixed-medi"

author: Ruffa Sakay
body:
"IROHA — The Closet That Remembers
A mobile-first personal wardrobe app. Built without a dev background. My first makeathon. The Problem
The average piece of clothing is worn fewer than ten times before it's discarded. Most people wear 20% of what they own, 80% of the time. The rest sits invisible — forgotten at the back of the wardrobe, waiting.
Fast fashion produces 92 million tonnes of textile waste per year. And most of that waste begins not at the factory, but at the wardrobe: overfull, un-curated, invisible.
IROHA is the quiet antidote. What IROHA is
Not a shopping app. Not algorithm-driv"

author: Madeleine Matzer
body: "Drift — Where inspiration finds you Drift is an inspiration tool built for creatives who know the feeling of scrolling Pinterest, Instagram and Dribbble for hours without finding the spark they need. Instead of searching keywords, you describe a feeling, a direction, or a half-formed idea — and Drift's Claude-powered interpretation layer translates that into a fully generated inspiration board pulling from photography, fine art (The Met Collection), typography, real quotes from books and films, color palettes and music — all in seconds. You're never locked into one result — keep pulling in new"

author: Halle Clark
body: "Cartographer: Group Planning, Mapped Fairly Cartographer is a group planning app designed to make choosing a meetup spot easier, fairer, and way less chaotic. I created it for the #configmakeathon  Instead of letting the group chat spiral with “I’m fine with anything,” “that’s too far,” or “where should we go?”, Cartographer turns the planning process into a shared map. One person creates a plan, invites their crew, and each member adds their starting location, transportation method, max travel time, budget, and preferences. From there, Cartographer helps uncover meetup spots that work best fo"

author: Leslie A
body: "Config submission: 80%, a fashion discovery tool that analyses your outfit and gives you pointers on how to improve.  My problem area: Finding clothing inspiration is very easy but the way it falls on you largely depends on proportions and other little factors. 80% would help the user through the aftermath of finding the right outfit and re-imagining what would make it better.  How it works: 80% runs through Claude to make the prediction a bit more accurate and allows you to upload or take your picture live! Try 80% here:  https://80percent.figma.site/    Community file:  https://www.figma.com"

author: Ibrahim Tejuosho
body: "I've been thinking about how we keep memories. Not the photos we take out of habit, or the messages we never look at again. The real ones. The ones that still have a feeling attached. I wanted to build something that takes those moments seriously. That's why I built Memori. It's a personal diary app designed around one idea: your memories deserve a book, not a database. You compose entries in a full-canvas editor using illustrated tool blocks, then read your diary as a flippable 3D journal with drag-to-turn pages, a key moments rail, and AI-generated art for each spread, created from your own "

author: Tiaara Jain
body: "I spent days obsessing over this and I need you to try it. Every day in history happened. But most never got a front page. Until now. Introducing The Chronicle — two experiences that will make you stop everything:        1.  The Archives — type any date from 1800 to today. Get a fully designed, historically accurate vintage newspaper front page for that exact day. The Moon Landing. The Berlin Wall falling. The day YOU were born.       2. The Daily You — type 3 boring things that happened to you today. Watch Victorian journalists completely lose their minds over your Tuesday. ""CATASTROPHIC CERE"

author: Priyanka Patel
body: "What if your family needed critical information tomorrow and couldn't reach you? Family Vault uses AI to help families organize essential information, identify preparedness gaps, and stay ready before emergencies happen. Built with Figma Make. https://notice-decay-17883734.figma.site/"

That's a project in itself. Design it, code it, host it, maintain it. Most people never do. So t"

author: Finn Gedge Gibb
body: "🎹 🎶 PadLab: an SP-404 sampler that lives in your browser I used to make beats on a Roland SP-404. Chop a sample, lay down a drum loop, play melodies over the top, resample, repeat. It's one of the most fun ways to make music there is. The problem is you need the hardware in front of you, you need the space to set it up, and if an idea hits while you're away from your gear, it's gone by the time you get back. So I built the thing I wished existed: a browser-based SP-404 that captures that exact workflow, with nothing to buy and nothing to install. Open a tab and you're making beats. The probl"

author: DAIKI
body: "I designed  Pixel Journey  🟦, a creative playground where you draw your own pixel character and then play as it. Create a character pixel by pixel, preview exactly how it looks and moves, then send it on a side-scrolling adventure through the cities where Figma has offices — from the neon nights of Tokyo to Berlin, Paris, and San Francisco, finally arriving at Figma HQ. Each city features its own sky, flag, and iconic landmarks like Mount Fuji and Big Ben 🌍 What amazed me most was how quickly an idea became reality. Using Figma Make, I turned a single prompt into a fully playable game with a"

author: Tina Smonkar
body: "I can't wake up in the morning. Like, ever. So I built an alarm app that doesn't let me snooze my way back to sleep. 😴 Meet Yawn - an alarm clock that forces you straight into your morning routine the second it fires. Jumping jacks first (motion tracked, no faking it), then timed tasks one by one until your morning is actually done. Fall back asleep mid-routine? It rings again. No mercy. Built for the @figma Config Makeathon — from FigJam sketches to Figma Make prototypes to a real working app via Figma MCP + Claude Code. The whole workflow is in the video. 👇 Still mid-flight on getting Appl"

author: Sudipto
body:
"I run a YouTube channel and I kept wanting those cute, jittery, analog-feeling doodle text title cards you see all over socials. But making them meant the iPad, or After Effects, or fiddly custom plugins too much friction every time. I also wanted it in my own handwriting, from my own hand. So for the Config Makeathon I built  scribble  — you write in the air with your finger, and your handwriting becomes a hand-drawn, animated doodle you can drop over any video. You perform it, it animates itself. Try it 👉  https://emit-cling-97840637.figma.site What it does?
→ 🖐️ Draw with your finger — pi"

author: Qianru Yang
body: "Have you ever wanted to send hidden, personal notes to friends that feel far more special than plain text? For this year’s Config Makeathon, I built  Secret Letter Workshop  — a web-based cipher workspace to write, encrypt and decode private parchment-style messages. More than a regular encryption tool, it is a fun space for people to create and share private messages together. Two ways to create your secret note 🔹 Pre-built cipher presets: 5 ready-to-use encryption algorithms. Pick one instantly to encode and decode your secret messages without setup. 🔹 Custom cipher builder: Design your un"

author: Surbhi Newaskar
body:
"Two craft traditions. One tool. Block printing — centuries-old, carved wooden stamps pressed in natural dye onto fabric. Texture hunting — pressing clay onto surfaces, lifting impressions, carrying patterns home. This is Indikon. Press real block-print motifs onto a canvas. Scan your clay impressions and ink them. Get a seamless production-ready repeat. Save everything to a living field journal. Built in Figma Make and Weave. Indikon — from sketchbook to swatch
Check out my submission for #Configmakeathon
Full screen view
 https://figma.com/make/zZk3xstsOVJBnwmuM9vXOS/Indikon-by-Surbhi?p=f&t=F"

author: Kevin Tung
body:
"Introducing Nearr 🌸 What do you do when your best friend is sick with cancer 3000 miles away? Nearr makes it easy to send local care anywhere because ""I wish I was there"" sometimes isn't enough. "Hey everyone! ✨
I designed Checkpoints thinking about all the wins that never make it to a post. Behind every big moment is a long list of smaller ones nobody sees. The ones that quietly move you forward. I wanted to build a place for those. You land, you scroll through a pixel tower built by strangers. You find one that hits — and leave a heart. Then you log your own: pick your avatar, write your win, keep it small. That's the point. Your brick lands on top. Someone will see it. Someone will get it. Not highlights. Checkpoints. "I built an experimental social media platform called Analog Likes ❤️

Today's social media platforms reduce appreciation to a binary action: Like or Not Like.

But real emotions aren't that simple.
Sometimes you like something a little.
Sometimes you love it so much that it makes you smile.

So I asked: What if Likes weren't binary?
Analog Likes lets users create their own 4 levels of appreciation using facial expressions.

First, users record four different expressions through their webcam. Then, while browsing a feed, they can express their reactions using those expressions instead of tappin"

No more scrolling through 5,000 photos to find that one memory. Instead: Tap a book → flip through your memories
Double tap → upload photos instantly
Share any book → send it to anyone, view-only Your photos finally have a home. A shelf. A story"

author: Alen Joby
body: "Hello Creatives , I Designed and Developed Ember 😑 The Problem: The High Cost of Sharing We built the most connected generation in human history, but we accidentally made vulnerability architecturally impossible. Modern social networks attach four severe costs to sharing: The Identity Cost: Your name, face, and reputation are permanently attached to your words. The Performance Cost: Metrics like followers, likes, and views turn authentic sharing into a performance. The Social Cost: Sharing with friends creates a burden of obligation; you feel guilty for ""bringing them down."" The Permanence Co"

Looking at beautiful work, it was easy to assume that the creator possessed skills I simply didn't have. BLOCKT was built to challenge that assumption. Inspired by Armin Hoffman's Exercise 47 from Graphic Design Manual: Principles and Practice, BLOCKT reduces design to its most elementary form: a single dot divided into nine parts. Users create custom stamp shapes by combining segments of that dot and use them to build artwork through experimentation, play, and dis"

body: "I built Chirp, a temporary shared room for small moments — 10-second sounds, photos, doodles, and stickers that disappear after 24 hours. Most online spaces today feel public, permanent, and performative. I wanted to make the opposite: a private, playful, low-pressure room where people can leave tiny traces together without creating another profile, feed, or archive. Anyone can create a room without logging in, share a unique link, and invite others to record short sounds, reply with audio, add photos, place stickers, or doodle on the canvas. Chirp may look like a playful canvas, but it is not"

body:
"Scribe
a self-writing clinical note What it is Scribe is a mobile web dictation app for clinicians. Instead of just recording and transcribing, it listens to a doctor dictate after a procedure and then automatically organises their words into a structured clinical note covering Procedure, Findings, Plan, and Follow up, ready to review, amend, and send for approval. It keeps the familiar, trusted dictation gesture of the physical Dictaphone but adds the things a physical device never could: live transcription, automatic structuring, an editable patient record, and a history of past dictations. author: Ash Moarfa
body:
"Most ideas don't fail. They get forgotten.

That's the real insight behind CITYDEA, something I built for Figma's Config Makeathon after one too many half-finished side projects of my own.

The concept: your ideas become buildings in a small isometric city. give them progress— a note, a photo, a voice memo, a reflection — and keep them stand still. Without attention, they decay into abandoned ruins.

It's less ""productivity app"" and more a quiet, honest record of what you actually cared about, and for how long.

Try it:  https://citydea.figma.site/  
See it in Figma Community:  https://www.fig"

body:
"Project Name: MODULO
Tagline: The Motion Synthesis Interface for Front-End Engineers. Description:
MODULO is a high-fidelity, low-code interactive playground designed to bridge the gap between motion physics and production-ready code. Built with a stark Swiss editorial aesthetic, the system maps real-time UI physics—such as Synaptic Tension, Amplitude Scale, and Vector Frequency—directly into production-ready developer tokens. By dropping any standard SVG vector asset into the engine, MODULO dynamically compiles the file's spatial parameters across four synchronized dev-output blocks: raw CSS "

Users arrange image and text layers on a simple timeline, animate them, fine-tune the easing, and export — all without a subscription or a steep learning curve. author: Elmira Demanou
body: "I built the anti-AI-companion. In 2026, therapy and companionship became the #1 use of generative AI. Meanwhile, ""how to help someone with depression"" has been the top mental-health search for ten years. And a 2026 study just confirmed: AI chatbots reduce loneliness by zero — compared to texting a real human. We're not lonely because we don't have AI to talk to. We're lonely because we don't know what to say to the people we already love. So I built Reach — a tool that does the opposite of every AI companion. Every morning, it picks one person in your life who needs you, and drafts the way in."

What they can't give you is the thinking behind it the structure, the flow, the layout logic that makes a product actually make sense. WiFrame is built for that gap. WiFrame is an AI-powered wireframe generation platform that turns raw product ideas into editable, low-fidelity wireframes for web and "

author: Diya  Bansal
body:
"Live Project Link:  https://power-mock-30949346.figma.site Project Name: Elsewhere

Problem:
Most travel apps assume you already know where you want to go. I don't — I move between countries a lot, and the way I've always found a place that fits is through feeling and activity, not city names. Search-by-name discovery just doesn't match how people actually decide where to go. Solution:
So Elsewhere works backwards. Instead of typing a destination, you build one. You drag in the experiences you want — a café, a beach, a mountain — pick an atmosphere, and the system reads what you placed against"

author: Nacho Valenz
body:
"OmniLearn — be the hero of your own story, literally OmniLearn is a body-controlled learning game for kids aged 4–7. A child stands in
front of any webcam, gets scanned, and becomes a soft-voxel 3D avatar of themselves
that they control with their body — no controller, no keyboard. They solve
educational challenges by MOVING: catching falling leaves to count (the golden ones
worth three), and crossing ""Vowel River"" by grabbing the animals whose names say the
long-A sound. THE PROBLEM
Kids this age learn by moving, not by reading — but most educational software sits

author: Vijesh Nair
body: "Video calls end. The silence after doesn't. That's the real pain in long distance, not bad communication, just the moment the call drops and the room is empty again. So instead of building another way to talk, I built a way to just be near someone. Stay is a shared surface of water for two people who aren't in the same room. Touch it, and a ripple spreads on their side, instantly, no words, no calls. Just proof someone's there. Send a photo and it surfaces on the water. Send a voice note, and they feel it arrive before they even open it. Built in Figma Make + Claude: Water rendered in WebGL, a"

author: Fawn Media
body:
"iPod Music — A Tribute to Apple's UI Philosophy 
Verified Connection App for College Students was BOND's hook; yours is nostalgia and craft. Use this as your one-liner:
A pixel-faithful recreation of the iconic iOS/iPod music player, rebuilding the UI that made a generation fall in love with digital music.
🎯 The Problem: Modern music apps have buried the magic. Cluttered home screens, algorithm-driven feeds, and busy now-playing views have replaced the calm, focused listening experience Apple's early UI pioneered, the one that made music feel like the only thing on screen.

author: Boom Tumbali
body: "LOCI — Body, Decoded. There's a moment everyone has had: something hurts, you open a search engine, and realize you don't have the words for it. You can feel it — you just can't name it. WebMD asks you to pick from a dropdown of conditions. You can't pick from a list if you don't know where to start. LOCI bridges that gap. Tap a rotatable 3D body model exactly where it hurts. Describe it in plain language. Get educational context back — what's in that area, what might be causing it, and the medical vocabulary that lets you go further. No pre-listed diagnoses. No clinical framing. Just: point, "

body: "Last Product update, had fun using figma make. Blown away by how much I got done in 4 days. My main focus today was to make a better first-time user experience. Added a How To menu, About section and a ui information screens. Also connected a database for the asset library. Now I can keep adding image & video assets without changing any code.  heres the link to the app:  https://violin-admin-22735877.figma.site   check it out and play around with it. If you have feed back leave a comment:) Thanks to Figma for putting this on and looking forward to building more with Figma Make."

author: Sandip Das
body:
"Submitting my Config Makeathon entry — Simplify 🌿 An AI document translator and simplifier built for elderly users. Inspired by my grandmother struggling to understand her bank notices and medicine labels. The app automatically detects your document type and simplifies it instantly — no login, no data saved, no prompting needed. Just scan and understand. Built using Figma Make, Figma Weave.

🔗 Live project:  https://glad-sweat-80927657.figma.site/  

📁 Figma Community file:  https://www.figma.com/community/file/1649550081362020764/simplify-because-no-one-should-struggle-with-a-document-alon"

author: Karishma Sajjad
body:
"It started with a simple truth. Every woman in Pakistan knows a street
she avoids after dark. A route she only
takes because it is busier. A moment she
wished someone knew exactly where she was. I am a UI/UX designer from Gilgit-Baltistan, currently located in Islamabad, Pakistan. And I have felt that too. So I asked myself — what if an app could
make a woman feel powerful instead of scared?
What if safety felt like a superpower? That is how SafeRoute was born. SafeRoute is not just a safety app.
It is a companion. Before every journey it asks you one
simple question — how are you feeling
righ"

author: Arshad Pathan
body: "PoolOfPics Problem After every trip, photos end up scattered across chats, cloud drives, and personal galleries. Friends keep asking for photos while memories become difficult to organize and revisit. Solution PoolOfPics is a collaborative memory-sharing platform where users can upload photos or entire albums, share them with friends, and explore memories through an interactive canvas experience. Site:  https://wavy-pollen-03434174.figma.site/ project link:  https://www.figma.com/make/sSiRtYPJ4TUhha80fSP7ww/Dashboard-UI-for-POOLofPICS?p=f&t=9Yuov0KFSAErXqjr-0 Key Features • Upload photos or co"

author: Vara Creative
body:
"Hi Guys! 👋 For #ConfigMakeathon, we built a Y2K-inspired personal card maker using only Figma ✨ It’s
a playful space where you can design your own digital card—add your 
voice, signature, stickers, and shape it however you like. Think of it 
like a tiny, expressive corner of the internet that feels uniquely you. You
can also explore cards from others, save the ones you love, and 
discover personalities beyond portfolios. It’s part experiment, part 
community idea. We
didn’t fully finish everything we imagined—but even in its current 
state, it showed us something exciting: you can recreate an"

author: alexandria lefort
body:
"would you still love me if I was a worm? 🪱💗 a tiny two-player game for long-distance couples, or friends scattered across the globe. join with a room code, customize your worms, share a picnic, fill a love meter together… then finally answer the age old question. 

In a world where everything is growing more complex and high tech, it is important to preserve whimsy in our technology, experiences and daily life. Whimsy is part of the heart of expression. Design is not dead, and neither is having fun in creating perfectly imperfect things. The joy I derive from seeing this little worm wiggle a"

author: Deepak Bhardwaj
body:
"Introducing Clauxify. Most websites don't have a traffic problem. they have a UX problem. Users visit, explore, and leave without converting. The challenge is understanding why. Clauxify is an AI-powered UX Intelligence Platform that turns any website into a detailed UX case study. Simply enter a URL and get insights into: • User journeys
• Conversion funnels
• UX & UI patterns
• Trust signals
• Competitor gaps
• Revenue opportunities
• Actionable growth recommendations Instead of generic audit reports, Clauxify helps teams understand what's blocking growth, where users experience friction, an"

author: Eduardo Maldonado
body: "Hello designers, We need to free up space in the spaceship: delete the old project files that are no longer needed. This is "" A Space Mission"" : I present a futuristic interface to remove the files taking up space on your machines. I designed and prototyped it in Figma, from sketches to the presentation video. I aimed for a minimalist, futuristic style with straight lines and no rounded corners; the ship uses dark tones so the colors of the universe stand out. Stars and asteroids are generated dynamically. For more natural interactions I considered eye tracking and hand tracking; in the end I "

author: スタジオ猫の手
body: "Neko Jinja is an interactive prototype of a hidden cat shrine where English names are reimagined as poetic Japanese kanji interpretations. The problem I explored was not only how to create a charming cultural experience, but also how to keep an AI-assisted creative project coherent across many tools, sessions, and agents. When working with AI, ideas can drift, design decisions can disappear, and new sessions often lose the original context. To solve that, I organized the project around a shared markdown context system called the World Tree. It stores the concept, visual rules, character settin"

author: Nischal masand
body:
"Built ""Multi View"" (WIP name :p) for  #ConfigMakeathon  Figma's native preview only shows one screen at a time. This plugin shows up to 4, with synced scrolling. Light vs dark, mobile vs desktop, side by side.

Designed in Figma. Built with Claude + Figma MCP.

Try it on Figma Community ( in few hours. Its under review 😉 ) →  [  https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/1649652335482980228  ]"

author: Vivian Belosky
body: "🌟 It's a familiar problem statement: everyone feels disconnected from eachother, loneliness epidemics abound, relationship therapists talk about 'bids for connection' - and of course the paradox is it has never been easier to do what often seems impossible in a hustley, bustley, and high-tech world: make plans.  Let's Hang Out offers a way to make those 'bids for connection'  without the pressure of playing text-tag to see if anyone wants to do that thing you've been looking forward to only to be disappointed, or having to get a new hobby- though of course you could use this for that.  🌟 Bui"

author: Andrii Pavuk
body: "👋 Hi everyone! For #ConfigMakeathon, I built Node — an AI-powered goal planning experience that turns ideas into structured action plans. The project started as a few static screens in Figma. What surprised me most was how quickly Figma Make helped transform them into an interactive prototype with navigation, onboarding flows, progress tracking, specialists, and personalized blueprints. I also experimented with using AI to help shape the visual direction and brand identity, not just the UI itself. The result isn’t perfect, but it gave me a glimpse of what the future design workflow could look"

author: Isabella De Cuppis
body: "There's a moment every designer knows. You've been staring at the same file for three days and you can't see it anymore. You need someone to tell you the truth, but everyone around you is either too polite or too busy. The signs were always there. Ask the universe. ✨ Tarot critique is the Major Arcana, reread for designers. You paste your portfolio URL, turn the cards, and somewhere between a few centuries of borrowed wisdom and the thing you already knew but wouldn't admit, the reading finds you. Not your love life. Your work. The hierarchy that isn't working, the story you're not telling, th"

author: Rashida Abdul-rashid
body: "Someone left this behind. I built TRACES for the #ConfigMakeathon. It started as an archive of places and their history, but along the way I realized something: places don't just hold history, they hold memories, stories, and local knowledge too. So, I redesigned the experience around a simple interaction: Open a trace → Read a trace → Leave a trace. Every trace becomes part of a growing collection of human experiences attached to places around the world. Built with Figma Make. 🔗  https://jungle-agenda-80411862.figma.site/ @Figma @contra #ConfigMakeathon #Figma #FigmaMake"

author: Alexa Harrison
body: "I ran out of AI credits, but I still want to submit my work!: This a (very) rough draft of a concept to support decision making, and hope building through structured reflections and exercises.   I've noticed that people already turn to LLMs for life decisions, but often walk away with an answer and an vague understanding of why they got that result. I want users to leave my app feeling more confident, not just in what to decide, but in understanding themselves and what comes with the journey of pursuing their dreams. Yes, it's missing the beautiful UI and clean transitions and a lot more posit"

author: Deepakshi Varma
body:
"What if cleaning a dirty street got you free food? That's Saaf. Designed and prototyped in 5 days for Figma Config 2026 fully AI-built using Claude MCP and Figma Make. Before/after photo verification, real rewards, India-first. 🌿

Figma Make:  https://www.figma.com/make/Iqd7ysgHpvJxC4LMZzLFkg/Saaf-Civic-Cleanliness-App?t=o3fsA81N4waKideR-1  

Documentation:  https://deepakshi-varma.github.io/saaf/"

author: Marleen Phangestu
body: "this year I moved away from everyone I love. and on my birthday, I felt it. so I built something about it. 🥹 Wish U Here — a birthday experience where your friends' videos hang like silk panels in a gallery you can walk through. feel their presence, hear their voice, maybe cry a little 🥹😭🥺 built with Figma Make for Config Makeathon 2026. I explored Figma Weave for the 360° room backgrounds... ended up pivoting to a gallery concept instead. sometimes the process teaches you more than the final product. happy birthday to everyone who's ever celebrated alone. this one's for you. 🎂 #ConfigMak"

author: Washington Teran
body: "Welcome to Brick World:  Welcome to Brick World, a cozy sandbox building experience for LEGO enthusiasts and anyone who loves creative construction games. Build anything you can imagine and do not limit yourself to castles. Create towers, villages, bridges, forts, and floating islands; the limit is your own imagination. 🚀  Key features - Browser-based 3D building - Fast, satisfying snapping (stud/grid snapping tuned for speed and feel) - Starter brick palette with classic shapes, slopes, arches, and castle pieces - Build tools: place, move, rotate, delete, multi-select + group, undo/redo - Cu"

author: Alister Felix
body:
"I've always had way too many ideas and no good way to take care of them... so I built this:  IDEA GALAXY Idea Galaxy turns your ideas into living flowers on your own little planet. You plant an idea, nurture it with design-thinking prompts, and watch it bloom into something unique. Every flower is procedurally generated from how you think. No two are ever the same. - Plant your idea as a seed in your own personal planet
- Nurture it! answer prompts across Empathize, Define, Ideate, Prototype, and Test.  There are pre-populated answers to make it easier.
- Watch it bloom with your thinking shap"

author: Shahroz Ahmad
body:
"The Neural Internet
 https://internet.figma.site A Living Network for Human Understanding The internet was built to organise pages. The Neural Internet reimagines it as a living intelligence that organises understanding. Instead of returning links or conversations, every topic becomes an interactive universe where users can explore what it is, why it exists, how it works, what it connects to, what it influenced, and what it may become in the future. The knowledge graph never stays static. It continuously evolves, generates new interdisciplinary fields, visualises causality, simulates complex s"

body:
"finally cooked some fire iterations using chatgpt 

moodweave is starting to look so much better, but I can’t implement them cause figma ai credits already ran out 💀"

author: Denis Gulyaikin
body: "I built Marquise for the Config Makeathon. A simple browser-based tool for creating animated text on SVG paths. Marquee animations are usually boring straight lines. Marquise solves that — you draw a path, choose a font, enter text, set speed and get export-ready SVG animation code instantly. Build with: → Figma Make for the full UI and app logic → Supabase for the template gallery backend → Figma MCP dev mode for design-to-code context 🔗 Live project:  https://marquee-tool.figma.site/ Figma   Contra  #ConfigMakeathon #FigmaMake"

author: S M Saad Uddin
body:
"Plot twist: I am a complete beginner at UI design, but I wanted to build a hyper-premium geospatial operations platform anyway. 🚀
Lol, map platform maps. Hehe.         I wrestled with pure  HeroUI  components then switched to  Coss UI , commanded an AI to build an infinite spatial canvas, and integrated a Bring-Your-Own-Key AI chat assistant. If you click something and it breaks, that's just a feature & I didn't have enough credits. 😅 Explore the Cirkul Map prototype here:
🔗  https://cirkulmap.figma.site/"

author: Halle Clark
body:
"HomeBase is an AI-powered home memory system that helps homeowners organize, track, and maintain everything in their home from one place. Key Features
📸 Smart Scan — Scan appliances, systems, labels, paint colors, documents, and household items.
🏠 Room-Based Organization — Organize everything by room for quick access.
📄 Document Vault — Store manuals, warranties, receipts, inspection reports, and insurance documents, and note which room you keep the physical copies for when the need arises. 
🔔 Maintenance Tracking — Keep track of maintenance history and upcoming service reminders.
🗺️ Home"

author: Andres Sandrea
body:
"I designed a mobile app for dog parents called Pawwsitive. 🐾

It helps you keep track of your dog’s health, activity, location, medication, and training — all in one place.

The idea came from wanting pet care to feel less scattered and more connected. Something that gives owners a clearer picture of their dog’s day-to-day life, while also making room for support, learning, and community.

My favorite part is how it turns everyday care into something simple, visual, and a little more human.

Prototype link:  https://www.figma.com/proto/nv1yA4NXDjy28MXkpoN1ik/Pawwsitvive_Config_Makeathon2026?n"

author: Kairul F.
body:
"Just shipped MoodWeave for #configmakeathon Turn your feelings into healing character visuals. Built entirely in Figma using Make + Weave in under 10 hours. Problem: Journaling feels boring and hard to express emotions. Solution: Mood input → cute therapeutic character + shareable card. Live demo:  https://garnet-wager-16485773.figma.site  
Figma file:  https://www.figma.com/make/Yjy9hev3aLw750u88uwOJ9/-Makeathon--MoodWeave-App?t=D5S1HsMtaW5aIcZC-1

author: Jan Poupa
body:
"Glimmer – Help you, help others.
Glimmer is a gentle way to shift how you feel. A bad day, a flat afternoon, a restless evening, or just a free five minutes: you pick how you'd like to feel, and it hands you one tiny, doable thing to get you a little closer. Knowing that doing something would help is not the hard part. It's getting yourself to start, and Glimmer makes starting easy.

The idea 
Tell Glimmer what you want to feel (calm, joy, connection, confidence, energy, or creativity) and how much of an effort you’re willing to make: mini, modest, or mighty. It gives you a spark, one small th"

author: Manya Kaushik
body: "What if the internet left stamps instead of comments? Stamp Studio is a digital playground inspired by the joy of stamping, collecting, and sharing. Create a custom stamp that captures a feeling, memory, idea, or identity, then press it onto paper-like canvases with realistic textures and imperfections. But your stamp doesn’t stop with you. Every creation becomes part of a living Community Stamp Wall - a global collection of stories, emotions, and moments left behind by people around the world. Create stamps. Explore marks. Share postcards. Because some ideas deserve more than a post. They des"

author: Mithradevi   T
body:
"For the last few weeks, I've been building Dotlet for the Config Makeathon. Start here: 
🔗 Try Dotlet:  link

That's a real barrier — for students, founders, creators, anyone with an idea but no formal design training. So I built a different approach: instead of drawing paths, you place dots on a grid. They merge into sh"

author: Asaad Mahmood
body: "I built a reading game for my son. It's called  Dino Words. He's four, and he's obsessed with dinosaurs. He's also just starting to read. So I made him something where those two things become the same thing. You see a picture. You say the word out loud. A dinosaur is chasing you, and your voice is the only thing keeping it back. Say it right and it falls behind. Get stuck and it creeps closer. Teaching language is usually taught with drills and flashcards, things kids endure. But my son doesn't endure this. He doesn't know he's learning at all. He thinks he's outrunning a dinosaur, and somewhe"

author: Adriana Pérez
body: "What a thrill — it's my first time at the Makeathon! 🚂 I joined the challenge a little late, but I worked hard to finish my project: Cozy Night Train (No es mucho pero es trabajo honesto :p ) I always use pomodoro and ambient music to focus, but this time I wanted to create something of my own. It has switchable modes, an interactive radio, and — to get creative — a typewriter to jot down your tasks!  I built the whole thing with Figma Make, and also used Weave and the Figma MCP so I wouldn't burn through all my Make credits too fast haha. Take a ride 👉  https://gauge-tarot-09304731.figma.si"

author: Bharat Nisar
body:
"Design Arcana is my submission for the @Figma #ConfigMakeathon. The idea started with a simple question: How can starter designers uncover their blind spots before they become design mistakes? Design Arcana is a tarot-inspired reflection experience that helps designers explore assumptions, challenges, and opportunities hidden within their work. Instead of predicting the future, each card reveals design principles, blind spots, reflection prompts, and actionable next steps. Built with:
✨ Figma Make for the interactive experience, flows, and prototyping
🎨 Figma Weave for generating and explorin"

author: Michal Giernatowski
body: "NOTALONE - a living, anonymous map of feelings. You type the one feeling you can't say out loud - ""I feel alone"" - and press share. It doesn't vanish. It becomes a light on a glass globe, pinned near your city, and it stays. Around it, hundreds of other lights - each a real person who feels the same. You drift across the map and read them: no names, no profiles, no likes. Just feelings, held in one place. Loneliness isn't a lack of people. It's the belief that no one feels what you feel. This answers that - instantly, with proof you can see.  How we built it: our design system became a Figma S"

author: ANJALI VISHNU
body: "My idea was born on a recent rainy day with terrible network. As reels struggled to load, the pauses between every scroll were filled with my mom reminding me constantly of my pending chores. Normally, those reminders blend into the endless doomscroll, but the buffering gave them space to be heard. I realized that the few seconds of interruption was really enough for me to take my mind off of the loop and get to work! That led to a speculative question: What if social media used intentional intervention to remind us of what truly matters to us beyond the screens? Presenting to you- CURB; your "

author: Agastya Khati
body: "Built Pixel Mosaic for #ConfigMakeathon Pixel Mosaic is a collaborative pixel-art world where every visitor gets a permanent tile on a shared infinite canvas. Users can create pixel art, publish it to the community, discover artwork from others, and collect their favorite creations as reusable stamps using a custom Stamp Cutter system. How I built it with Figma: I started with a simple prompt in Figma Make and iterated entirely through conversation. I refined the UI, redesigned the canvas experience, added persistent user tiles, built a collectible Stamp Book system, integrated custom animatio"

author: Angela Andreea Paralescu
body: "Idea is a digital and physical product for sharing and gifting memories. Audio memories. And what better way than a vinyl style pin. I used my entire subscription credits to build it. I will continue building on this idea and make it a market ready product but for now this is what I have, i hope you will enjoy it. The next editing will happen in cursor and claude.  https://foyer-polka-75299867.figma.site"

author: Devang Patel
body: "Designing Better Days: Presenting ""Nail Your Day"" 🎯Nail Your Day redefines daily planning by turning schedule management into a highly visual, drag-and-drop interactive canvas inside Figma. Key Elements: Visual Timeline: Map out energy levels and tasks side-by-side. Frictionless UI: Designed to keep creatives in their flow state without app-fatigue. Built for Teams: Highly componentized and ready for collaborative syncing. Check out the preview and let me know your thoughts!"

author: Tabarka Atiq
body:
"Excited to share my submission for the #configMakeathon 🌟 
From a random spark of an idea to a fully functional cosmic interface, I’ve built ""StarScript"".  It’s an interactive journey that merges a user’s identity with a unique 'Cosmic Constellation' through dynamic design and robust backend integration. 🔗Live Project Link:  https://jury-bonus-39443593.figma.site/  Key Features: Unique Constellation Generation: Every letter of a user's name becomes a living star arranged into a unique constellation.  Personality Codex: An interactive feature where users can click on stars to reveal personali"

author: Hanna Savostianova
body:
"Design Crit — the full story (problem → solution → what I used) For the Config Makeathon I built Design Crit — a feedback space for designers who work without a team. The problem 🩶

You want feedback on a design — but it's too rough to post. Sharing unfinished work feels like posting a mistake. Dribbble is a gallery, not a crit. Friends say ""looks great."" AI checks your spacing but can't read intent. So the work that needs eyes the most gets none — and solo designers, freelancers and juniors are left guessing alone. The solution ✦

Design Crit lets you post unfinished work with one clear ques"

author: Ashley Phillips
body:
"Howdy!
 CookieDraft  is a planning studio for cottage bakers. It was built end-to-end for Configmakeathon. The user can design a cookie, get the exact icing and dye amounts, and walk into the store or start decorating with a print-ready spec. The problem: 
Cottage bakers have a lot going on, and planning in a pinch is sometimes difficult for those last-minute orders. They mix too much icing, buy a color that dries wrong, or sometimes freehand a batch, hoping cookie ten looks right next to cookie one. It wastes money and time on every order.

The solution:
  CookieDraft  turns your design into "

author: Samuel Adediran
body: "Proud to share my submission for the #ConfigMakeathon 🚀 For this challenge, I created The Last Archive which is an interactive speculative experience that allows visitors to travel across the complete timeline of humanity, from the present day to the final surviving memory of civilization 100 trillion years in the future. The idea began with a question: If humanity disappeared, what would be the last thing we leave behind? Rather than building a traditional website, I wanted to create somewhat a  machine. A retro-futuristic archival system inspired by NASA mission control, CRT terminals, scie"

author: Willow Munaba
body:
"Ever wished you could learn how to use design tools without getting bored? 

   and I built a side-scrolling puzzle experience called ""BezierDrive"" that throws you into a cartoon obstacle course where the only way forward is to use the design tools. 

And before you know it, you've mastered the basics of design. 

We utilized Figma's suite of products including Figma make, Figma MCP, FigJam and Figma Design to create this experience just for you!


Try it out for yourself using the link below:
 https://dull-pogo-12811982.figma.site/  


[warning: shouting]"

author: ANTHONIA OJO
body: "Project Name: FitThread mobile App Problem Independent fashion designers often manage client consultations, measurements, and orders across multiple disconnected tools such as WhatsApp, Instagram, spreadsheets, and handwritten notes. This creates inefficiencies, measurement errors, and an unprofessional client experience. Solution FitThread is a mobile-first platform that centralizes the bespoke fashion workflow. Designers can invite clients, collect accurate body measurements remotely, schedule consultations, track order progress, and manage client relationships from a single workspace. Built"

author: Isabella Guran
body:
"The Excuse Generator — a delightfully absurd machine that gives you a wonderfully specific excuse for any situation. Built in Figma Make, this one is just for fun. Live app:  https://offset-chorus-99223099.figma.site  
Figma Community:  https://www.figma.com/community/file/1649477945985579753  
Posted to social:  https://x.com/isabellaguran/status/2067650896847052851?s=20"

author: Jan Slabihoudek
body: "Cumulus is a small space for noticing. Most digital products are designed to help us move faster, do more, and stay productive. Cumulus explores the opposite idea. It creates a moment of pause instead of asking for more attention. Inspired by the simple act of watching clouds, Cumulus invites people to slow down, look up, and let their minds wander. Users are shown a photograph of a cloud and asked one simple question: What do you see? They share their interpretation and discover how other people perceived the very same image. There are no accounts, no feeds, no scores, and no streaks to maint"

author: Rishi T
body:
"Your mirror has been lying to you. It's been looking at you every day for years. It knows exactly how good or bad your outfit looks and yet it says absolutely nothing. So I built one that finally talks back. Meet MIROR. 
A digital mirror that gives men an honest style verdict before they walk out the door. 1. Upload a photo.
2. Choose the occasion.
3. Get the truth - SHARP. CLOSE. NOT YET. The idea started with a simple observation: Every man stands in front of a mirror before leaving the house and asks the same question: ""Do I look good?"" The mirror reflects.It never responds. 

But MIROR res"

author: Domenico Amalfitano
body: "Built Nelio, a browser-based node-based image and video editor created with Figma. https://nelio.figma.site/   The project explores how visual programming can make creative workflows more accessible by letting users build editing pipelines through simple node connections instead of traditional timelines and menus. Using Figma Design, Figma Weave, and Figma Make, I designed the visual identity, prototyped the experience, and built a fully functional web application. Nelio includes real-time image and video processing, effects, LUTs, animation controls, blend modes, reusable node packages, and b"

author: Waiphop Jantharatwong
body:
"LATENCY  — some signals take time.

We send everything instantly now. I wanted to build something that brings back the meaning of waiting. It's called LATENCY. 🚀 A signal in space takes real time to cross the distance — light, radio, a spacecraft, all bound by it. I turned that delay into a way to send messages. You write a letter and send it to a planet. It travels in real time. You share the link, and the person on the other end can't open it early — they watch it travel, and read it only when it lands. The Moon takes minutes. Neptune takes a day. Write to yourself at the end of the day. Se"

author: Ann Karanja
body: "MindMirror Millions of people are expected to design a future they cannot see. We choose careers, make life-changing decisions, and abandon dreams while carrying one question in our minds: ""What if I fail?"" The people who need guidance most often have the least access to it. Career counselling is expensive. Professional networks are limited. Many young people are left to figure everything out alone. We built MindMirror to change that. MindMirror is a product that helps people meet a possible version of their future selves; before becoming them. Users choose a dream career, answer five question"

author: Susanna Anese
body: "For the #ConfigMakeathon I built The Invisible Load 🩶 I had used Figma before, but building something interactive with it felt like a completely different challenge. I saw the Makeathon very last minute and decided to give it a go. 💭 The problem So many people know they feel overwhelmed, but cannot explain what they are actually carrying. It is the noticing. The remembering. The checking. The planning. The emotional weight of keeping things moving when no one else can see the full picture. Because that load often stays unnamed, it can feel personal, isolating, or hard to explain. ✨ The idea "

author: Emmanuel  Bliss
body: "I've spent an entire day in a hospital waiting to see a doctor. Not because treatment wasn't available. But because I had no idea what was happening. No updates. No visibility into where I was in the queue. No indication of whether I'd be attended to in 10 minutes or 5 hours. Just waiting. That experience stayed with me long before I became a product designer. Years later, while working on the Config Makeathon, I found myself returning to that moment. Because despite all the technology around us, one thing still feels broken. We can track our food deliveries. We can track our rides. We can tra"

author: Ebenezer Adjei-Sah
body: "Hi creatives! 👋 For #ConfigMakeathon, I created Ripple Reflect — a calming multiplayer reflection space designed for caregivers such as teachers, nurses, doctors, counsellors, home carers, and firefighters, who often carry a heavy emotional load but rarely have a place to pause and be heard. Every visitor can anonymously share a thought, emotion, or small win. Each reflection becomes a ripple in a shared digital water space, blending with others to create a quiet sense of connection and collective calm. The water and ripple metaphor was intentionally chosen to show how even the smallest act o"

author: Laura Maria Lisjak Hoffmann
body: "Hey everyone, excited to be joining the Config Makeathon ☺️ The idea for Dear Diary came from a simple feeling: sometimes you have something on your mind, whether it's the best day of your life, a bad day, or just a random thought, and you don't have anyone to share it with. Dear Diary is an anonymous shared diary where people can write about their day and read what others are experiencing around the world. You can write one entry per day and receive a random flower as a reward. As you keep writing, you collect flowers and after 7 days unlock a bouquet that you can share with others. You can a"

author: Florin Grunder
body:
"I built a multiplayer surrealist drawing game with Figma Make.

The Exquisite Corpse is a 100-year-old game where each player draws their section of a figure in secret. Nobody sees the full picture until the reveal. Head, middle, legs. Strangers become collaborators. The result is always unexpected. I rebuilt it for the web real-time, multiplayer, ready to play in 30 seconds. Here's how Figma Make changed my process: I started with a design prototype in Figma. Then I prompted Figma Make to turn it into a working codebase and it did. What used to take days of scaffolding took hours. From there "

author: Dorcas Ajayi
body: "You can be surrounded by people and still carry something you've never said out loud. Something you want to say, but not if anyone knows it was you. 🤍 For #ConfigMakeathon, I built Felt →  https://felt.figma.site/   You type one honest sentence. ""its been a hard week."" ""i almost didn't submit, but I did."" Your words turn into a piece of living art, ink spreading through water, colored by the feeling behind them. Every one is different, because it's made from exactly what you said. Then the part I can't stop thinking about is the moment your feeling appears, Felt shows you you're not alone. So"

🎨 All the room assets and the cat "

I wanted to strip away the clinical coldness of modern software and create something beautifully simple that could be truly, deeply helpful on someone's hardest days "

author: Daniel Onadipe
body: "Tethered Outpost: Benefits without borders Remote work has crossed borders, but employee benefits have not. Tethered Outpost is a stablecoin-powered benefits platform designed for distributed teams. It helps companies fund category-based benefit wallets for remote employees, who can then redeem those benefits with verified local providers in their own countries. The project follows Amara, a remote software engineer in Lagos, as she receives an employer-funded Health benefit, discovers a verified local clinic, and unlocks a full health screening without paying out of pocket. author: Debashis Sarker
body:
"I am excited to share my submission for #ConfigMakeathon

Retrokey – A Human-First Approach to Digital Security

Keys secure our physical lives, but passwords secure our entire digital existence. Yet, humanity is losing the battle of digital security. 
Today, over 80% of hacking-related breaches are tied to weak or reused passwords. creating and managing passwords remains one of the most frustrating experiences. Most people don't think in random strings of characters. They think in memories, stories, words, names, and moments that matter to them. Yet they're expected to create complex password"

author: Neelanjana Basu
body:
"Decision Gym is a gym for your judgment. We make our biggest calls on gut feel, then never learn if we were right. Decision Gym changes that. You lock in your gut and your confidence, estimate the payoffs, and it shows you the expected-value math, a clear recommendation, and the bias behind your call. Over time, it tracks how calibrated your judgment becomes. Built end to end in Figma. Written as a spec, prompted into Figma Make, refined in chat, published as a live site. No code by hand. Generation is cheap now. Judgment is the edge.

Live app:  https://flower-plugin-92759538.figma.site

author: Marco Ponce de León
body:
"DUMBSCROLL What problem am I solving?

In 2004, the average person could focus on a screen for two and a half minutes. Today it's 47 seconds. We touch our phones 228 times a day, 10 seconds each. We don't have a screen-time problem — we have an attention problem. Most apps fight this by blocking or lecturing you, which just makes you close them. The Idea

Dumbscroll is an app that sits on top of any feed you already use — TikTok, Instagram, X. The moment it detects you're doomscrolling (skipping past content faster than your set attention threshold), it starts pixelating the feed. The more you"

author: Axel Jeremy
body: "I built a sleep aid disguised as a game. It's called driftshuffle. I couldn't sleep the night a friend told me about this Makeathon, so I built the thing I needed at 3am, an auto-battler that's secretly a wind-down tool, built on cognitive shuffling (a published technique by Dr. Luc Beaudoin). The random calm creatures you watch are the actual science; they quiet a racing mind. No score, no winning, even losing (fallen asleep mid-game) is a winning. On the build: I made it solo entirely in Figma Make, hand-tuning the code myself, the swarm logic, the fade timings, the deliberate slow-and-dim p"

author: Virat Mahant
body:
"Introducing my Project
HALF-LIFE — the interface that forgets, so you remember. We made everything permanent. 41,372 photos none of us ever open again. When nothing can be lost, nothing feels precious. HALF-LIFE inverts that. It's a memory keeper where every memory decays on screen exactly the way human memory does — along the Ebbinghaus forgetting curve. Neglect a memory and it softens, drains of color, and fragments into an impressionist ghost. Return to it, retell what you remember now, and it re-develops like a photograph — gaining a new sediment layer each time. Nothing is ever deleted. B"

body:
"The Cartoon Company

A comic strip being written by the entire world. One panel at a time. — Browse the strip and read the story so far

— Build your panel using character stamps and speech bubbles

— Add it to the strip permanently

— S"

The idea came from seeing many founders spend weeks researching names, creating branding, studying competitors, and preparing launch materials before they can even begin building. FounderFlow brings these steps together in one simple experience. Users can describe their business idea in a few sentences and receive business name suggestions, branding concepts, landing page ideas, competitor insights, marketing strategies, and a starter pitch deck outline. The platform is des"

author: Hritik Pawar
body:
"For #ConfigMakeathon, I created  ""Meow Board"" 🐾 😼 

 meow-board.figma.site

 It's a drawing board where a judgmental-but-loving cat challenges you to sketch then teaches you to draw better by showing you how, stroke by stroke.

Pick a subject, draw it your way, get a paw rating (never less than 3 the cat is supportive, not cruel 😼), then watch the cat draw it back beautifully so you can trace along and actually improve. Earn a 10/10 and your sketch gets hung in the Meow Museum.

It's designed so kids (and grown-ups who swear they ""can't draw"") can build real interest in art through play ins"

author: Shanif Khan
body: "The Archive of Almost Every possibility leaves a ripple. The Archive of Almost is an interactive storytelling experience built with #FigmaMake. It explores the moments that almost happened—the train never boarded, the message left unsent, the conversation that never began—and reimagines them not as regrets, but as possibilities worthy of preservation. Visitors enter a dreamlike sanctuary where forgotten possibilities are preserved as living fragments of light. Through unfolding stories, ripples, and reflection, they discover that even the smallest moments can quietly shape who we become. Rathe"

author: GAURI KALE
body:
"Walk With Me —> Real-Time Comfort Routing
Every navigation app tells you the fastest route. But what about the safest one?
Walk With Me is a real-time comfort router built for anyone who walks alone. It scores every route by lighting, footpaths, and crowd levels and is updated live by the community. Share your walk with a trusted contact, report road hazards in real time, and trigger an instant SOS if you ever need help.
Built for pedestrians in India, starting with Nagpur, because this is where the idea was born.
Check it out here - 
🔗  https://lnkd.in/dMUJBcgh

 The idea: Growing up, my mum"

author: Quin Chan
body:
"Hey everyone, I'm Quin. I'd like to share a small Figma plugin I built called FroQ Reminder — a simple tool that reminds designers to drink water and take stretch breaks during long work sessions. Creative flow is important, but so is health. The plugin provides customizable reminders, simple completion tracking, and lightweight statistics to help users build healthier habits without disrupting their workflow. 

Designed for people who are so focused on creating great work that they sometimes forget to take care of themselves. 🔥 😍 Thank you

Figma Community:
 https://www.figma.com/community/"

author: Isidoros Manolas
body:
"Deltos is a mobile-first community contribution ledger that replaces
market prices with real-cost tracking. Credits are earned through labor hours and materials contributed, and
deducted for ecological costs — energy (kWh) and CO₂ (kg). A 10% weekly
decay prevents accumulation, keeping the economy circular rather than
extractive. Credits are non-transferable by design: they measure
contribution to the community, not purchasing power. Built for neighborhoods and local nodes, Deltos includes contribution
logging, a live decay chart, on-device ID verification (documents never
leave the phone), a "

author: Kevin Kwok
body:
"I made an app where you turn your days into flames. It's called Amber. I wanted something that captures the intensity of a day instead of scoring it, a quiet day isn't a failed day, it's just a quiet one. One question: How did your day go? You slide to the flame that matches, add a note if you feel like it, done in about fifteen seconds. A month later your calendar is a grid of flames you can actually feel. On the build: I made all five flame states in Figma Weave, and I designed every screen myself in Figma and handed it to Make through MCP. Try Amber:  https://amber-app.figma.site

body:
"TasteBud — AI-native zero-waste cooking app THE PROBLEM
A third of all food gets wasted. TasteBud tackles this by turning

Scan your fridge, pantry and fresh produce: the AI detects every

one tap reshapes the entire app to match who you are: ⚡ Student Rush — fast, cheap, minimal equipment

body: "Built entirely in FigmaMake. No external IDE. No hand-written code. Just prompts, vision, and a refusal to stay boring. author: Muhamad Aldi Ramdani
body: "RUNNER HUB PRO — Performance Management System for Runners PROBLEM: Latihan lari tanpa sistem? Pace inkonsisten, tidur kurang diabaikan, injury datang tiba-tiba, dan target race gagal tercapai. Kebanyakan runner hanya fokus volume — tapi performa = biometrik + recovery + fuel optimization. SOLUTION: Runner Hub Pro adalah HUD-style performance dashboard yang mengintegrasikan cortisol monitoring, VO2 Max modeling berbasis Pace/HR ratio, dan auto-intensity adjustment. Sistem interrupt otomatis aktif saat sleep debt terdeteksi — melindungi kamu dari overtraining sambil tetap building aerobic base "

author: Sabik Biswas
body:
"Lens — never sign what you can't read.

My submission for the Figma Config Makeathon. You sign leases you didn't fully read. Pay bills you couldn't really check. Open lab results and can't tell if the number is bad. It's not you — the documents that decide your money, your home, and your health are built to be unreadable, with the parts that can hurt you buried where you'd never find them. Lens reads them back to you. Drop in any document and it knows what it's looking at, reads every line, and shows you exactly what matters — and what to do about it. Bills — ""Am I being overcharged?"" On a $16"

author: Soubhagya Roy
body: "Smart homes are getting smarter, but understanding what is actually happening inside them is still surprisingly difficult. Devices live in separate apps, automations feel invisible, and managing everything often requires digging through menus and settings. HomeSphere was created to change that. Instead of treating a home as a list of devices, HomeSphere turns it into a living digital twin. Users can upload photos of their rooms, generate an interactive isometric version of their home, explore connected devices in context, and see exactly how automations flow between rooms, sensors, and actions"

body: "Project Name: Taste Test Tagline : A personality test for your design taste. Sixteen questions reveal the archetype behind your design philosophy — and hand you a portable  taste.md  to take into your projects. Problem Statement: Everyone's talking about taste right now but no one knows how to use it/make it tangible...  Taste Test turns the conversation into something practical. You answer sixteen questions framed as planning a birthday party. From those choices it reads your aesthetic and places you in one of six archetypes: the Architect, the Maximalist, the Purist, the Composer, the Invent"

author: Osita Miles
body: "Recruiting is often fragmented across inboxes, spreadsheets, ATS tools, calendars, sourcing platforms, LinkedIn, and disconnected candidate notes. Important context gets lost, follow-ups slip through gaps, and recruiters spend too much time carrying information from one system to another. For lean hiring teams, this problem is even more pronounced. A founder may be the hiring manager, sourcer, recruiter, scheduler, and follow-up owner at the same time. They may know exactly who they need to hire, but every new search starts cold and the pipeline rarely has the chance to compound. Jobraker Recr"

author: John Freund
body: "Link: Working File-  https://www.figma.com/make/hymnP9Wz7O4nMy1eivvYra/Create-storyboard?t=kYXgdkHvFol6Sgd4-1 Link Live File link-  https://www.figma.com/make/hymnP9Wz7O4nMy1eivvYra/Create-storyboard?t=kYXgdkHvFol6Sgd4-20&fullscreen=1 HOMEIQ is an AI-powered home repair assessment app that helps homeowners instantly determine whether damage is a safe DIY fix or requires urgent professional help. Built entirely with Figma Make, the project includes a full prototype storyboard, camera scan flow, AI analysis screen, dual-outcome results, and a brand standards guide — all in one working file"

author: Smriti Kinra
body: "Most people prompt AI like they're texting a friend - vague, iterative, back-and-forth until it's close enough. Every one of those follow-ups burns real compute, real electricity, real water. PromptShot is a daily Wordle-style game that fixes that. You're shown an AI-generated output: a paragraph, a list, a code snippet, and you have to reverse-engineer the single prompt that produced it. One shot. Scored out of 100 on accuracy, format, and brevity. While you wait for your score, you see exactly how much water your query used in a data center. That number is the point. Better prompts = less AI"

author: Kaley
body: "One week of experimenting and using Figma Make, my partner and I are proud to announce Open Seat. Everyone has found themselves alone in public before.  Maybe you're at the gym and you want a spotter. Maybe you find yourself alone at a bar, in the mood to meet new people. Maybe you're on campus and wouldn't mind having company to study with. Either way, it's hard to find connection when you're on-the-go and you're not sure who to approach. Open Seat removes the guesswork and makes those intentions visible. You get to create temporary signals for what kinds of social interaction you're open to "

author: Maria Kimberly Furtado
body: "Hat Tip: a wisdom dispenser for the many hats we wear. We all seek advice as we change careers, start new chapters, and figure things out. The Last Page is a playful, creative tool that lets users draw a doodle, animate it with simple behaviours, and turn it into a wallpaper, screensaver, GIF, or video. author: Garv Gupta
body: "Neurotrace - A Cognitive Care Companion for Dementia Patients Every three seconds, someone develops dementia. 55 million people worldwide. No cure. The only proven defense is consistent, gentle cognitive engagement - but every tool out there feels like a clinical exam or a children's game. Patients quit within weeks. Neurotrace is a cognitive care companion that doesn't feel like a tool. It feels like tending a garden. Every design decision - the warm tones, the botanical imagery, the soft typography was tested with real patients and caregivers. This is what they felt safe coming back to every"

author: Pratik Makhija
body:
"one layout that fits every language, Clamped.
 
Most websites break when the language changes, German overflows buttons, Arabic needs RTL, Japanese and Hindi shift line height. So teams build separate layouts per language. Clamped fixes this with elastic ratios instead of fixed pixels: one layout that re-moulds itself to fit any language or script no duplicate designs, no broken UI. Built in Figma Make, extended with Figma MCP. Bring-your-own-key translation lets you add any language live and export the production code."

author: Saowalak Whitley
body:
"Hi everyone, I'm so exited to share my Config Makeathon project: Introducing "" Alistly ""

It's like a little command center for apartment hunting. Helps you keep your apartment hunt organized, collaborated and way less chaotic. Instead of drowning in random links, screenshots, notes, group chats, and “wait, which one had the good bathroom?” moments, Alistly keeps everything in one place. You can make lists and save apartments with just one tap using AI. You can track and update each place’s status, favorite the good ones, write notes, and rate apartments based on what actually matters to you. "

author: Shreya Khettry
body: "Perspective 📖 Overview Every designer naturally reviews user interfaces through a single, biased lens: their own. Accessibility is routinely treated as a final checklist rather than an integrated part of the design process. Designers typically lack quick, intuitive ways to understand how their UI behaves under real-world constraints, making testing fragmented, manual, and easy to deprioritize. Perspective transforms compliance evaluation into an active, empathetic simulation. By letting creators drop any UI screen straight into the web app, the platform maps real-world constraints directly on"

author: AMAN ULLAH
body:
"Title: Modern Business Card Design Description:

I designed a clean and professional business card using Figma for the Config Makeathon challenge. The design focuses on simplicity, readability, and a modern layout suitable for personal branding. I used a minimal color palette, balanced typography, and proper spacing to create a strong visual hierarchy. The goal was to make a card that feels modern, professional, and easy to read at first glance. This design is suitable for freelancers, students, and professionals who want a clear and modern identity."

author: Elvis Owino
body:
"Project Name: TrustOS The portable trust layer for the internet.

TrustOS is the missing infrastructure for the internet — portable trust. Just as Stripe solved payments and Plaid solved financial connectivity, TrustOS solves the question every transaction depends on: ""Can I trust this person, business, or AI agent?"" Built entirely in Figma using Make, MCP, and Agent, TrustOS generates trust scores, AI risk analyses, and portable Trust Passports  all from a beautiful dark-mode dashboard. TrustOS gives Maria a single button: ""Verify this person."" One click. A Trust Score, a risk rating, a porta"

author: Clara Chrysilla Yahya
body:
"Most digital products are designed to be faster. Senbazuru  is designed to be slower. Inspired by the Japanese tradition of folding 1,000 paper cranes for someone you care about, we asked: What if a wish wasn't sent with a click, but built together, one crane at a time? With Senbazuru, friends, family, and even strangers can fold a real paper crane, capture it with their phone, and add it to a shared jar. Each crane becomes part of a collective message of hope, love, healing, or celebration. No AI-generated memories.
No instant gratification.
Just a simple ritual connecting the physical and di"

author: Kafui Tourner
body: "Hello again! For those who wanted to play my prototype: Here is the link:  https://www.figma.com/proto/NHfRdyCAp0vU9V8oc2vQtv/Troy-s-Journey--DEMO-?node-id=99-131&p=f&t=WOO1reV5bdBwWWMR-1&scaling=min-zoom&content-scaling=fixed&page-id=0%3A1&starting-point-node-id=99%3A131   It is still in development, but hopefully I can add more interactions to add into the prototype. This is how a game based on paths and decisions are made."

author: Rahul Singh
body: "CareLoop — Elder Care Coordination, Built with Purpose CareLoop is an elder care coordination app built for the Config Makeathon, designed around one core idea: the best medicine is knowing someone cares. Most care apps focus purely on monitoring — vitals, medicines, appointments. CareLoop does that too, but its real focus is emotional connection between elderly parents and the family members who worry about them from afar. Three roles, three experiences: Elderly Parent — a radically simple home screen with medicine reminders, a daily mood check-in (Great → Poor), and one-tap access to family "

author: Georg Moerth
body: "Loneliness is a growing epidemic - and so much of it is one quiet fear: that you're the only one with those embarrassing, intrusive thoughts, while everyone else has it together. Vulnerable Confessions is a simple wall built to break that fear. You tap confess, pick one of a few painfully relatable thoughts or experiences, and instantly see how many other people have confessed to the exact same one. Each confession even becomes a piece of art you can download and set as your phone screensaver - a small, quiet way to share something vulnerable, so that together we all show we're human after all"

author: Nivetha M S
body:
"Doodle & Beats - Where Every Child's Drawing Becomes a Song Not an app to learn from. An app to have fun with. Kids always want fun and joy in everything they do. But today — they're addicted to phones. Watching
unwanted stuff. Scrolling endlessly. And parents
can't do anything about it. That broke my heart. So one idea came to my mind - Why can't doodle and music come together? That's why I made Doodle & Beats. ──────────────────────────────
THE IDEA
────────────────────────────── There's nothing to learn here.
No lessons. No rules. No tutorials. Kids just draw - any color, any shape,
anythin"

author: Vishnu Bhaskar

"Small Repairs Small Repairs is a digital kintsugi-inspired experience where users repair a broken object, add a small note, and place it on a shared shelf of repairs to show that they are not alone. It is not a tool that claims to heal people.
It is simply a reminder that letting go can have a shape, a ritual, and a place. Live site:  https://brand-smudge-06550447.figma.site/
 Figma Community:  https://www.figma.com/community/file/1648441901892673435 The idea came from something simple: sometimes, letting go needs to feel symbolic. Inspired by kintsugi, the Japanese art of repairing broken pot"
